        severity: { type: string, enum: [INFO, WARN, ERROR, FATAL] }
        code: { type: string, pattern: "^[A-Z0-9_]{3,64}$" }
        message: { type: string, minLength: 1, maxLength: 8000 }
        details: {}
    Envelope:
      type: object
      additionalProperties: false
      required: [ok, ts, request_id, result, errors, warnings]
      properties:
        ok: { type: boolean }
        ts: { type: string, format: date-time }
        request_id: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/Uuid" }
        correlation_id: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/Uuid" }
        result: {}
        errors:
          type: array
          items: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/Error" }
        warnings:
          type: array
          items: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/Error" }
    GraphSearchRequest:
      type: object
      additionalProperties: false
      required: [query, limit]
      properties:
        query: { type: string, minLength: 1, maxLength: 2048 }
        filters:
          type: object
          additionalProperties: false
          properties:
            labels: { type: array, items: { type: string } }
            edge_types: { type: array, items: { type: string } }
            case_id: { type: string }
        limit: { type: integer, minimum: 1, maximum: 5000 }
    Node:
      type: object
      additionalProperties: false
      required: [id, labels, properties]
      properties:
        id: { type: string, minLength: 1, maxLength: 256 }
        labels: { type: array, minItems: 1, items: { type: string } }
        properties: { type: object }
    Edge:
      type: object
      additionalProperties: false
      required: [id, source, target, type, properties]
      properties:
        id: { type: string, minLength: 1, maxLength: 256 }
        source: { type: string, minLength: 1, maxLength: 256 }
        target: { type: string, minLength: 1, maxLength: 256 }
        type: { type: string, minLength: 1 }
        properties: { type: object }
    GraphSlice:
      type: object
      additionalProperties: false
      required: [nodes, edges]
      properties:
        nodes: { type: array, items: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/Node" } }
        edges: { type: array, items: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/Edge" } }
    GraphSearchResponse:
      allOf:
        - $ref: "#/components/schemas/GraphSlice"
        - type: object
          additionalProperties: false
          required: [truncated]
          properties:
            truncated: { type: boolean }
    NeighborhoodExpandRequest:
      type: object
      additionalProperties: false
      required: [node_id, depth, limit]
      properties:
        node_id: { type: string, minLength: 1, maxLength: 256 }
        depth: { type: integer, minimum: 1, maximum: 3 }
        limit: { type: integer, minimum: 1, maximum: 20000 }
    EnvelopeGraphSearchResponse:
      allOf:
        - $ref: "#/components/schemas/Envelope"
        - type: object
          properties:
            result: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/GraphSearchResponse" }
    EnvelopeGraphSlice:
      allOf:
        - $ref: "#/components/schemas/Envelope"
        - type: object
          properties:
            result: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/GraphSlice" }
"""))

# Adapter OpenAPI documents
write_text("contracts/openapi/tika_adapter.openapi.yaml", openapi_header("LitigationOS Tika Adapter API", "v0001", "http://127.0.0.1:37831") + textwrap.dedent("""\
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      operationId: health
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/Envelope" }
  /tika/extract:
    post:
      operationId: extractText
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/TikaExtractRequest" }
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Extracted
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/EnvelopeTikaExtractResponse" }
components:
  schemas:
    Uuid:
      type: string
      pattern: "^[0-9a-fA-F]{8}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[1-5][0-9a-fA-F]{3}-[89abAB][0-9a-fA-F]{3}-[0-9a-fA-F]{12}$"
    Sha256:
      type: string
      pattern: "^[0-9a-f]{64}$"
    Error:
      type: object
      additionalProperties: false
      required: [severity, code, message]
      properties:
        severity: { type: string, enum: [INFO, WARN, ERROR, FATAL] }
        code: { type: string, pattern: "^[A-Z0-9_]{3,64}$" }
        message: { type: string, minLength: 1, maxLength: 8000 }
        details: {}
    Envelope:
      type: object
      additionalProperties: false
      required: [ok, ts, request_id, result, errors, warnings]
      properties:
        ok: { type: boolean }
        ts: { type: string, format: date-time }
        request_id: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/Uuid" }
        correlation_id: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/Uuid" }
        result: {}
        errors: { type: array, items: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/Error" } }
        warnings: { type: array, items: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/Error" } }
    TikaExtractRequest:
      type: object
      additionalProperties: false
      required: [input_path, mode, tika_base_url]
      properties:
        input_path: { type: string, minLength: 1 }
        mode: { type: string, enum: [text, rmeta] }
        tika_base_url: { type: string, default: "http://127.0.0.1:9998" }
        output_dir: { type: string }
        timeout_ms: { type: integer, minimum: 100, maximum: 600000 }
    ArtifactPointer:
      type: object
      additionalProperties: false
      required: [rel_path, sha256, media_type]
      properties:
        rel_path: { type: string }
        sha256: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/Sha256" }
        media_type: { type: string }
    TikaExtractResponse:
      type: object
      additionalProperties: false
      required: [artifacts, content_sha256]
      properties:
        content_sha256: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/Sha256" }
        artifacts: { type: array, items: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/ArtifactPointer" } }
    EnvelopeTikaExtractResponse:
      allOf:
        - $ref: "#/components/schemas/Envelope"
        - type: object
          properties:
            result: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/TikaExtractResponse" }
"""))

write_text("contracts/openapi/ocr_adapter.openapi.yaml", openapi_header("LitigationOS OCR Adapter API", "v0001", "http://127.0.0.1:37841") + textwrap.dedent("""\
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      operationId: health
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/Envelope" }
  /ocr/pdf:
    post:
      operationId: ocrPdf
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/OcrPdfRequest" }
      responses:
        "200":
          description: OCR completed
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/EnvelopeOcrPdfResponse" }
components:
  schemas:
    Uuid:
      type: string
      pattern: "^[0-9a-fA-F]{8}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[1-5][0-9a-fA-F]{3}-[89abAB][0-9a-fA-F]{3}-[0-9a-fA-F]{12}$"
    Sha256:
      type: string
      pattern: "^[0-9a-f]{64}$"
    Error:
      type: object
      additionalProperties: false
      required: [severity, code, message]
      properties:
        severity: { type: string, enum: [INFO, WARN, ERROR, FATAL] }
        code: { type: string, pattern: "^[A-Z0-9_]{3,64}$" }
        message: { type: string, minLength: 1, maxLength: 8000 }
        details: {}
    Envelope:
      type: object
      additionalProperties: false
      required: [ok, ts, request_id, result, errors, warnings]
      properties:
        ok: { type: boolean }
        ts: { type: string, format: date-time }
        request_id: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/Uuid" }
        correlation_id: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/Uuid" }
        result: {}
        errors: { type: array, items: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/Error" } }
        warnings: { type: array, items: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/Error" } }
    OcrPdfRequest:
      type: object
      additionalProperties: false
      required: [input_pdf, output_pdf, engine, language, dpi]
      properties:
        input_pdf: { type: string, minLength: 1 }
        output_pdf: { type: string, minLength: 1 }
        output_txt: { type: string }
        engine: { type: string, enum: [ocrmypdf, tesseract, paddleocr, doctr] }
        language: { type: string, default: "eng" }
        dpi: { type: integer, minimum: 72, maximum: 600, default: 300 }
        deskew: { type: boolean, default: true }
        rotate: { type: boolean, default: true }
        timeout_ms: { type: integer, minimum: 100, maximum: 1800000 }
    OcrPdfResponse:
      type: object
      additionalProperties: false
      required: [output_pdf_sha256, report_path, report_sha256]
      properties:
        output_pdf_sha256: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/Sha256" }
        report_path: { type: string }
        report_sha256: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/Sha256" }
    EnvelopeOcrPdfResponse:
      allOf:
        - $ref: "#/components/schemas/Envelope"
        - type: object
          properties:
            result: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/OcrPdfResponse" }
"""))

write_text("contracts/openapi/ollama_adapter.openapi.yaml", openapi_header("LitigationOS Ollama Adapter API", "v0001", "http://127.0.0.1:37851") + textwrap.dedent("""\
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      operationId: health
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/Envelope" }
  /llm/chat:
    post:
      operationId: chat
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/ChatRequest" }
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Chat response
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/EnvelopeChatResponse" }
  /llm/embed:
    post:
      operationId: embed
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/EmbedRequest" }
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Embeddings
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/EnvelopeEmbedResponse" }
  /llm/models:
    get:
      operationId: listModels
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Model list
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/EnvelopeModelList" }
components:
  schemas:
    Uuid:
      type: string
      pattern: "^[0-9a-fA-F]{8}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[1-5][0-9a-fA-F]{3}-[89abAB][0-9a-fA-F]{3}-[0-9a-fA-F]{12}$"
    Error:
      type: object
      additionalProperties: false
      required: [severity, code, message]
      properties:
        severity: { type: string, enum: [INFO, WARN, ERROR, FATAL] }
        code: { type: string, pattern: "^[A-Z0-9_]{3,64}$" }
        message: { type: string, minLength: 1, maxLength: 8000 }
        details: {}
    Envelope:
      type: object
      additionalProperties: false
      required: [ok, ts, request_id, result, errors, warnings]
      properties:
        ok: { type: boolean }
        ts: { type: string, format: date-time }
        request_id: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/Uuid" }
        correlation_id: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/Uuid" }
        result: {}
        errors: { type: array, items: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/Error" } }
        warnings: { type: array, items: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/Error" } }
    ChatMessage:
      type: object
      additionalProperties: false
      required: [role, content]
      properties:
        role: { type: string, enum: [system, user, assistant, tool] }
        content: { type: string }
    ChatRequest:
      type: object
      additionalProperties: false
      required: [ollama_base_url, model, messages, stream]
      properties:
        ollama_base_url: { type: string, default: "http://127.0.0.1:11434/api" }
        model: { type: string, minLength: 1 }
        messages:
          type: array
          minItems: 1
          items: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/ChatMessage" }
        options: { type: object }
        stream: { type: boolean, default: false }
    ChatResponse:
      type: object
      additionalProperties: false
      required: [role, content]
      properties:
        role: { type: string, const: assistant }
        content: { type: string }
        usage: { type: object }
    EmbedRequest:
      type: object
      additionalProperties: false
      required: [ollama_base_url, model, input]
      properties:
        ollama_base_url: { type: string, default: "http://127.0.0.1:11434/api" }
        model: { type: string, minLength: 1 }
        input:
          type: array
          minItems: 1
          items: { type: string }
    EmbedResponse:
      type: object
      additionalProperties: false
      required: [embeddings]
      properties:
        embeddings:
          type: array
          items:
            type: array
            items: { type: number }
    ModelList:
      type: object
      additionalProperties: false
      required: [models]
      properties:
        models: { type: array, items: { type: object } }
    EnvelopeChatResponse:
      allOf:
        - $ref: "#/components/schemas/Envelope"
        - type: object
          properties:
            result: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/ChatResponse" }
    EnvelopeEmbedResponse:
      allOf:
        - $ref: "#/components/schemas/Envelope"
        - type: object
          properties:
            result: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/EmbedResponse" }
    EnvelopeModelList:
      allOf:
        - $ref: "#/components/schemas/Envelope"
        - type: object
          properties:
            result: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/ModelList" }
"""))

write_text("contracts/openapi/rclone_adapter.openapi.yaml", openapi_header("LitigationOS rclone Adapter API", "v0001", "http://127.0.0.1:37861") + textwrap.dedent("""\
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      operationId: health
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/Envelope" }
  /rclone/rc:
    post:
      operationId: rcCall
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/RcloneRcRequest" }
      responses:
        "200":
          description: RC response
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/EnvelopeRcloneRcResponse" }
  /rclone/sync:
    post:
      operationId: sync
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/RcloneSyncRequest" }
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Sync started or completed
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/EnvelopeRcloneSyncResponse" }
components:
  schemas:
    Uuid:
      type: string
      pattern: "^[0-9a-fA-F]{8}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[1-5][0-9a-fA-F]{3}-[89abAB][0-9a-fA-F]{3}-[0-9a-fA-F]{12}$"
    Error:
      type: object
      additionalProperties: false
      required: [severity, code, message]
      properties:
        severity: { type: string, enum: [INFO, WARN, ERROR, FATAL] }
        code: { type: string, pattern: "^[A-Z0-9_]{3,64}$" }
        message: { type: string, minLength: 1, maxLength: 8000 }
        details: {}
    Envelope:
      type: object
      additionalProperties: false
      required: [ok, ts, request_id, result, errors, warnings]
      properties:
        ok: { type: boolean }
        ts: { type: string, format: date-time }
        request_id: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/Uuid" }
        correlation_id: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/Uuid" }
        result: {}
        errors: { type: array, items: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/Error" } }
        warnings: { type: array, items: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/Error" } }
    RcloneRcRequest:
      type: object
      additionalProperties: false
      required: [rc_base_url, command, params]
      properties:
        rc_base_url: { type: string, default: "http://127.0.0.1:5572" }
        command: { type: string, minLength: 1 }
        params: { type: object }
        timeout_ms: { type: integer, minimum: 100, maximum: 1800000 }
    RcloneRcResponse:
      type: object
      additionalProperties: false
      required: [response]
      properties:
        response: {}
    RcloneSyncRequest:
      type: object
      additionalProperties: false
      required: [rc_base_url, src, dst, direction, flags]
      properties:
        rc_base_url: { type: string, default: "http://127.0.0.1:5572" }
        src: { type: string, minLength: 1 }
        dst: { type: string, minLength: 1 }
        direction: { type: string, enum: [push, pull, bidirectional] }
        flags: { type: array, items: { type: string } }
        timeout_ms: { type: integer, minimum: 100, maximum: 1800000 }
    RcloneSyncResponse:
      type: object
      additionalProperties: false
      required: [mode, summary]
      properties:
        mode: { type: string, enum: [rc_sync, rc_job] }
        summary: {}
    EnvelopeRcloneRcResponse:
      allOf:
        - $ref: "#/components/schemas/Envelope"
        - type: object
          properties:
            result: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/RcloneRcResponse" }
    EnvelopeRcloneSyncResponse:
      allOf:
        - $ref: "#/components/schemas/Envelope"
        - type: object
          properties:
            result: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/RcloneSyncResponse" }
"""))

# ---- Docs
write_text("docs/PORTS.md", textwrap.dedent("""\
# Ports and Local Endpoints (Canonical)

All endpoints are loopback-only unless explicitly overridden.

| Service | Default URL | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Orchestrator API | http://127.0.0.1:37811 | Workflow control plane |
| Graph API | http://127.0.0.1:37821 | Bloom-style graph queries for UI |
| Tika Adapter | http://127.0.0.1:37831 | Wraps Apache Tika Server |
| OCR Adapter | http://127.0.0.1:37841 | Wraps OCRmyPDF and optional deep OCR lanes |
| Ollama Adapter | http://127.0.0.1:37851 | Wraps local Ollama daemon |
| rclone Adapter | http://127.0.0.1:37861 | Wraps rclone RC calls |
| Apache Tika Server | http://127.0.0.1:9998 | External process started by app |
| Ollama | http://127.0.0.1:11434/api | External process started by operator or app |
| rclone RC | http://127.0.0.1:5572 | External process started by operator or app |
| Neo4j Bolt | bolt://127.0.0.1:7687 | Database driver transport |
| Neo4j HTTP | http://127.0.0.1:7474 | Optional administrative transport |
"""))

write_text("docs/SERVICE_MATRIX.md", textwrap.dedent("""\
# Service Boundary Contracts (Canonical)

This addendum defines the boundary contracts and machine-checkable schemas for each local interface.

## Contract invariants
- Every request carries request_id and may carry correlation_id.
- Every response is wrapped in the Envelope schema.
- Every service MUST be idempotent for any request that includes idempotency_key.
- Any filing-capable export remains blocked until PCW and PCG gates PASS.

## Boundary list
- UI IPC: schemas/ipc/ipc_message.schema.json and schemas/ipc/ipc_response.schema.json
- Orchestrator API: contracts/openapi/orchestrator.openapi.yaml
- Graph API: contracts/openapi/graph.openapi.yaml
- Tika Adapter: contracts/openapi/tika_adapter.openapi.yaml
- OCR Adapter: contracts/openapi/ocr_adapter.openapi.yaml
- Ollama Adapter: contracts/openapi/ollama_adapter.openapi.yaml
- rclone Adapter: contracts/openapi/rclone_adapter.openapi.yaml
"""))

write_text("docs/VALIDATION.md", textwrap.dedent("""\
# Validation (Canonical)

## JSON Schema
All schemas declare Draft 2020-12 via:
- $schema = https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema

## OpenAPI
All OpenAPI documents use:
- openapi: 3.1.0
- jsonSchemaDialect: https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema

Validation pipeline expectation:
1) Validate all *.schema.json files under schemas/ using a Draft 2020-12 validator.
2) Parse all OpenAPI YAML files and verify openapi field is 3.1.0 and jsonSchemaDialect matches.
3) Contract tests: simulate a request to each /health endpoint and validate Envelope response shape.
"""))

write_text("docs/SECURITY.md", textwrap.dedent("""\
# Security and Local-Only Assumptions (Canonical)

- Loopback-only binding for all internal APIs.
- Electron security posture:
  - contextIsolation enabled
  - nodeIntegration disabled
  - preload exposes a minimal contextBridge surface
- No telemetry by default.
- Any network features require explicit operator opt-in.
"""))

# Spec Addendum Pack v0001 (Append-Only)

This pack adds Draft 2020-12 JSON Schemas and OpenAPI 3.1 service contracts for LitigationOS local boundaries.

## Contents
- schemas/common: Envelope, IDs, hashes, errors, pinpoints, policy decisions, artifacts
- schemas/ipc: Electron IPC message and response schemas
- schemas/api and schemas/reports: Orchestrator request schemas and report schemas
- contracts/openapi: OpenAPI 3.1 contracts for Orchestrator, Graph, Tika adapter, OCR adapter, Ollama adapter, rclone adapter
- docs: ports, service matrix, validation, security
- examples: request examples for major adapters

## Integration
Unzip into the bundle root: LITIGATIONOS__MASTERv1.0

No files in this pack overwrite existing files by default when placed under a new versioned subfolder.
"""))

file_records = []
for p in base.rglob("*"):
    if p.is_file():
        rel = str(p.relative_to(base)).replace("\\","/")
        file_records.append({
            "rel_path": rel,
            "bytes": p.stat().st_size,
            "sha256": sha256_file(p),
            "modified_utc": datetime.datetime.utcfromtimestamp(p.stat().st_mtime).replace(microsecond=0).isoformat() + "Z"
        })

pack_manifest = {
    "pack_name": base.name,
    "created_at_utc": datetime.datetime.utcnow().replace(microsecond=0).isoformat() + "Z",
    "schema_dialect": "draft-2020-12",
    "openapi_version": "3.1.0",
    "file_count": len(file_records),
    "files": sorted(file_records, key=lambda x: x["rel_path"])
}
write_json("PACK_MANIFEST.json", pack_manifest)

zip_path = Path("/mnt/data") / f"{base.name}.zip"
with zipfile.ZipFile(zip_path, "w", compression=zipfile.ZIP_DEFLATED) as z:
    for rec in pack_manifest["files"]:
        z.write(base / rec["rel_path"], arcname=f"{base.name}/{rec['rel_path']}")

import json, shutil, zipfile, hashlib, datetime, textwrap
from pathlib import Path

src_dir = Path("/mnt/data/LITIGATIONOS__MASTERv1.0__SPEC_ADDENDUM_v0001")
assert src_dir.exists(), "v0001 dir missing"

dst_dir = Path("/mnt/data/LITIGATIONOS__MASTERv1.0__SPEC_ADDENDUM_v0002")
if dst_dir.exists():
    shutil.rmtree(dst_dir)
shutil.copytree(src_dir, dst_dir)

SCHEMA_URI = "https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema"

def write_text(rel_path: str, content: str):
    p = dst_dir / rel_path
    p.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    p.write_text(content, encoding="utf-8")
    return p

def write_json(rel_path: str, obj):
    p = dst_dir / rel_path
    p.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    p.write_text(json.dumps(obj, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
    return p

def sha256_file(p: Path) -> str:
    h = hashlib.sha256()
    with p.open("rb") as f:
        for chunk in iter(lambda: f.read(1024*1024), b""):
            h.update(chunk)
    return h.hexdigest()

  "$schema": SCHEMA_URI,
  "$id":"litigationos://schemas/reports/auth_snapshot_index.schema.json",
  "title":"AUTH_SNAPSHOT_INDEX",
  "type":"object",
  "properties":{
    "snapshot_id":{"type":"string","pattern":"^AUTH_SNAP_[0-9]{8}_[0-9]{6}_[A-Z0-9]{6}$"},
    "generated_at":{"type":"string","format":"date-time"},
    "jurisdiction":{"type":"string","const":"Michigan"},
    "scope":{
      "type":"object",
      "properties":{
        "authority_types":{"type":"array","minItems":1,"items":{"type":"string","enum":["MCR","MCL","MRE","BENCHBOOK","SCAO_FORM","ADMIN_ORDER","CASELAW","LOCAL_ORDER"]}},
        "effective_as_of":{"type":"string","format":"date"},
        "sources":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"string","minLength":1}}
      },
      "required":["authority_types","effective_as_of","sources"],
      "additionalProperties": False
    },
    "authorities":{
      "type":"array",
      "items":{
        "type":"object",
        "properties":{
          "authority_id":{"type":"string","minLength":1},
          "authority_type":{"type":"string","enum":["MCR","MCL","MRE","BENCHBOOK","SCAO_FORM","ADMIN_ORDER","CASELAW","LOCAL_ORDER"]},
          "title":{"type":"string","minLength":1},
          "publisher":{"type":"string","minLength":1},
          "url":{"type":"string"},
          "version_label":{"type":"string"},
          "effective_date":{"type":"string","format":"date"},
          "retrieved_at":{"type":"string","format":"date-time"},
          "hashes":{"$ref":"../common/hash.schema.json#/$defs/hashes"},
          "chunks":{
            "type":"array",
            "items":{
              "type":"object",
              "properties":{
                "chunk_id":{"type":"string","minLength":1},
                "section":{"type":"string","minLength":1},
                "chunk_pointer":{"type":"string","minLength":1},
                "hashes":{"$ref":"../common/hash.schema.json#/$defs/hashes"}
              },
              "required":["chunk_id","section","chunk_pointer","hashes"],
              "additionalProperties": False
            }
          }
        },
        "required":["authority_id","authority_type","title","publisher","effective_date","retrieved_at","hashes","chunks"],
        "additionalProperties": False
      }
    },
    "blockers":{"type":"array","items":{"$ref":"../common/errors.schema.json#/$defs/error"}}
  },
  "required":["snapshot_id","generated_at","jurisdiction","scope","authorities","blockers"],
  "additionalProperties": False
})

# ---- Add schema catalog and contract catalog (hash-locked)
def build_catalog():
    schemas = []
    for p in (dst_dir / "schemas").rglob("*.schema.json"):
        schemas.append(str(p.relative_to(dst_dir)).replace("\\","/"))
    contracts = []
    for p in (dst_dir / "contracts/openapi").glob("*.openapi.yaml"):
        contracts.append(str(p.relative_to(dst_dir)).replace("\\","/"))
    return sorted(schemas), sorted(contracts)

schemas_list, contracts_list = build_catalog()

schema_catalog = {
  "catalog_id": "SCHEMA_CATALOG_v0002",
  "generated_at_utc": datetime.datetime.utcnow().replace(microsecond=0).isoformat() + "Z",
  "json_schema_dialect": "draft-2020-12",
  "schemas": [{"path": p, "sha256": sha256_file(dst_dir/p)} for p in schemas_list]
}
contract_catalog = {
  "catalog_id": "CONTRACT_CATALOG_v0002",
  "generated_at_utc": datetime.datetime.utcnow().replace(microsecond=0).isoformat() + "Z",
  "openapi_version": "3.1.0",
  "contracts": [{"path": p, "sha256": sha256_file(dst_dir/p)} for p in contracts_list]
}
write_json("REGISTRY/SCHEMA_CATALOG.json", schema_catalog)
write_json("REGISTRY/CONTRACT_CATALOG.json", contract_catalog)

# ---- Add breaking-change policy and deterministic compatibility gate rules
write_text("docs/COMPATIBILITY_POLICY.md", textwrap.dedent("""\
# Compatibility and Breaking-Change Policy (Canonical)

## Goals
- Machine-checkable contracts that remain stable across versions.
- Fail-closed behavior when contracts drift.
- Monotonic versioning: Spec Addendum versions are v0001, v0002, ... and never decrease.

## Contract drift classes
### 1) Non-breaking
- Adding an optional field
- Adding a new endpoint
- Adding a new enum value **only** when the consumer treats enums as open sets

### 2) Breaking (must bump major in internal service versions; must bump Spec Addendum version)
- Removing a field or endpoint
- Changing a field type or tightening constraints
- Changing enum semantics or treating enum as a closed set
- Changing default behavior of a gate (Truth-Lock or PCW/PCG)

## Hash locks
- `REGISTRY/SCHEMA_CATALOG.json` and `REGISTRY/CONTRACT_CATALOG.json` are authoritative.
- A CI gate MUST compare previous catalog hashes to current.
- Any breaking change MUST be intentional and recorded in CHANGELOG.

## Required artifacts per build
- PACK_MANIFEST.json
- SCHEMA_CATALOG.json
- CONTRACT_CATALOG.json
- CONTRACT_TEST_REPORT.json (schema defined in this pack)
"""))

  "title":"CONTRACT_TEST_REPORT",
  "type":"object",
  "properties":{
    "test_run_id":{"type":"string","pattern":"^CTEST_[0-9]{8}_[0-9]{6}_[A-Z0-9]{6}$"},
    "generated_at":{"type":"string","format":"date-time"},
    "spec_addendum_version":{"type":"string","pattern":"^v[0-9]{4}$"},
    "schemas_validated":{"type":"integer","minimum":0},
    "contracts_validated":{"type":"integer","minimum":0},
    "checks":{
      "type":"array",
      "items":{
        "type":"object",
        "properties":{
          "name":{"type":"string","minLength":1},
          "ok":{"type":"boolean"},
          "detail":{"type":"string","maxLength":8000},
          "artifacts":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"string","minLength":1}}
        },
        "required":["name","ok","detail","artifacts"],
        "additionalProperties": False
      }
    },
    "errors":{"type":"array","items":{"$ref":"../common/errors.schema.json#/$defs/error"}},
    "warnings":{"type":"array","items":{"$ref":"../common/errors.schema.json#/$defs/warning"}}
  },
  "required":["test_run_id","generated_at","spec_addendum_version","schemas_validated","contracts_validated","checks","errors","warnings"],
  "additionalProperties": False
})

write_text("tools/contract_smoke_test.py", textwrap.dedent("""\
#!/usr/bin/env python3
\"\"\"LitigationOS Spec Addendum — Contract Smoke Test (offline, no extra deps)

Validates that:
- All *.schema.json files are parseable JSON and contain a Draft 2020-12 $schema URI.
- All OpenAPI YAML contracts:
  - contain `openapi: 3.1.0`
  - contain `jsonSchemaDialect` referencing Draft 2020-12
  - include at least /health
This is a structural sanity test, not a full semantic validator.

Usage:
  python tools/contract_smoke_test.py --root .
Outputs:
  writes reports/CONTRACT_TEST_REPORT.json

Exit codes:
  0 OK
  2 FAIL
\"\"\"

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import datetime
import json
import os
import re
import uuid
from pathlib import Path

DRAFT_2020_12 = "https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema"

def gen_id(prefix: str) -> str:
    now = datetime.datetime.utcnow().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
    token = uuid.uuid4().hex[:6].upper()
    return f"{prefix}_{now}_{token}"

def parse_args():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    ap.add_argument("--root", default=".", help="Pack root")
    return ap.parse_args()

def main() -> int:
    args = parse_args()
    root = Path(args.root).resolve()

    schemas = sorted(root.glob("schemas/**/*.schema.json"))
    contracts = sorted(root.glob("contracts/openapi/*.openapi.yaml"))

    errors = []
    warnings = []
    checks = []

    # Schema checks
    good = 0
    for sp in schemas:
        try:
            data = json.loads(sp.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
        except Exception as e:
            errors.append({"severity":"ERROR","code":"SCHEMA_JSON_PARSE_FAIL","message":str(e),"details":{"path":str(sp)}})
            continue
        if data.get("$schema") != DRAFT_2020_12:
            errors.append({"severity":"ERROR","code":"SCHEMA_DIALECT_MISMATCH","message":"$schema is not Draft 2020-12","details":{"path":str(sp),"$schema":data.get("$schema")}})
            continue
        good += 1
    checks.append({"name":"schemas_parse_and_dialect","ok":len(errors)==0,"detail":f"validated {good}/{len(schemas)} schemas","artifacts":[]})

    # OpenAPI YAML structural checks (regex-based, avoids YAML deps)
    o_ok = 0
    for cp in contracts:
        txt = cp.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
        if not re.search(r"^openapi:\\s*3\\.1\\.0\\s*$", txt, flags=re.MULTILINE):
            errors.append({"severity":"ERROR","code":"OAS_VERSION_MISSING_OR_WRONG","message":"openapi: 3.1.0 missing","details":{"path":str(cp)}})
            continue
        if not re.search(r'^jsonSchemaDialect:\\s*\"?https://json-schema\\.org/draft/2020-12/schema\"?\\s*$', txt, flags=re.MULTILINE):
            errors.append({"severity":"ERROR","code":"OAS_DIALECT_MISSING_OR_WRONG","message":"jsonSchemaDialect missing or wrong","details":{"path":str(cp)}})
            continue
        if "/health:" not in txt:
            warnings.append({"severity":"WARN","code":"OAS_HEALTH_ENDPOINT_MISSING","message":"/health not found in contract","details":{"path":str(cp)}})
        o_ok += 1
    checks.append({"name":"openapi_3_1_and_dialect","ok":all(c.get("code","").startswith("OAS_") is False for c in errors) if errors else True,"detail":f"checked {o_ok}/{len(contracts)} contracts","artifacts":[]})

    ok = (len([e for e in errors if e["severity"] in ("ERROR","FATAL")]) == 0)

    report = {
        "test_run_id": gen_id("CTEST"),
        "generated_at": datetime.datetime.utcnow().replace(microsecond=0).isoformat()+"Z",
        "spec_addendum_version": "v0002",
        "schemas_validated": len(schemas),
        "contracts_validated": len(contracts),
        "checks": checks,
        "errors": errors,
        "warnings": warnings
    }

    out_dir = root / "reports"
    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    out_path = out_dir / "CONTRACT_TEST_REPORT.json"
    out_path.write_text(json.dumps(report, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False) + "\\n", encoding="utf-8")

    return 0 if ok else 2

if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())
"""))

write_text("reports/README.md", textwrap.dedent("""\

Workflow engines write (example set):

- PCW_REPORT.json
- OCR_REPORT.json
- NLP_REPORT.json
- AUTH_SNAPSHOT_INDEX.json
- VEHICLEMAP_INDEX.json
- FORM_OVERLAY_AUDIT.json
"""))

write_text("docs/SUPPLY_CHAIN.md", textwrap.dedent("""\
# Supply-chain Hygiene (Canonical)

LitigationOS ships offline-first and open-source. To preserve trust and reproducibility:

## SBOM outputs
Preferred SBOM formats (operator selectable):
- CycloneDX (JSON) — broad supply-chain object model and official schemas.
- SPDX — ISO/IEC 5962:2021 recognized SBOM and licensing vocabulary.

## Integrity controls
- Hash every release artifact (SHA-256)
- Keep dependency ledger with license expressions
- Enforce monotonic versions and immutable releases
- Apply SLSA-inspired controls for build provenance where feasible

This pack provides:
- SCHEMA_CATALOG.json and CONTRACT_CATALOG.json (hash locks)
- COMPATIBILITY_POLICY.md (breaking-change rules)
"""))

# ---- Update index doc to include v0002 deltas
write_text("SPEC_ADDENDUM_PACK__INDEX.md", (dst_dir/"SPEC_ADDENDUM_PACK__INDEX.md").read_text(encoding="utf-8") + "\n" + textwrap.dedent("""\
## v0002 additions
- New machine-checkable report schemas:
  - AUTH_SNAPSHOT_INDEX
  - VEHICLEMAP_INDEX
  - FORM_OVERLAY_AUDIT
  - CONTRACT_TEST_REPORT
- Hash-locked registries:
  - REGISTRY/SCHEMA_CATALOG.json
  - REGISTRY/CONTRACT_CATALOG.json
- Compatibility policy and contract smoke-test runner:
  - docs/COMPATIBILITY_POLICY.md
  - tools/contract_smoke_test.py
"""))

# ---- Rebuild PACK_MANIFEST.json and zip
file_records = []
for p in dst_dir.rglob("*"):
    if p.is_file():
        rel = str(p.relative_to(dst_dir)).replace("\\","/")
        file_records.append({
            "rel_path": rel,
            "bytes": p.stat().st_size,
            "sha256": sha256_file(p),
            "modified_utc": datetime.datetime.utcfromtimestamp(p.stat().st_mtime).replace(microsecond=0).isoformat() + "Z"
        })

pack_manifest = {
    "pack_name": dst_dir.name,
    "created_at_utc": datetime.datetime.utcnow().replace(microsecond=0).isoformat() + "Z",
    "schema_dialect": "draft-2020-12",
    "openapi_version": "3.1.0",
    "file_count": len(file_records),
    "files": sorted(file_records, key=lambda x: x["rel_path"])
}
write_json("PACK_MANIFEST.json", pack_manifest)

zip_path = Path("/mnt/data") / f"{dst_dir.name}.zip"
if zip_path.exists():
    zip_path.unlink()
with zipfile.ZipFile(zip_path, "w", compression=zipfile.ZIP_DEFLATED) as z:
    for rec in pack_manifest["files"]:
        z.write(dst_dir / rec["rel_path"], arcname=f"{dst_dir.name}/{rec['rel_path']}")

(zip_path, zip_path.stat().st_size, len(pack_manifest["files"]))

Always show details
import json, shutil, zipfile, hashlib, datetime, textwrap
from pathlib import Path

src_dir = Path("/mnt/data/LITIGATIONOS__MASTERv1.0__SPEC_ADDENDUM_v0001")
assert src_dir.exists(), f"v0001 dir missing at {src_dir}"

dst_dir = Path("/mnt/data/LITIGATIONOS__MASTERv1.0__SPEC_ADDENDUM_v0002")
if dst_dir.exists():
    shutil.rmtree(dst_dir)
shutil.copytree(src_dir, dst_dir)

SCHEMA_URI = "https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema"

def write_text(rel_path: str, content: str):
    p = dst_dir / rel_path
    p.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    p.write_text(content, encoding="utf-8")
    return p

def write_json(rel_path: str, obj):
    p = dst_dir / rel_path
    p.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    p.write_text(json.dumps(obj, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
    return p

def sha256_file(p: Path) -> str:
    h = hashlib.sha256()
    with p.open("rb") as f:
        for chunk in iter(lambda: f.read(1024*1024), b""):
            h.update(chunk)
    return h.hexdigest()

# ---- New report schemas: AUTH_SNAPSHOT_INDEX, VEHICLEMAP_INDEX, FORM_OVERLAY_AUDIT
write_json("schemas/reports/auth_snapshot_index.schema.json", {
  "$schema": SCHEMA_URI,
  "$id":"litigationos://schemas/reports/auth_snapshot_index.schema.json",
  "title":"AUTH_SNAPSHOT_INDEX",
  "type":"object",
  "properties":{
    "snapshot_id":{"type":"string","pattern":"^AUTH_SNAP_[0-9]{8}_[0-9]{6}_[A-Z0-9]{6}$"},
    "generated_at":{"type":"string","format":"date-time"},
    "jurisdiction":{"type":"string","const":"Michigan"},
    "scope":{
      "type":"object",
      "properties":{
        "authority_types":{"type":"array","minItems":1,"items":{"type":"string","enum":["MCR","MCL","MRE","BENCHBOOK","SCAO_FORM","ADMIN_ORDER","CASELAW","LOCAL_ORDER"]}},
        "effective_as_of":{"type":"string","format":"date"},
        "sources":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"string","minLength":1}}
      },
      "required":["authority_types","effective_as_of","sources"],
      "additionalProperties": False
    },
    "authorities":{
      "type":"array",
      "items":{
        "type":"object",
        "properties":{
          "authority_id":{"type":"string","minLength":1},
          "authority_type":{"type":"string","enum":["MCR","MCL","MRE","BENCHBOOK","SCAO_FORM","ADMIN_ORDER","CASELAW","LOCAL_ORDER"]},
          "title":{"type":"string","minLength":1},
          "publisher":{"type":"string","minLength":1},
          "url":{"type":"string"},
          "version_label":{"type":"string"},
          "effective_date":{"type":"string","format":"date"},
          "retrieved_at":{"type":"string","format":"date-time"},
          "hashes":{"$ref":"../common/hash.schema.json#/$defs/hashes"},
          "chunks":{
            "type":"array",
            "items":{
              "type":"object",
              "properties":{
                "chunk_id":{"type":"string","minLength":1},
                "section":{"type":"string","minLength":1},
                "chunk_pointer":{"type":"string","minLength":1},
                "hashes":{"$ref":"../common/hash.schema.json#/$defs/hashes"}
              },
              "required":["chunk_id","section","chunk_pointer","hashes"],
              "additionalProperties": False
            }
          }
        },
        "required":["authority_id","authority_type","title","publisher","effective_date","retrieved_at","hashes","chunks"],
        "additionalProperties": False
      }
    },
    "blockers":{"type":"array","items":{"$ref":"../common/errors.schema.json#/$defs/error"}}
  },
  "required":["snapshot_id","generated_at","jurisdiction","scope","authorities","blockers"],
  "additionalProperties": False
})

write_json("schemas/reports/vehiclemap_index.schema.json", {
  "$schema": SCHEMA_URI,
  "$id":"litigationos://schemas/reports/vehiclemap_index.schema.json",
  "title":"VEHICLEMAP_INDEX",
  "type":"object",
  "properties":{
    "vehiclemap_id":{"type":"string","pattern":"^VMAP_[0-9]{8}_[0-9]{6}_[A-Z0-9]{6}$"},
    "generated_at":{"type":"string","format":"date-time"},
    "jurisdiction":{"type":"string","const":"Michigan"},
    "vehicles":{
      "type":"array",
      "minItems":1,
      "items":{
        "type":"object",
        "properties":{
          "vehicle_id":{"type":"string","minLength":1},
          "vehicle_name":{"type":"string","minLength":1},
          "relief_types":{"type":"array","minItems":1,"items":{"type":"string","minLength":1}},
          "primary_forms":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"string","minLength":1}},
          "governing_authority":{
            "type":"array",
            "minItems":1,
            "items":{"$ref":"../common/pinpoints.schema.json#/$defs/LawPinpoint"}
          },
          "standards":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"string","minLength":1,"maxLength":2000}},
          "proof_obligations":{
            "type":"array",
            "minItems":1,
            "items":{
              "type":"object",
              "properties":{
                "po_id":{"type":"string","minLength":1},
                "is_core":{"type":"boolean"},
                "description":{"type":"string","minLength":1,"maxLength":8000},
                "requires_authority":{"type":"array","items":{"$ref":"../common/pinpoints.schema.json#/$defs/LawPinpoint"}},
                "requires_evidence":{"type":"array","items":{"$ref":"../common/pinpoints.schema.json#/$defs/EvidencePinpoint"}},
                "deadline_rules":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"string","minLength":1}},
                "service_requirements":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"string","minLength":1}},
                "fee_or_bond_rules":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"string","minLength":1}}
              },
              "required":["po_id","is_core","description","requires_authority","requires_evidence","deadline_rules","service_requirements","fee_or_bond_rules"],
              "additionalProperties": False
            }
          },
          "fallbacks":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"string","minLength":1,"maxLength":2000}},
          "risks":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"string","minLength":1,"maxLength":2000}}
        },
        "required":["vehicle_id","vehicle_name","relief_types","primary_forms","governing_authority","standards","proof_obligations","fallbacks","risks"],
        "additionalProperties": False
      }
    },
    "blockers":{"type":"array","items":{"$ref":"../common/errors.schema.json#/$defs/error"}}
  },
  "required":["vehiclemap_id","generated_at","jurisdiction","vehicles","blockers"],
  "additionalProperties": False
})

write_json("schemas/reports/form_overlay_audit.schema.json", {
  "$schema": SCHEMA_URI,
  "$id":"litigationos://schemas/reports/form_overlay_audit.schema.json",
  "title":"FORM_OVERLAY_AUDIT",
  "type":"object",

    "form_id":{"type":"string","minLength":1},
    "form_title":{"type":"string","minLength":1},
    "form_version":{"type":"string","minLength":1},
    "output_file":{"type":"string","minLength":1},

    "field_map":{
      "type":"array",
      "items":{
        "type":"object",

          "field_value":{"type":["string","number","boolean","null"]},
          "source_evidence":{"type":"array","items":{"$ref":"../common/pinpoints.schema.json#/$defs/EvidencePinpoint"}},
          "source_authority":{"type":"array","items":{"$ref":"../common/pinpoints.schema.json#/$defs/LawPinpoint"}},
          "transform_notes":{"type":"string","maxLength":4000}
        },
        "required":["field_name","field_value","source_evidence","source_authority"],
        "additionalProperties": False
      }
    },
    "warnings":{"type":"array","items":{"$ref":"../common/errors.schema.json#/$defs/warning"}},
    "blockers":{"type":"array","items":{"$ref":"../common/errors.schema.json#/$defs/error"}}
  },
  "required":["audit_id","generated_at","form_id","form_title","form_version","output_file","output_hashes","field_map","warnings","blockers"],
  "additionalProperties": False
})

# ---- Schema + contract catalogs (hash-locked)
def build_catalog():
    schemas = []
    for p in (dst_dir / "schemas").rglob("*.schema.json"):
        schemas.append(str(p.relative_to(dst_dir)).replace("\\","/"))
    contracts = []
    for p in (dst_dir / "contracts/openapi").glob("*.openapi.yaml"):
        contracts.append(str(p.relative_to(dst_dir)).replace("\\","/"))
    return sorted(schemas), sorted(contracts)

schemas_list, contracts_list = build_catalog()

schema_catalog = {
  "catalog_id": "SCHEMA_CATALOG_v0002",
  "generated_at_utc": datetime.datetime.utcnow().replace(microsecond=0).isoformat() + "Z",
  "json_schema_dialect": "draft-2020-12",
  "schemas": [{"path": p, "sha256": sha256_file(dst_dir/p)} for p in schemas_list]
}
contract_catalog = {
  "catalog_id": "CONTRACT_CATALOG_v0002",
  "generated_at_utc": datetime.datetime.utcnow().replace(microsecond=0).isoformat() + "Z",
  "openapi_version": "3.1.0",
  "contracts": [{"path": p, "sha256": sha256_file(dst_dir/p)} for p in contracts_list]
}
write_json("REGISTRY/SCHEMA_CATALOG.json", schema_catalog)
write_json("REGISTRY/CONTRACT_CATALOG.json", contract_catalog)

# ---- Compatibility policy + contract test report schema + smoke test tool
write_text("docs/COMPATIBILITY_POLICY.md", textwrap.dedent("""\
# Compatibility and Breaking-Change Policy (Canonical)

## Goals
- Machine-checkable contracts that remain stable across versions.
- Fail-closed behavior when contracts drift.
- Monotonic versioning: Spec Addendum versions are v0001, v0002, ... and never decrease.

## Contract drift classes
### 1) Non-breaking
- Adding an optional field
- Adding a new endpoint
- Adding a new enum value only when consumers treat enums as open sets

### 2) Breaking (must be intentional)
- Removing a field or endpoint
- Changing a field type or tightening constraints
- Changing enum semantics (closed-set enforcement)
- Changing default behavior of a gate (Truth-Lock or PCW/PCG)

## Hash locks
- `REGISTRY/SCHEMA_CATALOG.json` and `REGISTRY/CONTRACT_CATALOG.json` are authoritative.
- A CI gate MUST compare previous catalog hashes to current.
- Any breaking change MUST be recorded in CHANGELOG.

## Required artifacts per build
- PACK_MANIFEST.json
- REGISTRY/SCHEMA_CATALOG.json
- REGISTRY/CONTRACT_CATALOG.json
- reports/CONTRACT_TEST_REPORT.json
"""))

write_json("schemas/reports/contract_test_report.schema.json", {
  "$schema": SCHEMA_URI,
  "$id":"litigationos://schemas/reports/contract_test_report.schema.json",
  "title":"CONTRACT_TEST_REPORT",
  "type":"object",
  "properties":{
    "test_run_id":{"type":"string","pattern":"^CTEST_[0-9]{8}_[0-9]{6}_[A-Z0-9]{6}$"},
    "generated_at":{"type":"string","format":"date-time"},
    "spec_addendum_version":{"type":"string","pattern":"^v[0-9]{4}$"},
    "schemas_validated":{"type":"integer","minimum":0},
    "contracts_validated":{"type":"integer","minimum":0},
    "checks":{
      "type":"array",
      "items":{
        "type":"object",
        "properties":{
          "name":{"type":"string","minLength":1},
          "ok":{"type":"boolean"},
          "detail":{"type":"string","maxLength":8000},
          "artifacts":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"string","minLength":1}}
        },
        "required":["name","ok","detail","artifacts"],
        "additionalProperties": False
      }
    },
    "errors":{"type":"array","items":{"$ref":"../common/errors.schema.json#/$defs/error"}},
    "warnings":{"type":"array","items":{"$ref":"../common/errors.schema.json#/$defs/warning"}}
  },
  "required":["test_run_id","generated_at","spec_addendum_version","schemas_validated","contracts_validated","checks","errors","warnings"],
  "additionalProperties": False
})

write_text("tools/contract_smoke_test.py", textwrap.dedent("""\
#!/usr/bin/env python3
\"\"\"LitigationOS Spec Addendum — Contract Smoke Test (offline)

Validates:
- All *.schema.json files are parseable JSON and declare Draft 2020-12 dialect.
- All OpenAPI YAML contracts contain openapi: 3.1.0 and jsonSchemaDialect Draft 2020-12.
This is a structural sanity test (no external dependencies).

Usage:
  python tools/contract_smoke_test.py --root .
Outputs:
  reports/CONTRACT_TEST_REPORT.json
\"\"\"

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import datetime
import json
import re
import uuid
from pathlib import Path

DRAFT_2020_12 = "https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema"

def gen_id(prefix: str) -> str:
    now = datetime.datetime.utcnow().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
    token = uuid.uuid4().hex[:6].upper()
    return f"{prefix}_{now}_{token}"

def parse_args():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    ap.add_argument("--root", default=".", help="Pack root")
    return ap.parse_args()

def main() -> int:
    args = parse_args()
    root = Path(args.root).resolve()

    schemas = sorted(root.glob("schemas/**/*.schema.json"))
    contracts = sorted(root.glob("contracts/openapi/*.openapi.yaml"))

    errors = []
    warnings = []
    checks = []

    good = 0
    for sp in schemas:
        try:
            data = json.loads(sp.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
        except Exception as e:
            errors.append({"severity":"ERROR","code":"SCHEMA_JSON_PARSE_FAIL","message":str(e),"details":{"path":str(sp)}})
            continue
        if data.get("$schema") != DRAFT_2020_12:
            errors.append({"severity":"ERROR","code":"SCHEMA_DIALECT_MISMATCH","message":"$schema is not Draft 2020-12","details":{"path":str(sp),"$schema":data.get("$schema")}})
            continue
        good += 1
    checks.append({"name":"schemas_parse_and_dialect","ok":len([e for e in errors if e.get('code','').startswith('SCHEMA_')])==0,"detail":f"validated {good}/{len(schemas)} schemas","artifacts":[]})

    o_ok = 0
    for cp in contracts:
        txt = cp.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
        if not re.search(r"^openapi:\\s*3\\.1\\.0\\s*$", txt, flags=re.MULTILINE):
            errors.append({"severity":"ERROR","code":"OAS_VERSION_MISSING_OR_WRONG","message":"openapi: 3.1.0 missing","details":{"path":str(cp)}})
            continue
        if not re.search(r'^jsonSchemaDialect:\\s*\"?https://json-schema\\.org/draft/2020-12/schema\"?\\s*$', txt, flags=re.MULTILINE):
            errors.append({"severity":"ERROR","code":"OAS_DIALECT_MISSING_OR_WRONG","message":"jsonSchemaDialect missing or wrong","details":{"path":str(cp)}})
            continue
        if "/health:" not in txt:
            warnings.append({"severity":"WARN","code":"OAS_HEALTH_ENDPOINT_MISSING","message":"/health not found in contract","details":{"path":str(cp)}})
        o_ok += 1
    checks.append({"name":"openapi_3_1_and_dialect","ok":len([e for e in errors if e.get('code','').startswith('OAS_')])==0,"detail":f"checked {o_ok}/{len(contracts)} contracts","artifacts":[]})

    ok = len(errors) == 0

    report = {
        "test_run_id": gen_id("CTEST"),
        "generated_at": datetime.datetime.utcnow().replace(microsecond=0).isoformat()+"Z",
        "spec_addendum_version": "v0002",
        "schemas_validated": len(schemas),
        "contracts_validated": len(contracts),
        "checks": checks,
        "errors": errors,
        "warnings": warnings
    }

    out_dir = root / "reports"
    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    (out_dir / "CONTRACT_TEST_REPORT.json").write_text(json.dumps(report, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False) + "\\n", encoding="utf-8")

    return 0 if ok else 2

if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())
"""))

write_text("docs/SUPPLY_CHAIN.md", textwrap.dedent("""\
# Supply-chain Hygiene (Canonical)

LitigationOS is offline-first and open-source. Supply-chain hygiene is mandatory:

## SBOM outputs
Preferred SBOM formats (operator selectable):
- CycloneDX (JSON)
- SPDX (ISO/IEC 5962:2021)

## Integrity controls
- Hash every release artifact (SHA-256)
- Keep a dependency ledger with SPDX license expressions
- Enforce monotonic versions and immutable releases
- Apply SLSA-inspired controls for build provenance where feasible

This pack provides:
- Hash-locked catalogs (SCHEMA_CATALOG, CONTRACT_CATALOG)
- Compatibility policy for breaking changes
- Contract smoke test tool emitting CONTRACT_TEST_REPORT.json
"""))

# ---- Update index file
idx_path = dst_dir / "SPEC_ADDENDUM_PACK__INDEX.md"

## v0002 additions
- New machine-checkable report schemas:
  - AUTH_SNAPSHOT_INDEX
  - VEHICLEMAP_INDEX
  - FORM_OVERLAY_AUDIT
  - CONTRACT_TEST_REPORT
- Hash-locked registries:
  - REGISTRY/SCHEMA_CATALOG.json
  - REGISTRY/CONTRACT_CATALOG.json
- Compatibility policy and contract smoke-test runner:
  - docs/COMPATIBILITY_POLICY.md
  - tools/contract_smoke_test.py
"""))

# ---- Rebuild PACK_MANIFEST.json and zip

for p in dst_dir.rglob("*"):
    if p.is_file():
        rel = str(p.relative_to(dst_dir)).replace("\\","/")
        file_records.append({
            "rel_path": rel,
            "bytes": p.stat().st_size,
            "sha256": sha256_file(p),
            "modified_utc": datetime.datetime.utcfromtimestamp(p.stat().st_mtime).replace(microsecond=0).isoformat() + "Z"
        })

pack_manifest = {
    "pack_name": dst_dir.name,
    "created_at_utc": datetime.datetime.utcnow().replace(microsecond=0).isoformat() + "Z",
    "schema_dialect": "draft-2020-12",
    "openapi_version": "3.1.0",
    "file_count": len(file_records),
    "files": sorted(file_records, key=lambda x: x["rel_path"])
}
write_json("PACK_MANIFEST.json", pack_manifest)

zip_path = Path("/mnt/data") / f"{dst_dir.name}.zip"
if zip_path.exists():
    zip_path.unlink()

with zipfile.ZipFile(zip_path, "w", compression=zipfile.ZIP_DEFLATED) as z:
    for rec in pack_manifest["files"]:
        z.write(dst_dir / rec["rel_path"], arcname=f"{dst_dir.name}/{rec['rel_path']}")

assert zip_path.exists() and zip_path.stat().st_size > 0
(zip_path.as_posix(), zip_path.stat().st_size, pack_manifest["file_count"])

import json, shutil, zipfile, hashlib, datetime, textwrap
from pathlib import Path

v0001_zip = Path("/mnt/data/LITIGATIONOS__MASTERv1.0__SPEC_ADDENDUM_v0001.zip")
assert v0001_zip.exists() and v0001_zip.stat().st_size > 0

tmp_root = Path("/mnt/data/_tmp_spec_addendum_v0001")
if tmp_root.exists():
    shutil.rmtree(tmp_root)
tmp_root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

with zipfile.ZipFile(v0001_zip, "r") as z:
    z.extractall(tmp_root)

# The zip contains a top folder named like the pack. Find it.
tops = [p for p in tmp_root.iterdir() if p.is_dir()]
assert len(tops) == 1
src_dir = tops[0]

dst_dir = Path("/mnt/data/LITIGATIONOS__MASTERv1.0__SPEC_ADDENDUM_v0002")
if dst_dir.exists():
    shutil.rmtree(dst_dir)
shutil.copytree(src_dir, dst_dir)

SCHEMA_URI = "https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema"

def write_text(rel_path: str, content: str):
    p = dst_dir / rel_path
    p.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    p.write_text(content, encoding="utf-8")
    return p

def write_json(rel_path: str, obj):
    p = dst_dir / rel_path
    p.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    p.write_text(json.dumps(obj, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
    return p

def sha256_file(p: Path) -> str:
    h = hashlib.sha256()
    with p.open("rb") as f:
        for chunk in iter(lambda: f.read(1024*1024), b""):
            h.update(chunk)
    return h.hexdigest()

# ---- New report schemas: AUTH_SNAPSHOT_INDEX, VEHICLEMAP_INDEX, FORM_OVERLAY_AUDIT
write_json("schemas/reports/auth_snapshot_index.schema.json", {
  "$schema": SCHEMA_URI,
  "$id":"litigationos://schemas/reports/auth_snapshot_index.schema.json",
  "title":"AUTH_SNAPSHOT_INDEX",
  "type":"object",
  "properties":{
    "snapshot_id":{"type":"string","pattern":"^AUTH_SNAP_[0-9]{8}_[0-9]{6}_[A-Z0-9]{6}$"},
    "generated_at":{"type":"string","format":"date-time"},
    "jurisdiction":{"type":"string","const":"Michigan"},
    "scope":{
      "type":"object",
      "properties":{
        "authority_types":{"type":"array","minItems":1,"items":{"type":"string","enum":["MCR","MCL","MRE","BENCHBOOK","SCAO_FORM","ADMIN_ORDER","CASELAW","LOCAL_ORDER"]}},
        "effective_as_of":{"type":"string","format":"date"},
        "sources":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"string","minLength":1}}
      },
      "required":["authority_types","effective_as_of","sources"],
      "additionalProperties": False
    },
    "authorities":{
      "type":"array",
      "items":{
        "type":"object",
        "properties":{
          "authority_id":{"type":"string","minLength":1},
          "authority_type":{"type":"string","enum":["MCR","MCL","MRE","BENCHBOOK","SCAO_FORM","ADMIN_ORDER","CASELAW","LOCAL_ORDER"]},
          "title":{"type":"string","minLength":1},
          "publisher":{"type":"string","minLength":1},
          "url":{"type":"string"},
          "version_label":{"type":"string"},
          "effective_date":{"type":"string","format":"date"},
          "retrieved_at":{"type":"string","format":"date-time"},
          "hashes":{"$ref":"../common/hash.schema.json#/$defs/hashes"},
          "chunks":{
            "type":"array",
            "items":{
              "type":"object",
              "properties":{
                "chunk_id":{"type":"string","minLength":1},
                "section":{"type":"string","minLength":1},
                "chunk_pointer":{"type":"string","minLength":1},
                "hashes":{"$ref":"../common/hash.schema.json#/$defs/hashes"}
              },
              "required":["chunk_id","section","chunk_pointer","hashes"],
              "additionalProperties": False
            }
          }
        },
        "required":["authority_id","authority_type","title","publisher","effective_date","retrieved_at","hashes","chunks"],
        "additionalProperties": False
      }
    },
    "blockers":{"type":"array","items":{"$ref":"../common/errors.schema.json#/$defs/error"}}
  },
  "required":["snapshot_id","generated_at","jurisdiction","scope","authorities","blockers"],
  "additionalProperties": False
})

    "vehicles":{
      "type":"array",
      "minItems":1,
      "items":{

                "requires_evidence":{"type":"array","items":{"$ref":"../common/pinpoints.schema.json#/$defs/EvidencePinpoint"}},
                "deadline_rules":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"string","minLength":1}},
                "service_requirements":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"string","minLength":1}},
                "fee_or_bond_rules":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"string","minLength":1}}

# ---- Catalogs
def build_catalog():
    schemas = []
    for p in (dst_dir / "schemas").rglob("*.schema.json"):
        schemas.append(str(p.relative_to(dst_dir)).replace("\\","/"))
    contracts = []
    for p in (dst_dir / "contracts/openapi").glob("*.openapi.yaml"):
        contracts.append(str(p.relative_to(dst_dir)).replace("\\","/"))
    return sorted(schemas), sorted(contracts)

schemas_list, contracts_list = build_catalog()

schema_catalog = {
  "catalog_id": "SCHEMA_CATALOG_v0002",
  "generated_at_utc": datetime.datetime.utcnow().replace(microsecond=0).isoformat() + "Z",
  "json_schema_dialect": "draft-2020-12",
  "schemas": [{"path": p, "sha256": sha256_file(dst_dir/p)} for p in schemas_list]
}
contract_catalog = {
  "catalog_id": "CONTRACT_CATALOG_v0002",
  "generated_at_utc": datetime.datetime.utcnow().replace(microsecond=0).isoformat() + "Z",
  "openapi_version": "3.1.0",
  "contracts": [{"path": p, "sha256": sha256_file(dst_dir/p)} for p in contracts_list]
}
write_json("REGISTRY/SCHEMA_CATALOG.json", schema_catalog)
write_json("REGISTRY/CONTRACT_CATALOG.json", contract_catalog)

# ---- Policy + test tool
write_text("docs/COMPATIBILITY_POLICY.md", textwrap.dedent("""\
# Compatibility and Breaking-Change Policy (Canonical)

OpenAPI 3.1 schema objects inherit JSON Schema Draft 2020-12. This pack locks the dialect explicitly.

## Rules
- Spec Addendum versions are monotonic: v0001, v0002, ...
- Releases are immutable: do not rewrite past zips.

## Breaking change examples
- Removing a field or endpoint
- Tightening validation constraints (e.g., min/max, pattern changes)
- Making enums closed-set where consumers expected open-set

## Hash locks
- REGISTRY/SCHEMA_CATALOG.json and REGISTRY/CONTRACT_CATALOG.json are authoritative fingerprints.
- A build gate compares the previous and current catalogs and refuses release on unrecorded drift.
"""))

  "$schema": SCHEMA_URI,
  "$id":"litigationos://schemas/reports/contract_test_report.schema.json",
  "title":"CONTRACT_TEST_REPORT",
  "type":"object",
  "properties":{
    "test_run_id":{"type":"string","pattern":"^CTEST_[0-9]{8}_[0-9]{6}_[A-Z0-9]{6}$"},
    "generated_at":{"type":"string","format":"date-time"},
    "spec_addendum_version":{"type":"string","pattern":"^v[0-9]{4}$"},
    "schemas_validated":{"type":"integer","minimum":0},
    "contracts_validated":{"type":"integer","minimum":0},
    "checks":{
      "type":"array",
      "items":{
        "type":"object",
        "properties":{
          "name":{"type":"string","minLength":1},
          "ok":{"type":"boolean"},
          "detail":{"type":"string","maxLength":8000},
          "artifacts":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"string","minLength":1}}
        },
        "required":["name","ok","detail","artifacts"],
        "additionalProperties": False
      }
    },
    "errors":{"type":"array","items":{"$ref":"../common/errors.schema.json#/$defs/error"}},
    "warnings":{"type":"array","items":{"$ref":"../common/errors.schema.json#/$defs/warning"}}
  },
  "required":["test_run_id","generated_at","spec_addendum_version","schemas_validated","contracts_validated","checks","errors","warnings"],
  "additionalProperties": False
})

write_text("tools/contract_smoke_test.py", textwrap.dedent("""\
#!/usr/bin/env python3
\"\"\"LitigationOS Spec Addendum — Contract Smoke Test (offline)

Structural checks only (no external dependencies):
- JSON Schemas: parseable JSON + Draft 2020-12 $schema.
- OpenAPI YAML: contains openapi: 3.1.0 and jsonSchemaDialect Draft 2020-12.

Usage:
  python tools/contract_smoke_test.py --root .

Output:
  reports/CONTRACT_TEST_REPORT.json
\"\"\"

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import datetime
import json
import re
import uuid
from pathlib import Path

DRAFT_2020_12 = "https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema"

def gen_id(prefix: str) -> str:
    now = datetime.datetime.utcnow().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
    token = uuid.uuid4().hex[:6].upper()
    return f"{prefix}_{now}_{token}"

def parse_args():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    ap.add_argument("--root", default=".", help="Pack root")
    return ap.parse_args()

def main() -> int:
    args = parse_args()
    root = Path(args.root).resolve()

    schemas = sorted(root.glob("schemas/**/*.schema.json"))
    contracts = sorted(root.glob("contracts/openapi/*.openapi.yaml"))

    errors = []
    warnings = []
    checks = []

    good = 0
    for sp in schemas:
        try:
            data = json.loads(sp.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
        except Exception as e:
            errors.append({"severity":"ERROR","code":"SCHEMA_JSON_PARSE_FAIL","message":str(e),"details":{"path":str(sp)}})
            continue
        if data.get("$schema") != DRAFT_2020_12:
            errors.append({"severity":"ERROR","code":"SCHEMA_DIALECT_MISMATCH","message":"$schema is not Draft 2020-12","details":{"path":str(sp),"$schema":data.get("$schema")}})
            continue
        good += 1
    checks.append({"name":"schemas_parse_and_dialect","ok":len([e for e in errors if e['code'].startswith('SCHEMA_')])==0,"detail":f"validated {good}/{len(schemas)} schemas","artifacts":[]})

    ok_contracts = 0
    for cp in contracts:
        txt = cp.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
        if not re.search(r"^openapi:\\s*3\\.1\\.0\\s*$", txt, flags=re.MULTILINE):
            errors.append({"severity":"ERROR","code":"OAS_VERSION_MISSING_OR_WRONG","message":"openapi: 3.1.0 missing","details":{"path":str(cp)}})
            continue
        if not re.search(r'^jsonSchemaDialect:\\s*\"?https://json-schema\\.org/draft/2020-12/schema\"?\\s*$', txt, flags=re.MULTILINE):
            errors.append({"severity":"ERROR","code":"OAS_DIALECT_MISSING_OR_WRONG","message":"jsonSchemaDialect missing or wrong","details":{"path":str(cp)}})
            continue
        ok_contracts += 1
    checks.append({"name":"openapi_3_1_and_dialect","ok":len([e for e in errors if e['code'].startswith('OAS_')])==0,"detail":f"checked {ok_contracts}/{len(contracts)} contracts","artifacts":[]})

    ok = len(errors) == 0

    report = {
        "test_run_id": gen_id("CTEST"),
        "generated_at": datetime.datetime.utcnow().replace(microsecond=0).isoformat()+"Z",
        "spec_addendum_version": "v0002",
        "schemas_validated": len(schemas),
        "contracts_validated": len(contracts),
        "checks": checks,
        "errors": errors,
        "warnings": warnings
    }

    out_dir = root / "reports"
    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    (out_dir / "CONTRACT_TEST_REPORT.json").write_text(json.dumps(report, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False) + "\\n", encoding="utf-8")

    return 0 if ok else 2

if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())
"""))

write_text("docs/SUPPLY_CHAIN.md", textwrap.dedent("""\
# Supply-chain Hygiene (Canonical)

SBOM and provenance are required for reproducibility and trust.

Supported SBOM formats:
- CycloneDX (JSON)
- SPDX (ISO/IEC 5962:2021)

This pack adds:
- Hash-locked catalogs for every schema and OpenAPI contract
- Breaking-change policy
- Offline contract smoke-test tool producing reports/CONTRACT_TEST_REPORT.json
"""))

# ---- Update index doc (append-only)
idx_path = dst_dir / "SPEC_ADDENDUM_PACK__INDEX.md"
idx_base = idx_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8") if idx_path.exists() else "# Spec Addendum Pack Index\n"
if "## v0002 additions" not in idx_base:
    idx_base = idx_base + "\n" + textwrap.dedent("""\
    ## v0002 additions
    - New machine-checkable report schemas:
      - AUTH_SNAPSHOT_INDEX
      - VEHICLEMAP_INDEX
      - FORM_OVERLAY_AUDIT
      - CONTRACT_TEST_REPORT
    - Hash-locked registries:
      - REGISTRY/SCHEMA_CATALOG.json
      - REGISTRY/CONTRACT_CATALOG.json
    - Compatibility policy and contract smoke-test runner:
      - docs/COMPATIBILITY_POLICY.md
      - tools/contract_smoke_test.py
    """)
write_text("SPEC_ADDENDUM_PACK__INDEX.md", idx_base)

# ---- Rebuild PACK_MANIFEST.json and zip
file_records = []
for p in dst_dir.rglob("*"):
    if p.is_file():
        rel = str(p.relative_to(dst_dir)).replace("\\","/")
        file_records.append({
            "rel_path": rel,
            "bytes": p.stat().st_size,
            "sha256": sha256_file(p),
            "modified_utc": datetime.datetime.utcfromtimestamp(p.stat().st_mtime).replace(microsecond=0).isoformat() + "Z"
        })

pack_manifest = {
    "pack_name": dst_dir.name,
    "created_at_utc": datetime.datetime.utcnow().replace(microsecond=0).isoformat() + "Z",
    "schema_dialect": "draft-2020-12",
    "openapi_version": "3.1.0",
    "file_count": len(file_records),
    "files": sorted(file_records, key=lambda x: x["rel_path"])
}
write_json("PACK_MANIFEST.json", pack_manifest)

zip_path = Path("/mnt/data") / f"{dst_dir.name}.zip"
if zip_path.exists():
    zip_path.unlink()

with zipfile.ZipFile(zip_path, "w", compression=zipfile.ZIP_DEFLATED) as z:
    for rec in pack_manifest["files"]:
        z.write(dst_dir / rec["rel_path"], arcname=f"{dst_dir.name}/{rec['rel_path']}")

assert zip_path.exists() and zip_path.stat().st_size > 0
(zip_path.as_posix(), zip_path.stat().st_size, pack_manifest["file_count"])

Result

('/mnt/data/LITIGATIONOS__MASTERv1.0__SPEC_ADDENDUM_v0002.zip', 31374, 39)

Canonical Spec GOD Grade Escalations (Autonomy, OCR, NLP, and Contract Hardening)

You already have the correct spine: Michigan authority lock + Truth-Lock + Forms-First VehicleMap + PCW/PCG fail-closed. “Spec GOD grade” is achieved when every boundary becomes machine-checkable, replayable, auditable, and enforceable—not just described.

Below is the next escalation layer, organized as hard, checkable contracts plus autonomy lanes (OCR/NLP) that never leak uncertainty into filing-capable outputs.
1) Contract Hardening to “Fail-Closed by Construction”
1.1 Lock the dialects and validation semantics

OpenAPI 3.1 inherits JSON Schema Draft 2020-12 semantics for Schema Objects. That means you must:

    Set openapi: 3.1.0 and explicitly set jsonSchemaDialect: https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema.

    Require every schema file to declare $schema Draft 2020-12, or the build fails.

Why this matters: if dialects drift, you get “valid in tool A, invalid in tool B,” which breaks determinism and auditability.
1.2 Add hash-locked catalogs + drift gates

To eliminate silent breakage:

    Maintain a SCHEMA_CATALOG (path → SHA-256) and a CONTRACT_CATALOG (OpenAPI contract → SHA-256).

    Require CI/local release gates to compare catalogs across versions and refuse release on unrecorded drift.

This is the core move that converts “a spec” into an enforceable supply chain of meaning.
1.3 Define “Breaking Change” as a first-class object

A contract should treat the following as breaking unless explicitly allowed:

    Removing fields/endpoints

    Tightening validation constraints

    Changing enum semantics from open-set to closed-set

    Changing default behavior of Truth-Lock or PCW/PCG gates

This is how you prevent “quiet weakening” over time.
1.4 Add a contract smoke-test that runs offline

A minimal gate that requires no external dependencies:

    Parse every *.schema.json

    Confirm $schema is Draft 2020-12

    Confirm every OpenAPI contract declares 3.1.0 + dialect

    Emit a machine-checkable CONTRACT_TEST_REPORT

This is the “minimum viable enforcement loop.”
2) Autonomy: Controlled Self-Driving Without Unsafe Output

Autonomy is not “agent does stuff.” Autonomy is:

    bounded

    idempotent

    replayable

    observable

    fail-closed

2.1 Run-state machine and event ledger

Define a strict run lifecycle:

    DISCOVERED → QUEUED → RUNNING → (DEGRADED|BLOCKED|DONE|FAILED)

    Every transition emits a RUN_EVENT record (append-only JSONL) with:

        run_id, step_id, ts, inputs, outputs, hashes, errors, warnings, blockers

    The UI only renders “ready-to-file” artifacts when:

        PCW core obligations are SATISFIED

        PCG passes with explicit evidence pins and authority pins

2.2 Deterministic job decomposition

Autonomy becomes reliable when jobs are small and typed:

    HARVEST_JOB: unzip → inventory → dedupe → extract text → normalize

    OCR_JOB: triggered only if text quality gate fails

    NLP_JOB: entity/quote/timeline extraction + contradiction candidates

    GRAPH_UPSERT_JOB: nodes/edges + constraints enforced

    PROOF_EVAL_JOB: compute PO status + blockers

    PACKAGE_JOB: compile bundles only when gates pass

Each job:

    has a typed input/output schema

    writes a run ledger entry

    is idempotent (same inputs → same outputs)

2.3 Local service boundaries must be explicit

Your charter correctly lists boundaries:

    UI IPC ↔ Orchestrator ↔ Neo4j ↔ Tika ↔ OCR ↔ Ollama ↔ rclone

Spec GOD grade requires:

    consistent request envelope (request_id, run_id, idempotency_key, case_profile)

    consistent error taxonomy (fatal/blocker/warn)

    consistent provenance fields on every output object

3) OCR Lane: “Text is Evidence” Discipline

OCR is not a feature; it is a risk surface. You only run it when required, you keep originals immutable, and you produce explicit provenance.
3.1 OCR trigger gate (objective, measurable)

Before OCR:

    measure extracted text coverage (bytes, character classes, page coverage)

    detect “image-only” PDFs vs embedded text

    classify “low quality” (e.g., empty/near-empty, garbage extraction)

If below threshold → OCR lane.
3.2 OCR toolchain contract

For offline-first and reproducibility:

    Use OCRmyPDF as the orchestrator for PDF OCR, because it provides standardized workflows (deskew, rotation handling, optimization) and outputs searchable PDFs while preserving layout.

    Canonical controls:

        --deskew for slight skew

        --rotate-pages for cardinal rotation issues

    Produce:

        OCR_REPORT.json (per page: engine, confidence proxies, errors)

        OCR_TEXT.txt (canonical text)

        OCR_PDF.pdf (searchable output)

        hashes for every produced artifact

3.3 OCR provenance must be mandatory

Every OCR’d page must map to:

    EvidencePinpoint (source_path, page, capture time, hash)

    OCR parameters (engine version, flags)

    quality indicators and blockers

No OCR output may directly satisfy a PO unless:

    it is pinned to original source and page

    it is linked to a QuotePin that is reviewed/confirmed by downstream validators (human or deterministic checks)

4) NLP Lane: QuoteDB, ChronoDB, Contradiction Map—All With Pins

NLP must never produce “facts.” It produces:

    candidates

    structured extractions

    links

    confidence + provenance

    and it must always be reversible back to source pins.

4.1 Canonical NLP outputs (machine-checkable)

Define schemas for:

    QUOTE_DB.jsonl: each quote has source pin + text span + normalized speaker/role + timestamp

    CHRONO_DB.jsonl: bitemporal events with:

        event_time (alleged occurrence)

        record_time (document creation/filing time)

        pins to supporting quotes/exhibits

    ENTITY_LEDGER.jsonl: extracted entities with canonical IDs and alias sets

    CONTRADICTION_MAP.json: conflict edges between QuotePins (with explicit rationale and pins)

4.2 Hallucination containment rule

NLP/LLM outputs are never “true” by default:

    They are DISPUTED until:

        linked to a QuotePin

        and the QuotePin resolves to a pinned location in a source artifact
        This is your Truth-Lock principle, but applied to NLP products.

4.3 LLM usage boundary (offline)

Local inference (Ollama) is a tool, not an authority:

    LLM may propose:

        candidate entities

        candidate timelines

        candidate VehicleMap routes

    LLM may not:

        invent authority

        invent facts

        mark proof obligations SATISFIED without pins

5) Extraction Boundary: Tika and rclone Contracts Must Be First-Class
5.1 Tika adapter contract

Tika is a local service. Treat it as such:

    Standardize a health check and version endpoint

    Standardize extraction request/response objects

    Record host/port used; Tika commonly defaults to localhost:9998 when not specified.

5.2 rclone adapter contract (sync, bisync, and RC)

rclone must be treated as:

    an operator-controlled tool

    invoked deterministically

    with logs captured, hashed, and stored

Remote control (rc) endpoints enable structured automation; rclone rc and rclone rcd are the canonical primitives.

Your spec should require:

    “no background side effects without a RUN record”

    explicit dry-run modes where possible

    a per-run mirror manifest (what changed, hashes, conflict resolution)

6) Supply Chain: SBOM + Provenance as Court-Grade Defensibility

For “spec GOD grade,” you need the same defensibility you demand from evidence:
6.1 SBOM formats and why

    CycloneDX provides official schemas and a modular object model for components/dependencies.

    SPDX is an ISO-recognized standard (ISO/IEC 5962:2021) for software supply chain artifacts and licensing vocabulary.

6.2 SLSA-inspired provenance controls

SLSA is an industry framework for improving build integrity and provenance; you do not need to adopt it wholesale, but you should:

    record build inputs/outputs

    record build environment versions

    hash release artifacts

    keep an append-only build ledger

This is how your LitigationOS becomes tamper-evident.
7) What “Spec GOD Grade” Next Means in Concrete Pack Terms

You now want v0003–v0005 to lock the remaining autonomy/OCR/NLP surfaces:
v0003 — Run State + Event Stream Contracts

    RUN_STATE.schema.json

    RUN_EVENT.schema.json (JSONL line schema)

    OpenAPI endpoints for:

        /runs create/list

        /runs/{run_id} get status

        /runs/{run_id}/events stream (SSE/NDJSON)

        /artifacts/{artifact_id} provenance fetch

v0004 — OCR Adapter Contract + OCR_REPORT Schema

    OCR_REQUEST.schema.json

    OCR_RESULT.schema.json

    OCR_REPORT.schema.json (page-level metrics, parameters, blockers)

    Standardize OCRmyPDF parameter set and capture them in reports

v0005 — NLP Adapter Contract + Quote/Chrono/Contradiction Schemas

    NLP_REQUEST.schema.json

    NLP_RESULT.schema.json

    QUOTE_DB_LINE.schema.json

    CHRONO_DB_LINE.schema.json

    CONTRADICTION_MAP.schema.json

    “DISPUTED until pinned” rule encoded as required fields and statuses

8) Delivered: Spec Addendum Pack v0002 (Schemas + Hash Locks + Smoke Test)

This continuation pack contains:

    New machine-checkable schemas:

        AUTH_SNAPSHOT_INDEX

        VEHICLEMAP_INDEX

        FORM_OVERLAY_AUDIT

        CONTRACT_TEST_REPORT

    Hash-locked catalogs:

        REGISTRY/SCHEMA_CATALOG.json
