import os, re, json, csv, hashlib, time, zipfile, pathlib, mimetypes, textwrap
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from pathlib import Path

ROOT_IN = Path("/mnt/data")
run_ts = datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ")
out_root = ROOT_IN / f"LITIGATIONOS_FORMS_LANES_HARVEST__RUN_{run_ts}"
(out_root / "OUT" / "REPORTS").mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
(out_root / "OUT" / "ENVELOPES").mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
(out_root / "OUT" / "DERIVED").mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
(out_root / "tools").mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
(out_root / "input_templates").mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
(out_root / "docs").mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

def sha256_file(p: Path, chunk=1024*1024):
    h = hashlib.sha256()
    with p.open("rb") as f:
        while True:
            b = f.read(chunk)
            if not b:
                break
            h.update(b)
    return h.hexdigest()

def sha256_stream(fobj, chunk=1024*1024):
    h = hashlib.sha256()
    while True:
        b = fobj.read(chunk)
        if not b:
            break
        h.update(b)
    return h.hexdigest()

def crc32_hex(crc_int: int) -> str:
    return f"{crc_int & 0xffffffff:08x}"

def guess_mime(path_str: str) -> str:
    mt, _ = mimetypes.guess_type(path_str)
    return mt or "application/octet-stream"

ZIP_EXT = {".zip"}
SKIP_HASH_EXT = {".gguf",".bin",".safetensors",".pt",".pth",".onnx",".tar",".7z",".mp4",".mkv",".mov",".wav",".mp3",".flac"}

def lane_classify(name: str) -> dict:
    s = name.lower()
    tags = []
    lane = "UNCLASSIFIED"
    if "scao" in s or "scao-approved" in s:
        lane = "MI_SCAO_FORMS"
        tags.append("scao")
    if re.search(r"\b(mcr|michigan court rules|court-rules)\b", s) or "michigan-court-rules" in s:
        lane = "MI_AUTHORITY_MCR"
        tags.append("mcr")
    if "benchbook" in s or "dvbb" in s or "benchbooks" in s:
        tags.append("benchbook")
        lane = lane if lane != "UNCLASSIFIED" else "MI_BENCHBOOKS"
    if "muskegon" in s:
        tags.append("muskegon")
        lane = lane if lane != "UNCLASSIFIED" else "MUSKEGON_LOCAL"
    if "wdmi" in s or "western district of michigan" in s:
        tags.append("wdmi")
        lane = lane if lane != "UNCLASSIFIED" else "FED_WDMI"
    if "scotus" in s or "supreme court of the united states" in s:
        tags.append("scotus")
        lane = lane if lane != "UNCLASSIFIED" else "FED_SCOTUS"
    if "rclone" in s:
        tags.append("rclone")
        lane = lane if lane != "UNCLASSIFIED" else "TOOLING_RCLONE"
    if "neo4j" in s or "bloom" in s:
        tags.append("neo4j")
        lane = lane if lane != "UNCLASSIFIED" else "GRAPH_STACK"
    if "pii" in s or "deident" in s or "de-ident" in s or "ner" in s:
        tags.append("pii")
        lane = lane if lane != "UNCLASSIFIED" else "PII_TOOLING"
    if re.search(r"\b(mc|cc|dc|pc|foc|jc)\s*\d+", s) and s.endswith(".pdf"):
        tags.append("form_number_like")
        lane = "MI_SCAO_FORMS"
    if "instruction" in s and s.endswith(".pdf"):
        tags.append("instructions_like")
        lane = "MI_SCAO_FORMS_INSTRUCTIONS" if lane == "UNCLASSIFIED" else lane
    return {"lane": lane, "tags": tags}

# Inventory top-level uploads
uploads = []
for p in sorted(ROOT_IN.iterdir()):
    if p.is_file():
        uploads.append(p)

bundle_registry = []
for p in uploads:
    try:
        h = sha256_file(p)
    except Exception as e:
        h = None
    bundle_registry.append({
        "observed_path": str(p),
        "basename": p.name,
        "bytes": p.stat().st_size,
        "sha256": h,
        "ext": p.suffix.lower(),
        "observed_at_utc": run_ts,
    })

(Path(out_root / "OUT" / "REPORTS" / "INPUT_BUNDLE_REGISTRY.json")).write_text(
    json.dumps({"run_utc": run_ts, "bundles": bundle_registry}, indent=2),
    encoding="utf-8"
)

# Inventory contents
records = []
zip_summaries = []
max_bytes_to_hash_total = 800 * 1024 * 1024  # 800MB budget for inner hashing (fail-soft if exceeded)
bytes_hashed = 0

for p in uploads:
    ext = p.suffix.lower()
    if ext in ZIP_EXT:
        zsum = {
            "zip_path": str(p),
            "zip_sha256": next((b["sha256"] for b in bundle_registry if b["observed_path"] == str(p)), None),
            "zip_bytes": p.stat().st_size,
            "entries": 0,
            "files": 0,
            "dirs": 0,
            "hashed_entries": 0,
            "hash_budget_bytes": max_bytes_to_hash_total,
            "bytes_hashed": 0,
            "skipped_hash_entries": 0,
            "skipped_hash_reasons": {},
        }
        try:
            with zipfile.ZipFile(p, "r") as zf:
                for zi in zf.infolist():
                    zsum["entries"] += 1
                    is_dir = zi.is_dir()
                    if is_dir:
                        zsum["dirs"] += 1
                        continue
                    zsum["files"] += 1
                    inner_name = zi.filename
                    inner_ext = Path(inner_name).suffix.lower()
                    lane_info = lane_classify(inner_name)
                    rec = {
                        "record_type": "zip_entry",
                        "zip_origin": str(p),
                        "zip_sha256": zsum["zip_sha256"],
                        "entry_name": inner_name,
                        "entry_bytes": zi.file_size,
                        "entry_compressed_bytes": zi.compress_size,
                        "entry_crc32": crc32_hex(zi.CRC),
                        "entry_sha256": None,
                        "hash_status": "NOT_COMPUTED",
                        "hash_skipped_reason": None,
                        "mime_guess": guess_mime(inner_name),
                        "ext": inner_ext,
                        "lane": lane_info["lane"],
                        "tags": lane_info["tags"],
                        "observed_at_utc": run_ts,
                    }
                    should_hash = True
                    if inner_ext in SKIP_HASH_EXT:
                        should_hash = False
                        rec["hash_status"] = "SKIPPED"
                        rec["hash_skipped_reason"] = f"EXT_IN_SKIP_LIST:{inner_ext}"
                    elif zi.file_size > 80 * 1024 * 1024:
                        should_hash = False
                        rec["hash_status"] = "SKIPPED"
                        rec["hash_skipped_reason"] = "SIZE_GT_80MB"
                    elif bytes_hashed + zi.file_size > max_bytes_to_hash_total:
                        should_hash = False
                        rec["hash_status"] = "SKIPPED"
                        rec["hash_skipped_reason"] = "HASH_BUDGET_EXCEEDED"
                    if should_hash:
                        try:
                            with zf.open(zi, "r") as f:
                                rec["entry_sha256"] = sha256_stream(f)
                            rec["hash_status"] = "COMPUTED"
                            zsum["hashed_entries"] += 1
                            bytes_hashed += zi.file_size
                            zsum["bytes_hashed"] = bytes_hashed
                        except Exception as e:
                            rec["hash_status"] = "ERROR"
                            rec["hash_skipped_reason"] = f"EXC:{type(e).__name__}"
                    else:
                        zsum["skipped_hash_entries"] += 1
                        r = rec["hash_skipped_reason"] or "UNKNOWN"
                        zsum["skipped_hash_reasons"][r] = zsum["skipped_hash_reasons"].get(r, 0) + 1
                    records.append(rec)
        except Exception as e:
            zsum["error"] = f"{type(e).__name__}: {e}"
        zip_summaries.append(zsum)
    else:
        lane_info = lane_classify(p.name)
        rec = {
            "record_type": "file",
            "observed_path": str(p),
            "basename": p.name,
            "bytes": p.stat().st_size,
            "sha256": next((b["sha256"] for b in bundle_registry if b["observed_path"] == str(p)), None),
            "ext": ext,
            "mime_guess": guess_mime(p.name),
            "lane": lane_info["lane"],
            "tags": lane_info["tags"],
            "observed_at_utc": run_ts,
        }
        records.append(rec)

# Write manifests
(Path(out_root / "OUT" / "REPORTS" / "FILE_MANIFEST.jsonl")).write_text(
    "\n".join(json.dumps(r, ensure_ascii=False) for r in records) + "\n",
    encoding="utf-8"
)
(Path(out_root / "OUT" / "REPORTS" / "ZIP_SUMMARIES.json")).write_text(
    json.dumps({"run_utc": run_ts, "zip_summaries": zip_summaries}, indent=2),
    encoding="utf-8"
)

# Build indices
by_lane = {}
by_ext = {}
for r in records:
    lane = r.get("lane","UNCLASSIFIED")
    by_lane.setdefault(lane, 0)
    by_lane[lane] += 1
    ex = r.get("ext","").lower() or "(none)"
    by_ext.setdefault(ex, 0)
    by_ext[ex] += 1

(Path(out_root / "OUT" / "REPORTS" / "INDEX_BY_LANE.json")).write_text(
    json.dumps({"run_utc": run_ts, "counts": by_lane}, indent=2),
    encoding="utf-8"
)
(Path(out_root / "OUT" / "REPORTS" / "INDEX_BY_EXTENSION.json")).write_text(
    json.dumps({"run_utc": run_ts, "counts": by_ext}, indent=2),
    encoding="utf-8"
)

# PINPOINT_MISSING: datasets not present yet
missing = [
    {
        "missing_id": "SCAO_FORMS_FULL_PDF_CORPUS",
        "need": "A complete local corpus of SCAO-approved form PDFs and any paired instruction PDFs (or a captured index export enabling deterministic download).",
        "why": "Required to populate the MI forms pillar and to map relief->vehicle->form across all courts.",
        "acquire_plan": [
            "Use the official SCAO Forms page to export/search and collect the full list of form PDF URLs and any instruction URLs.",
            "Save the list as input_templates/scao_forms_index.csv (schema provided) or scao_forms_index.json.",
            "Run tools/scao_forms_downloader.py with --paths-config pointing at $ROOT\\config\\paths.json to download into OUT\\DERIVED\\SCAO_FORMS\\... and emit manifests/envelopes."
        ]
    },
    {
        "missing_id": "MUSKEGON_LOCAL_PACKETS_PDF",
        "need": "Local Muskegon County court packet PDFs (Probate packets, Circuit filing packets, local forms).",
        "why": "Required to add the Muskegon-specific procedural lane and local-packet node set.",
        "acquire_plan": [
            "Download local packets from Muskegon County official court pages (Probate/Circuit) and save into an intake ZIP you upload here.",
            "Run the same harvester to index and classify them as MUSKEGON_LOCAL."
        ]
    },
    {
        "missing_id": "WDMI_PRO_SE_FORMS_CORPUS",
        "need": "WDMI pro se form PDFs and instructions downloaded locally as a corpus.",
        "why": "Required to populate the federal lane for Western District of Michigan (civil/criminal).",
        "acquire_plan": [
            "Download WDMI pro se forms PDF set from the official WDMI pro se forms page.",
            "Upload as a ZIP and re-run harvest to index them under FED_WDMI."
        ]
    },
    {
        "missing_id": "SCOTUS_FILING_PORTAL_EXPORT",
        "need": "SCOTUS filing portal exports (rules PDFs, forms, guidance) captured as local files.",
        "why": "Required to populate SCOTUS lane references and portal/filing constraints.",
        "acquire_plan": [
            "Download the Supreme Court Rules PDF and any filing guidance/portal exports from official SCOTUS sources.",
            "Upload as a ZIP and re-run harvest to index them under FED_SCOTUS."
        ]
    }
]
(Path(out_root / "OUT" / "REPORTS" / "PINPOINT_MISSING.json")).write_text(
    json.dumps({"run_utc": run_ts, "missing": missing}, indent=2),
    encoding="utf-8"
)

# Template index schema for SCAO forms download
scao_csv = out_root / "input_templates" / "scao_forms_index.csv"
with scao_csv.open("w", newline="", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    w = csv.writer(f)
    w.writerow(["form_number","title","category","pdf_url","instructions_url","revision_date","notes"])
    # No sample rows to avoid placeholders; schema only.

# tools: scao_forms_downloader.py (no external calls executed here)
downloader_py = r'''#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
LitigationOS — SCAO Forms Corpus Downloader (offline-first, operator-controlled)

Purpose
- Read a captured index export of SCAO form links (CSV or JSON).
- Download form PDFs + instruction PDFs into canonical OUT layout.
- Emit:
  - FILE_MANIFEST.jsonl (downloaded artifacts)
  - TRANSFORM_ENVELOPES (one per download)
  - RUN_REPORT (counts + failures)
- Fail-closed behaviors are available via --strict.

Important
- This script is intended to run on YOUR Windows machine.
- It does not embed any URLs; it consumes your captured index export.
- No model weights or binaries are bundled.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import csv
import hashlib
import json
import os
import sys
import time
from dataclasses import dataclass
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Dict, Iterable, List, Optional, Tuple

try:
    import requests
except Exception as e:
    requests = None

def sha256_bytes_iter(it: Iterable[bytes]) -> str:
    h = hashlib.sha256()
    for b in it:
        h.update(b)
    return h.hexdigest()

def sha256_file(p: Path, chunk: int = 1024 * 1024) -> str:
    h = hashlib.sha256()
    with p.open("rb") as f:
        while True:
            b = f.read(chunk)
            if not b:
                break
            h.update(b)
    return h.hexdigest()

def utc_ts() -> str:
    return datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ")

def read_paths_config(paths_json: Path) -> Dict[str, str]:
    data = json.loads(paths_json.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    if not isinstance(data, dict) or "ROOT" not in data:
        raise ValueError("paths.json must be an object with at least a ROOT field.")
    return {k: str(v) for k, v in data.items()}

def ensure_dir(p: Path) -> None:
    p.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

def safe_filename(name: str) -> str:
    # Windows-safe basename
    bad = '<>:"/\\|?*'
    out = "".join("_" if c in bad else c for c in name)
    out = out.strip().rstrip(".")
    return out or "file"

@dataclass
class FormRow:
    form_number: str
    title: str
    category: str
    pdf_url: str
    instructions_url: str
    revision_date: str
    notes: str

def load_index_csv(path: Path) -> List[FormRow]:
    rows: List[FormRow] = []
    with path.open("r", encoding="utf-8", newline="") as f:
        reader = csv.DictReader(f)
        required = ["form_number","title","category","pdf_url","instructions_url","revision_date","notes"]
        for r in required:
            if r not in reader.fieldnames:
                raise ValueError(f"CSV missing required column: {r}")
        for d in reader:
            rows.append(FormRow(
                form_number=(d.get("form_number") or "").strip(),
                title=(d.get("title") or "").strip(),
                category=(d.get("category") or "").strip(),
                pdf_url=(d.get("pdf_url") or "").strip(),
                instructions_url=(d.get("instructions_url") or "").strip(),
                revision_date=(d.get("revision_date") or "").strip(),
                notes=(d.get("notes") or "").strip(),
            ))
    return rows

def load_index_json(path: Path) -> List[FormRow]:
    obj = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    if not isinstance(obj, list):
        raise ValueError("JSON index must be a list of objects.")
    rows: List[FormRow] = []
    for d in obj:
        if not isinstance(d, dict):
            continue
        rows.append(FormRow(
            form_number=str(d.get("form_number","")).strip(),
            title=str(d.get("title","")).strip(),
            category=str(d.get("category","")).strip(),
            pdf_url=str(d.get("pdf_url","")).strip(),
            instructions_url=str(d.get("instructions_url","")).strip(),
            revision_date=str(d.get("revision_date","")).strip(),
            notes=str(d.get("notes","")).strip(),
        ))
    return rows

def http_get(url: str, timeout: int = 60, retries: int = 3, backoff: float = 1.5) -> bytes:
    if requests is None:
        raise RuntimeError("requests is not installed. Install with: pip install requests")
    last_exc: Optional[Exception] = None
    for i in range(retries):
        try:
            r = requests.get(url, timeout=timeout, stream=True, headers={"User-Agent":"LitigationOS-FormsHarvester/1.0"})
            r.raise_for_status()
            # Content-type guard (soft): accept if pdf or octet-stream
            ct = (r.headers.get("content-type") or "").lower()
            data = b"".join(r.iter_content(chunk_size=1024*256))
            if data[:4] != b"%PDF":
                # Some endpoints may return non-PDF HTML errors; fail.
                raise ValueError(f"Downloaded content does not appear to be a PDF (content-type={ct}).")
            return data
        except Exception as e:
            last_exc = e
            time.sleep(backoff * (i + 1))
    raise RuntimeError(f"Failed download after {retries} attempts: {url} :: {type(last_exc).__name__}: {last_exc}")

def write_envelope(envelope_path: Path, *, origin_url: str, origin_note: str, out_path: Path, out_sha256: str, paths_config_source: str, paths_config_sha256: str, paths_expanded: Dict[str,str], tool_name: str, tool_version: str, params: Dict[str, object]) -> None:
    env = {
        "schema": "LITIGATIONOS_TRANSFORM_ENVELOPE_v1",
        "created_at_utc": utc_ts(),
        "cas_origin_pointer": {
            "sha256": out_sha256,
            "observed_path": str(out_path),
            "observed_at_utc": utc_ts(),
            "ingest_method": origin_note,
            "origin_url": origin_url,
        },
        "transform": {
            "tool": tool_name,
            "version": tool_version,
            "params": params,
        },
        "digests": {
            "input": [],
            "output": [{"sha256": out_sha256, "path": str(out_path)}]
        },
        "config_snapshot": {
            "paths_config_source": paths_config_source,
            "paths_config_sha256": paths_config_sha256,
            "paths_expanded": paths_expanded,
        }
    }
    envelope_path.write_text(json.dumps(env, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")

def main() -> int:
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    ap.add_argument("--paths-config", required=True, help="Path to $ROOT\\config\\paths.json")
    ap.add_argument("--index", required=True, help="Path to scao_forms_index.csv or scao_forms_index.json")
    ap.add_argument("--strict", action="store_true", help="Fail-closed: missing urls/fields abort.")
    ap.add_argument("--timeout", type=int, default=60)
    ap.add_argument("--retries", type=int, default=3)
    args = ap.parse_args()

    paths_cfg_path = Path(args.paths_config).expanduser().resolve()
    paths = read_paths_config(paths_cfg_path)
    root = Path(paths["ROOT"]).resolve()

    out_base = root / "OUT"
    derived = out_base / "DERIVED" / "SCAO_FORMS"
    pdf_dir = derived / "PDF"
    inst_dir = derived / "INSTRUCTIONS"
    reports = out_base / "REPORTS" / "SCAO_FORMS"
    envelopes = out_base / "ENVELOPES" / "SCAO_FORMS"

    for d in [pdf_dir, inst_dir, reports, envelopes]:
        ensure_dir(d)

    paths_cfg_sha = sha256_file(paths_cfg_path)
    paths_cfg_source = str(paths_cfg_path)

    idx_path = Path(args.index).expanduser().resolve()
    if idx_path.suffix.lower() == ".csv":
        rows = load_index_csv(idx_path)
    elif idx_path.suffix.lower() == ".json":
        rows = load_index_json(idx_path)
    else:
        raise ValueError("Index must be .csv or .json")

    manifest_path = reports / "FILE_MANIFEST.jsonl"
    run_report_path = reports / "RUN_REPORT.json"
    failures: List[Dict[str,str]] = []
    wrote = 0
    skipped = 0

    with manifest_path.open("w", encoding="utf-8") as mf:
        for row in rows:
            if not row.pdf_url:
                if args.strict:
                    raise ValueError(f"Missing pdf_url for form {row.form_number} {row.title}")
                skipped += 1
                continue

            fn_base = safe_filename((row.form_number or "FORM").strip() + "__" + (row.title or "Untitled").strip())
            pdf_path = pdf_dir / f"{fn_base}.pdf"

            try:
                pdf_bytes = http_get(row.pdf_url, timeout=args.timeout, retries=args.retries)
                pdf_path.write_bytes(pdf_bytes)
                pdf_sha = sha256_file(pdf_path)

                env_path = envelopes / f"{fn_base}__PDF__ENVELOPE.json"
                write_envelope(
                    env_path,
                    origin_url=row.pdf_url,
                    origin_note="download:scao_forms_index",
                    out_path=pdf_path,
                    out_sha256=pdf_sha,
                    paths_config_source=paths_cfg_source,
                    paths_config_sha256=paths_cfg_sha,
                    paths_expanded=paths,
                    tool_name="scao_forms_downloader",
                    tool_version="1.0.0",
                    params={"timeout": args.timeout, "retries": args.retries, "index_path": str(idx_path)},
                )

                rec = {
                    "record_type": "downloaded_form_pdf",
                    "form_number": row.form_number,
                    "title": row.title,
                    "category": row.category,
                    "revision_date": row.revision_date,
                    "source_url": row.pdf_url,
                    "output_path": str(pdf_path),
                    "sha256": pdf_sha,
                    "bytes": pdf_path.stat().st_size,
                    "created_at_utc": utc_ts(),
                }
                mf.write(json.dumps(rec, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
                wrote += 1
            except Exception as e:
                failures.append({"form_number": row.form_number, "title": row.title, "url": row.pdf_url, "error": f"{type(e).__name__}: {e}"})
                if args.strict:
                    raise

            if row.instructions_url:
                inst_path = inst_dir / f"{fn_base}__INSTRUCTIONS.pdf"
                try:
                    inst_bytes = http_get(row.instructions_url, timeout=args.timeout, retries=args.retries)
                    inst_path.write_bytes(inst_bytes)
                    inst_sha = sha256_file(inst_path)

                    env_path = envelopes / f"{fn_base}__INSTRUCTIONS__ENVELOPE.json"
                    write_envelope(
                        env_path,
                        origin_url=row.instructions_url,
                        origin_note="download:scao_forms_index",
                        out_path=inst_path,
                        out_sha256=inst_sha,
                        paths_config_source=paths_cfg_source,
                        paths_config_sha256=paths_cfg_sha,
                        paths_expanded=paths,
                        tool_name="scao_forms_downloader",
                        tool_version="1.0.0",
                        params={"timeout": args.timeout, "retries": args.retries, "index_path": str(idx_path)},
                    )

                    rec = {
                        "record_type": "downloaded_form_instructions_pdf",
                        "form_number": row.form_number,
                        "title": row.title,
                        "category": row.category,
                        "revision_date": row.revision_date,
                        "source_url": row.instructions_url,
                        "output_path": str(inst_path),
                        "sha256": inst_sha,
                        "bytes": inst_path.stat().st_size,
                        "created_at_utc": utc_ts(),
                    }
                    mf.write(json.dumps(rec, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
                except Exception as e:
                    failures.append({"form_number": row.form_number, "title": row.title, "url": row.instructions_url, "error": f"{type(e).__name__}: {e}"})
                    if args.strict:
                        raise

    run_report = {
        "run_utc": utc_ts(),
        "paths_config_source": str(paths_cfg_path),
        "paths_config_sha256": sha256_file(paths_cfg_path),
        "index_path": str(idx_path),
        "downloaded_pdf_count": wrote,
        "skipped_rows": skipped,
        "failures": failures,
    }
    run_report_path.write_text(json.dumps(run_report, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
    print(json.dumps(run_report, indent=2))
    return 0 if (not args.strict or len(failures) == 0) else 2

if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())
'''
(Path(out_root / "tools" / "scao_forms_downloader.py")).write_text(downloader_py, encoding="utf-8")

# docs: acquisition instructions (no placeholders; just exact steps)
acq_md = r'''# Acquisition Plan — Forms & Packets Corpus (Michigan + Federal lanes)

This run harvested *only what you uploaded into this conversation* and produced machine-checkable inventories.

## A) SCAO Forms (Statewide) — recommended acquisition method

Authoritative search/catalog is served via the Michigan Courts SCAO forms site. citeturn5search1turn1search31

### Goal
Produce a **local corpus** of:
- SCAO-approved form PDFs, and
- any paired instruction PDFs (when published),
plus a deterministic **index export** that can be re-used for reproducible downloads.

### Steps (Windows)
1. Create a folder for the corpus:
   - `C:\Users\andre\Downloads\LITIGATIONOS__MASTERv1.0__FORMS_CORPUS\input`
2. Capture an index export:
   - Use the Michigan Courts SCAO forms site to locate forms by category and by form number. citeturn5search1turn1search31
   - For each form you want in the corpus, capture:
     - `form_number`, `title`, `category`, `pdf_url`, and (if available) `instructions_url`.
   - Save as: `input\scao_forms_index.csv` using the provided schema (see `input_templates\scao_forms_index.csv` in this pack).
3. Prepare `paths.json` (single source of truth):
   - Put it at: `C:\Users\andre\Downloads\LITIGATIONOS__MASTERv1.0__FORMS_CORPUS\config\paths.json`
   - Minimum fields:
     - `ROOT`: `C:\\Users\\andre\\Downloads\\LITIGATIONOS__MASTERv1.0__FORMS_CORPUS`
4. Run downloader:
   - `python tools\scao_forms_downloader.py --paths-config config\paths.json --index input\scao_forms_index.csv --strict`
5. Output:
   - PDFs: `OUT\DERIVED\SCAO_FORMS\PDF\...`
   - Instruction PDFs: `OUT\DERIVED\SCAO_FORMS\INSTRUCTIONS\...`
   - Manifests: `OUT\REPORTS\SCAO_FORMS\...`
   - Envelopes: `OUT\ENVELOPES\SCAO_FORMS\...`

## B) Muskegon local packets (Probate/Circuit/Friend of the Court)
Muskegon County posts some packet guidance and may link to local packet PDFs; example guardianship packet guidance is published on the county site. citeturn0search9

### Goal
Download any local packet PDFs and upload as ZIP to ingest under the `MUSKEGON_LOCAL` lane.

### Steps
1. Download packet PDFs from official Muskegon County court pages. citeturn0search9
2. Zip them (preserve filenames).
3. Upload here; re-run the harvester to index them.

## C) Federal lane — Western District of Michigan (WDMI) pro se forms
WDMI publishes an official pro se forms download page. citeturn0search0

### Steps
1. Download the WDMI pro se forms set. citeturn0search0
2. Zip the PDFs and upload here.
3. Re-run harvest to index them under `FED_WDMI`.

## D) SCOTUS lane — filing guidance / rules
The Supreme Court provides an official rules document; use it as the SCOTUS authority anchor. citeturn0search1

### Steps
1. Download SCOTUS rules / filing guidance. citeturn0search1
2. Zip and upload here.
3. Re-run harvest to index them under `FED_SCOTUS`.
'''
(Path(out_root / "docs" / "ACQUIRE_FORMS_AND_PACKETS.md")).write_text(acq_md, encoding="utf-8")

# Create a release zip
release_zip = ROOT_IN / f"LITIGATIONOS_FORMS_LANES_HARVEST_PACK__{run_ts}.zip"
if release_zip.exists():
    release_zip.unlink()

def zip_dir(src: Path, dst_zip: Path):
    with zipfile.ZipFile(dst_zip, "w", compression=zipfile.ZIP_DEFLATED) as z:
        for p in src.rglob("*"):
            if p.is_file():
                z.write(p, p.relative_to(src).as_posix())

zip_dir(out_root, release_zip)

release_zip, out_root, len(records), len(zip_summaries), bytes_hashed

Always show details
import os, re, json, csv, hashlib, zipfile, mimetypes
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from pathlib import Path

ROOT_IN = Path("/mnt/data")
run_ts = datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ")
out_root = ROOT_IN / f"LITIGATIONOS_FORMS_LANES_HARVEST__RUN_{run_ts}"
(out_root / "OUT" / "REPORTS").mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
(out_root / "OUT" / "ENVELOPES").mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
(out_root / "OUT" / "DERIVED").mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
(out_root / "tools").mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
(out_root / "input_templates").mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
(out_root / "docs").mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

def sha256_file(p: Path, chunk=1024*1024):
    h = hashlib.sha256()
    with p.open("rb") as f:
        while True:
            b = f.read(chunk)
            if not b:
                break
            h.update(b)
    return h.hexdigest()

def sha256_stream(fobj, chunk=1024*1024):
    h = hashlib.sha256()
    while True:
        b = fobj.read(chunk)
        if not b:
            break
        h.update(b)
    return h.hexdigest()

def crc32_hex(crc_int: int) -> str:
    return f"{crc_int & 0xffffffff:08x}"

def guess_mime(path_str: str) -> str:
    mt, _ = mimetypes.guess_type(path_str)
    return mt or "application/octet-stream"

ZIP_EXT = {".zip"}
SKIP_HASH_EXT = {".gguf",".bin",".safetensors",".pt",".pth",".onnx",".tar",".7z",".mp4",".mkv",".mov",".wav",".mp3",".flac"}

def lane_classify(name: str) -> dict:
    s = name.lower()
    tags = []
    lane = "UNCLASSIFIED"
    # Michigan authority
    if "michigan-court-rules" in s or re.search(r"\b(mcr|michigan court rules|court[- ]rules)\b", s):
        lane = "MI_AUTHORITY_MCR"
        tags.append("mcr")
    if "mcl" in s:
        tags.append("mcl")
        lane = lane if lane != "UNCLASSIFIED" else "MI_AUTHORITY_MCL"
    if "mre" in s:
        tags.append("mre")
        lane = lane if lane != "UNCLASSIFIED" else "MI_AUTHORITY_MRE"
    if "benchbook" in s or "benchbooks" in s:
        tags.append("benchbook")
        lane = lane if lane != "UNCLASSIFIED" else "MI_BENCHBOOKS"
    # Forms
    if "scao" in s or "scao-approved" in s:
        tags.append("scao")
        lane = "MI_SCAO_FORMS" if lane == "UNCLASSIFIED" else lane
    if re.search(r"\b(mc|cc|dc|pc|foc|jc)\s*\d+[a-z]?\b", s) and s.endswith(".pdf"):
        tags.append("form_number_like")
        lane = "MI_SCAO_FORMS"
    if "instruction" in s and s.endswith(".pdf"):
        tags.append("instructions_like")
        lane = "MI_SCAO_FORMS_INSTRUCTIONS" if lane == "UNCLASSIFIED" else lane
    # Local / federal
    if "muskegon" in s:
        tags.append("muskegon")
        lane = lane if lane != "UNCLASSIFIED" else "MUSKEGON_LOCAL"
    if "wdmi" in s or "western district of michigan" in s:
        tags.append("wdmi")
        lane = lane if lane != "UNCLASSIFIED" else "FED_WDMI"
    if "scotus" in s or "supreme court of the united states" in s:
        tags.append("scotus")
        lane = lane if lane != "UNCLASSIFIED" else "FED_SCOTUS"
    # Tooling
    if "rclone" in s:
        tags.append("rclone")
        lane = lane if lane != "UNCLASSIFIED" else "TOOLING_RCLONE"
    if "neo4j" in s or "bloom" in s or "erd" in s:
        tags.append("graph")
        lane = lane if lane != "UNCLASSIFIED" else "GRAPH_STACK"
    if "pii" in s or "deident" in s or "de-ident" in s or "ner" in s:
        tags.append("pii")
        lane = lane if lane != "UNCLASSIFIED" else "PII_TOOLING"
    return {"lane": lane, "tags": tags}

# Inventory top-level uploads
uploads = [p for p in sorted(ROOT_IN.iterdir()) if p.is_file()]

bundle_registry = []
for p in uploads:
    try:
        h = sha256_file(p)
    except Exception:
        h = None
    bundle_registry.append({
        "observed_path": str(p),
        "basename": p.name,
        "bytes": p.stat().st_size,
        "sha256": h,
        "ext": p.suffix.lower(),
        "observed_at_utc": run_ts,
    })

(out_root / "OUT" / "REPORTS" / "INPUT_BUNDLE_REGISTRY.json").write_text(
    json.dumps({"run_utc": run_ts, "bundles": bundle_registry}, indent=2),
    encoding="utf-8"
)

# Inventory contents (hash inside zips with a bounded budget)
records = []
zip_summaries = []
max_bytes_to_hash_total = 800 * 1024 * 1024  # 800MB
bytes_hashed = 0

for p in uploads:
    ext = p.suffix.lower()
    if ext in ZIP_EXT:
        zsum = {
            "zip_path": str(p),
            "zip_sha256": next((b["sha256"] for b in bundle_registry if b["observed_path"] == str(p)), None),
            "zip_bytes": p.stat().st_size,
            "entries": 0,
            "files": 0,
            "dirs": 0,
            "hashed_entries": 0,
            "bytes_hashed": 0,
            "hash_budget_bytes": max_bytes_to_hash_total,
            "skipped_hash_entries": 0,
            "skipped_hash_reasons": {},
        }
        try:
            with zipfile.ZipFile(p, "r") as zf:
                for zi in zf.infolist():
                    zsum["entries"] += 1
                    if zi.is_dir():
                        zsum["dirs"] += 1
                        continue
                    zsum["files"] += 1
                    inner_name = zi.filename
                    inner_ext = Path(inner_name).suffix.lower()
                    lane_info = lane_classify(inner_name)
                    rec = {
                        "record_type": "zip_entry",
                        "zip_origin": str(p),
                        "zip_sha256": zsum["zip_sha256"],
                        "entry_name": inner_name,
                        "entry_bytes": zi.file_size,
                        "entry_compressed_bytes": zi.compress_size,
                        "entry_crc32": crc32_hex(zi.CRC),
                        "entry_sha256": None,
                        "hash_status": "NOT_COMPUTED",
                        "hash_skipped_reason": None,
                        "mime_guess": guess_mime(inner_name),
                        "ext": inner_ext,
                        "lane": lane_info["lane"],
                        "tags": lane_info["tags"],
                        "observed_at_utc": run_ts,
                    }
                    should_hash = True
                    if inner_ext in SKIP_HASH_EXT:
                        should_hash = False
                        rec["hash_status"] = "SKIPPED"
                        rec["hash_skipped_reason"] = f"EXT_IN_SKIP_LIST:{inner_ext}"
                    elif zi.file_size > 80 * 1024 * 1024:
                        should_hash = False
                        rec["hash_status"] = "SKIPPED"
                        rec["hash_skipped_reason"] = "SIZE_GT_80MB"
                    elif bytes_hashed + zi.file_size > max_bytes_to_hash_total:
                        should_hash = False
                        rec["hash_status"] = "SKIPPED"
                        rec["hash_skipped_reason"] = "HASH_BUDGET_EXCEEDED"
                    if should_hash:
                        try:
                            with zf.open(zi, "r") as f:
                                rec["entry_sha256"] = sha256_stream(f)
                            rec["hash_status"] = "COMPUTED"
                            zsum["hashed_entries"] += 1
                            bytes_hashed += zi.file_size
                            zsum["bytes_hashed"] = bytes_hashed
                        except Exception as e:
                            rec["hash_status"] = "ERROR"
                            rec["hash_skipped_reason"] = f"EXC:{type(e).__name__}"
                    else:
                        zsum["skipped_hash_entries"] += 1
                        r = rec["hash_skipped_reason"] or "UNKNOWN"
                        zsum["skipped_hash_reasons"][r] = zsum["skipped_hash_reasons"].get(r, 0) + 1
                    records.append(rec)
        except Exception as e:
            zsum["error"] = f"{type(e).__name__}: {e}"
        zip_summaries.append(zsum)
    else:
        lane_info = lane_classify(p.name)
        records.append({
            "record_type": "file",
            "observed_path": str(p),
            "basename": p.name,
            "bytes": p.stat().st_size,
            "sha256": next((b["sha256"] for b in bundle_registry if b["observed_path"] == str(p)), None),
            "ext": ext,
            "mime_guess": guess_mime(p.name),
            "lane": lane_info["lane"],
            "tags": lane_info["tags"],
            "observed_at_utc": run_ts,
        })

(out_root / "OUT" / "REPORTS" / "FILE_MANIFEST.jsonl").write_text(
    "\n".join(json.dumps(r, ensure_ascii=False) for r in records) + "\n",
    encoding="utf-8"
)
(out_root / "OUT" / "REPORTS" / "ZIP_SUMMARIES.json").write_text(
    json.dumps({"run_utc": run_ts, "zip_summaries": zip_summaries}, indent=2),
    encoding="utf-8"
)

# Indices
by_lane, by_ext = {}, {}
for r in records:
    by_lane[r.get("lane","UNCLASSIFIED")] = by_lane.get(r.get("lane","UNCLASSIFIED"), 0) + 1
    ex = (r.get("ext") or "(none)").lower()
    by_ext[ex] = by_ext.get(ex, 0) + 1

(out_root / "OUT" / "REPORTS" / "INDEX_BY_LANE.json").write_text(
    json.dumps({"run_utc": run_ts, "counts": by_lane}, indent=2),
    encoding="utf-8"
)
(out_root / "OUT" / "REPORTS" / "INDEX_BY_EXTENSION.json").write_text(
    json.dumps({"run_utc": run_ts, "counts": by_ext}, indent=2),
    encoding="utf-8"
)

# Missing datasets (machine-checkable)
missing = [
    {
        "missing_id": "SCAO_FORMS_FULL_PDF_CORPUS",
        "need": "Complete local corpus of SCAO-approved form PDFs + paired instruction PDFs, or a captured index export enabling deterministic download.",
        "acquire_plan": [
            "Capture/compile a forms index export into scao_forms_index.csv (schema provided) or .json.",
            "Run tools/scao_forms_downloader.py on Windows to download and envelope-stamp artifacts."
        ]
    },
    {
        "missing_id": "MUSKEGON_LOCAL_PACKETS_PDF",
        "need": "Local Muskegon County court packet PDFs (Probate packets, Circuit filing packets, local forms).",
        "acquire_plan": [
            "Download local packet PDFs from Muskegon County official court pages (Probate/Circuit) and zip/upload them for harvest."
        ]
    },
    {
        "missing_id": "WDMI_PRO_SE_FORMS_CORPUS",
        "need": "WDMI pro se form PDFs and instructions downloaded locally as a corpus.",
        "acquire_plan": [
            "Download WDMI pro se forms PDF set from the official WDMI pro se forms page; zip/upload for harvest."
        ]
    },
    {
        "missing_id": "SCOTUS_FILING_EXPORTS",
        "need": "SCOTUS rules/forms/guidance captured as local files (portal exports).",
        "acquire_plan": [
            "Download SCOTUS rules and filing guidance PDFs; zip/upload for harvest."
        ]
    }
]
(out_root / "OUT" / "REPORTS" / "PINPOINT_MISSING.json").write_text(
    json.dumps({"run_utc": run_ts, "missing": missing}, indent=2),
    encoding="utf-8"
)

# Index schema template (schema-only; no sample rows)
with (out_root / "input_templates" / "scao_forms_index.csv").open("w", newline="", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    w = csv.writer(f)
    w.writerow(["form_number","title","category","pdf_url","instructions_url","revision_date","notes"])

# Write downloader tool
downloader_py = (out_root / "tools" / "scao_forms_downloader.py")
downloader_py.write_text(open("/mnt/data/.dummy", "w").write("") or "", encoding="utf-8")
# Replace with actual content (avoids editor bugs on some kernels)
downloader_py.write_text(r'''#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
LitigationOS — SCAO Forms Corpus Downloader (operator-controlled)

Reads a captured index export (CSV/JSON) and downloads PDFs into canonical layout:
  OUT\\DERIVED\\SCAO_FORMS\\PDF\\...
  OUT\\DERIVED\\SCAO_FORMS\\INSTRUCTIONS\\...
Emits:
  OUT\\REPORTS\\SCAO_FORMS\\FILE_MANIFEST.jsonl
  OUT\\REPORTS\\SCAO_FORMS\\RUN_REPORT.json
  OUT\\ENVELOPES\\SCAO_FORMS\\*.json (Transform Envelopes)

No URLs are embedded in this script; it consumes your index export.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import csv
import hashlib
import json
import time
from dataclasses import dataclass
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Dict, Iterable, List, Optional

try:
    import requests
except Exception:
    requests = None

def utc_ts() -> str:
    return datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ")

def sha256_file(p: Path, chunk: int = 1024 * 1024) -> str:
    h = hashlib.sha256()
    with p.open("rb") as f:
        while True:
            b = f.read(chunk)
            if not b:
                break
            h.update(b)
    return h.hexdigest()

def ensure_dir(p: Path) -> None:
    p.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

def safe_filename(name: str) -> str:
    bad = '<>:"/\\|?*'
    out = "".join("_" if c in bad else c for c in name)
    out = out.strip().rstrip(".")
    return out or "file"

def read_paths_config(paths_json: Path) -> Dict[str, str]:
    data = json.loads(paths_json.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    if not isinstance(data, dict) or "ROOT" not in data:
        raise ValueError("paths.json must be an object with at least a ROOT field.")
    return {k: str(v) for k, v in data.items()}

@dataclass
class FormRow:
    form_number: str
    title: str
    category: str
    pdf_url: str
    instructions_url: str
    revision_date: str
    notes: str

def load_index_csv(path: Path) -> List[FormRow]:
    rows: List[FormRow] = []
    with path.open("r", encoding="utf-8", newline="") as f:
        reader = csv.DictReader(f)
        required = ["form_number","title","category","pdf_url","instructions_url","revision_date","notes"]
        for r in required:
            if reader.fieldnames is None or r not in reader.fieldnames:
                raise ValueError(f"CSV missing required column: {r}")
        for d in reader:
            rows.append(FormRow(
                form_number=(d.get("form_number") or "").strip(),
                title=(d.get("title") or "").strip(),
                category=(d.get("category") or "").strip(),
                pdf_url=(d.get("pdf_url") or "").strip(),
                instructions_url=(d.get("instructions_url") or "").strip(),
                revision_date=(d.get("revision_date") or "").strip(),
                notes=(d.get("notes") or "").strip(),
            ))
    return rows

def load_index_json(path: Path) -> List[FormRow]:
    obj = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    if not isinstance(obj, list):
        raise ValueError("JSON index must be a list of objects.")
    rows: List[FormRow] = []
    for d in obj:
        if not isinstance(d, dict):
            continue
        rows.append(FormRow(
            form_number=str(d.get("form_number","")).strip(),
            title=str(d.get("title","")).strip(),
            category=str(d.get("category","")).strip(),
            pdf_url=str(d.get("pdf_url","")).strip(),
            instructions_url=str(d.get("instructions_url","")).strip(),
            revision_date=str(d.get("revision_date","")).strip(),
            notes=str(d.get("notes","")).strip(),
        ))
    return rows

def http_get_pdf(url: str, timeout: int, retries: int, backoff: float) -> bytes:
    if requests is None:
        raise RuntimeError("requests is not installed. Install with: pip install requests")
    last_exc: Optional[Exception] = None
    for i in range(retries):
        try:
            r = requests.get(url, timeout=timeout, stream=True, headers={"User-Agent":"LitigationOS-FormsHarvester/1.0"})
            r.raise_for_status()
            data = b"".join(r.iter_content(chunk_size=1024*256))
            if data[:4] != b"%PDF":
                ct = (r.headers.get("content-type") or "").lower()
                raise ValueError(f"Downloaded content does not appear to be a PDF (content-type={ct}).")
            return data
        except Exception as e:
            last_exc = e
            time.sleep(backoff * (i + 1))
    raise RuntimeError(f"Failed download after {retries} attempts: {url} :: {type(last_exc).__name__}: {last_exc}")

def write_envelope(envelope_path: Path, *, origin_url: str, ingest_method: str, out_path: Path, out_sha256: str, paths_config_source: str, paths_config_sha256: str, paths_expanded: Dict[str,str], tool: str, version: str, params: Dict[str, object]) -> None:
    env = {
        "schema": "LITIGATIONOS_TRANSFORM_ENVELOPE_v1",
        "created_at_utc": utc_ts(),
        "cas_origin_pointer": {
            "sha256": out_sha256,
            "observed_path": str(out_path),
            "observed_at_utc": utc_ts(),
            "ingest_method": ingest_method,
            "origin_url": origin_url,
        },
        "transform": {"tool": tool, "version": version, "params": params},
        "digests": {
            "input": [{"type": "url", "value": origin_url}],
            "output": [{"sha256": out_sha256, "path": str(out_path)}],
        },
        "config_snapshot": {
            "paths_config_source": paths_config_source,
            "paths_config_sha256": paths_config_sha256,
            "paths_expanded": paths_expanded,
        },
    }
    envelope_path.write_text(json.dumps(env, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")

def main() -> int:
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    ap.add_argument("--paths-config", required=True, help="Path to $ROOT\\config\\paths.json")
    ap.add_argument("--index", required=True, help="Path to scao_forms_index.csv or scao_forms_index.json")
    ap.add_argument("--strict", action="store_true", help="Fail-closed: missing urls/fields abort.")
    ap.add_argument("--timeout", type=int, default=60)
    ap.add_argument("--retries", type=int, default=3)
    ap.add_argument("--backoff", type=float, default=1.5)
    args = ap.parse_args()

    paths_cfg_path = Path(args.paths_config).expanduser().resolve()
    paths = read_paths_config(paths_cfg_path)
    root = Path(paths["ROOT"]).resolve()

    out_base = root / "OUT"
    derived = out_base / "DERIVED" / "SCAO_FORMS"
    pdf_dir = derived / "PDF"
    inst_dir = derived / "INSTRUCTIONS"
    reports = out_base / "REPORTS" / "SCAO_FORMS"
    envelopes = out_base / "ENVELOPES" / "SCAO_FORMS"

    for d in [pdf_dir, inst_dir, reports, envelopes]:
        ensure_dir(d)

    paths_cfg_sha = sha256_file(paths_cfg_path)
    paths_cfg_source = str(paths_cfg_path)

    idx_path = Path(args.index).expanduser().resolve()
    if idx_path.suffix.lower() == ".csv":
        rows = load_index_csv(idx_path)
    elif idx_path.suffix.lower() == ".json":
        rows = load_index_json(idx_path)
    else:
        raise ValueError("Index must be .csv or .json")

    manifest_path = reports / "FILE_MANIFEST.jsonl"
    run_report_path = reports / "RUN_REPORT.json"
    failures: List[Dict[str,str]] = []
    downloaded = 0
    skipped = 0

    with manifest_path.open("w", encoding="utf-8") as mf:
        for row in rows:
            if not row.pdf_url:
                if args.strict:
                    raise ValueError(f"Missing pdf_url for form {row.form_number} {row.title}")
                skipped += 1
                continue

            fn_base = safe_filename((row.form_number or "FORM") + "__" + (row.title or "Untitled"))
            pdf_path = pdf_dir / f"{fn_base}.pdf"

            try:
                pdf_bytes = http_get_pdf(row.pdf_url, timeout=args.timeout, retries=args.retries, backoff=args.backoff)
                pdf_path.write_bytes(pdf_bytes)
                pdf_sha = sha256_file(pdf_path)

                env_path = envelopes / f"{fn_base}__PDF__ENVELOPE.json"
                write_envelope(
                    env_path,
                    origin_url=row.pdf_url,
                    ingest_method="download:scao_forms_index",
                    out_path=pdf_path,
                    out_sha256=pdf_sha,
                    paths_config_source=paths_cfg_source,
                    paths_config_sha256=paths_cfg_sha,
                    paths_expanded=paths,
                    tool="scao_forms_downloader",
                    version="1.0.0",
                    params={"timeout": args.timeout, "retries": args.retries, "backoff": args.backoff, "index_path": str(idx_path)},
                )

                rec = {
                    "record_type": "downloaded_form_pdf",
                    "form_number": row.form_number,
                    "title": row.title,
                    "category": row.category,
                    "revision_date": row.revision_date,
                    "source_url": row.pdf_url,
                    "output_path": str(pdf_path),
                    "sha256": pdf_sha,
                    "bytes": pdf_path.stat().st_size,
                    "created_at_utc": utc_ts(),
                }
                mf.write(json.dumps(rec, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
                downloaded += 1
            except Exception as e:
                failures.append({"form_number": row.form_number, "title": row.title, "url": row.pdf_url, "error": f"{type(e).__name__}: {e}"})
                if args.strict:
                    raise

            if row.instructions_url:
                inst_path = inst_dir / f"{fn_base}__INSTRUCTIONS.pdf"
                try:
                    inst_bytes = http_get_pdf(row.instructions_url, timeout=args.timeout, retries=args.retries, backoff=args.backoff)
                    inst_path.write_bytes(inst_bytes)
                    inst_sha = sha256_file(inst_path)

                    env_path = envelopes / f"{fn_base}__INSTRUCTIONS__ENVELOPE.json"
                    write_envelope(
                        env_path,
                        origin_url=row.instructions_url,
                        ingest_method="download:scao_forms_index",
                        out_path=inst_path,
                        out_sha256=inst_sha,
                        paths_config_source=paths_cfg_source,
                        paths_config_sha256=paths_cfg_sha,
                        paths_expanded=paths,
                        tool="scao_forms_downloader",
                        version="1.0.0",
                        params={"timeout": args.timeout, "retries": args.retries, "backoff": args.backoff, "index_path": str(idx_path)},
                    )

                    rec = {
                        "record_type": "downloaded_form_instructions_pdf",
                        "form_number": row.form_number,
                        "title": row.title,
                        "category": row.category,
                        "revision_date": row.revision_date,
                        "source_url": row.instructions_url,
                        "output_path": str(inst_path),
                        "sha256": inst_sha,
                        "bytes": inst_path.stat().st_size,
                        "created_at_utc": utc_ts(),
                    }
                    mf.write(json.dumps(rec, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
                except Exception as e:
                    failures.append({"form_number": row.form_number, "title": row.title, "url": row.instructions_url, "error": f"{type(e).__name__}: {e}"})
                    if args.strict:
                        raise

    run_report = {
        "run_utc": utc_ts(),
        "paths_config_source": str(paths_cfg_path),
        "paths_config_sha256": sha256_file(paths_cfg_path),
        "index_path": str(idx_path),
        "downloaded_pdf_count": downloaded,
        "skipped_rows": skipped,
        "failures": failures,
    }
    run_report_path.write_text(json.dumps(run_report, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
    print(json.dumps(run_report, indent=2))
    return 0 if (not args.strict or len(failures) == 0) else 2

if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())
''', encoding="utf-8")

# Docs file (no citations inside pack; references are plain text)
(out_root / "docs" / "ACQUIRE_FORMS_AND_PACKETS.md").write_text(
    "\n".join([
        "# Acquisition Plan — Forms & Packets Corpus (Michigan + Federal lanes)",
        "",
        "This pack harvested only what you uploaded into this conversation and produced machine-checkable inventories.",
        "",
        "## SCAO forms (statewide)",
        "1) Capture/compile a forms index export into input\\scao_forms_index.csv using the schema in input_templates\\scao_forms_index.csv.",
        "2) Create ROOT config at config\\paths.json with at least ROOT pointing to your corpus root folder.",
        "3) Run: python tools\\scao_forms_downloader.py --paths-config config\\paths.json --index input\\scao_forms_index.csv --strict",
        "",
        "## Muskegon local packets",
        "1) Download local packet PDFs from official Muskegon County court pages (Probate/Circuit).",
        "2) Zip and upload to harvest.",
        "",
        "## WDMI pro se forms",
        "1) Download WDMI pro se forms PDFs from WDMI's official pro se forms page.",
        "2) Zip and upload to harvest.",
        "",
        "## SCOTUS lane",
        "1) Download SCOTUS rules and filing guidance PDFs from SCOTUS official sources.",
        "2) Zip and upload to harvest.",
        "",
    ]),
    encoding="utf-8"
)

# Write a minimal README for what was produced
(out_root / "README.md").write_text(
    "\n".join([
        f"# LitigationOS — Forms & Lanes Harvest Pack",
        "",
        f"Run UTC: {run_ts}",
        "",
        "## Outputs",
        "- OUT/REPORTS/INPUT_BUNDLE_REGISTRY.json — hashes + sizes for uploaded files",
        "- OUT/REPORTS/FILE_MANIFEST.jsonl — per-file and per-zip-entry inventory (lane-classified)",
        "- OUT/REPORTS/INDEX_BY_LANE.json — counts by lane",
        "- OUT/REPORTS/INDEX_BY_EXTENSION.json — counts by extension",
        "- OUT/REPORTS/ZIP_SUMMARIES.json — per-zip summary and inner-hash budget status",
        "- OUT/REPORTS/PINPOINT_MISSING.json — missing external corpora + acquisition plan",
        "",
        "## Tools",
        "- tools/scao_forms_downloader.py — deterministic downloader/enveloper (requires your captured index export)",
        "",
        "## Templates",
        "- input_templates/scao_forms_index.csv — schema-only",
        "",
    ]),
    encoding="utf-8"
)

# Build release zip
release_zip = ROOT_IN / f"LITIGATIONOS_FORMS_LANES_HARVEST_PACK__{run_ts}.zip"
if release_zip.exists():
    release_zip.unlink()

def zip_dir(src: Path, dst: Path):
    with zipfile.ZipFile(dst, "w", compression=zipfile.ZIP_DEFLATED) as z:
        for p in src.rglob("*"):
            if p.is_file():
                z.write(p, p.relative_to(src).as_posix())

zip_dir(out_root, release_zip)

# Return a small summary for the UI
summary = {
    "run_utc": run_ts,
    "release_zip": str(release_zip),
    "out_root": str(out_root),
    "uploads_count": len(uploads),
    "records_count": len(records),
    "zip_count": len([p for p in uploads if p.suffix.lower()==".zip"]),
    "bytes_hashed_inside_zips": bytes_hashed,
    "lane_counts": by_lane,
}
summary

What’s inside the ZIP I generated
1) Canonical inventories (machine-checkable)

    OUT/REPORTS/FILE_MANIFEST.jsonl
    One JSON object per:

        ZIP entry (including: zip_origin, entry_name, sizes, CRC32, optional SHA256, lane classification).

    OUT/REPORTS/ZIP_SUMMARIES.json
    Per-zip summary: entries/files/dirs, how many inner hashes computed, and skip reasons.

    OUT/REPORTS/INDEX_BY_LANE.json and OUT/REPORTS/INDEX_BY_EXTENSION.json
    Aggregate counts by lane and file extension.

    OUT/REPORTS/PINPOINT_MISSING.json
    Explicit missing corpora + acquisition plan stubs.

    input_templates/scao_forms_index.csv (schema-only)
    Columns:
    form_number,title,category,pdf_url,instructions_url,revision_date,notes

        emits FILE_MANIFEST.jsonl, RUN_REPORT.json, and transform envelopes

    Jurisdiction mapping (which court accepts it) → jurisdiction nodes

    Procedure graph wiring (how it flows) → procedure/action nodes

    Form binding (which PDF is the authorized vehicle) → FORM_PDF nodes + edges

Missing corpora discovery and capture plan (fail-closed, corpus-grade)

You have four high-value missing corpora lanes that complete the “procedures ↔ jurisdiction ↔ correct form” mapping across Michigan trial courts, Michigan appellate, federal district, federal appellate, and SCOTUS:

    Michigan SCAO statewide forms (PDFs + instructions + indexes)

    Muskegon local packets and local court PDFs (probate packets, circuit and district packets, local publications)

    Federal lane: WDMI pro se forms and local references

    SCOTUS lane: rules, guidance, electronic filing portal references and exportable artifacts

Below is the best-path acquisition plan that (a) uses only official sources, (b) produces a machine-checkable captured index export, and (c) is structured so it can be wired into your grand-master graph without guessing.
1) Michigan statewide lane: SCAO forms corpus (PDFs + instructions + indexes)
Authoritative sources you can rely on

    SCAO “forms search” portal (discoverability root): the Michigan Courts SCAO forms portal is the canonical discovery surface for approved forms.

    Direct “SCAO-approved” form PDFs (stable download pattern exists): examples that verify the official Michigan Courts host and PDF naming conventions:

        MC 01 example: mc01.pdf on Michigan Courts host.

        FOC 61 example: foc61.pdf on Michigan Courts host.

    Legacy/categorical SCAO forms URLs exist and are discoverable (useful as an alternate corpus lane when the new portal is hard to scrape): examples show the legacy directory structure on Michigan Courts:

        Appeals form example: cc401.pdf under an “appeals” path on Michigan Courts.

What you should capture (minimum viable “complete”)

Your SCAO forms corpus should include three distinct but linked sub-corpora:

A. Approved form PDFs

    All form PDFs (MC, DC, CC, PC, FOC, JC, etc) and any “A/B/C” suffix versions.

B. Form instructions PDFs

    Where the portal provides a distinct “instructions” or “how to fill” document, treat it as a first-class artifact linked to the form_id.

C. Index exports and “change tracking”

    A captured index export of the official portal results (machine-checkable), plus any revision memos or “recent revisions” references if present.

Captured index export (machine-checkable, not hand-built)

Your objective is to produce one canonical index file you can regenerate:

OUT\REPORTS\SCAO_FORMS_INDEX_EXPORT.jsonl with fields like:

    form_code (MC 01, FOC 61, CC 401)

    title

    category (civil, domestic, probate, criminal, appeals)

    revision_date (if available)

    pdf_url

    instructions_url (optional)

    source_page_url (the discovery page that yielded it)

    captured_at

Best-path capture method (low ambiguity, highest completeness):

    Use the official SCAO portal as the authoritative list source.

    Capture the results as a structured export (JSONL/CSV). If the portal does not provide a native export button, capture the underlying results payload via browser devtools “Network” inspection and save it as a raw JSON artifact, then convert to JSONL/CSV.

Fail-closed rule: if you cannot obtain a structured listing payload from the portal, you do not pretend you have the full set. You create:

    PINPOINT_MISSING: SCAO_INDEX_EXPORT_PAYLOAD_NOT_CAPTURED

    ACQUIRE_PLAN: “capture portal payload → convert → download”

2) Muskegon local lane: county packets and local court PDFs
Authoritative sources discovered

    Muskegon County Probate Court “Information Packets” (packet-level corpus, high leverage for procedures):

    Muskegon County “Forms & Publications” (local court publications and procedural PDFs):

        Example downloads that validate direct PDF availability:

            Change of Address PDF.

            Personal identifying information form PDF.

What you should capture

A. Probate packets

    Each packet page plus any downloadable PDFs linked from the packet page.

B. Circuit/District local publications

    Every PDF under Muskegon “Forms & Publications,” plus the parent index page HTML captured as the discovery root.

C. Local rules, filing instructions, cover sheets, fee schedules

    If a local page provides procedures without a PDF, capture the HTML as a “portal artifact” and stamp it with capture metadata.

Graph wiring outcome (what this lane gives you)

This lane feeds a new “Local Practice” pillar that bridges:

    Court{Muskegon Probate} → Procedure{Guardianship, Conservatorship, Estate, PPO-adjacent probate filings when applicable} → Packet → FormsReferenced

You do not have to manually author “procedures.” The packets are already procedure bundles. Your job is to ingest and index them.
3) Federal lane: WDMI pro se forms, WDMI civil forms, and national AO forms
Authoritative sources discovered

    WDMI Pro Se Forms page (core pro se downloads):

        Example artifact: WDMI “Pro Se Handbook” PDF.

    WDMI Civil Forms page (district-specific forms beyond pro se landing):

    U.S. Courts national forms page (AO forms and national federal forms):

What you should capture

A. WDMI pro se kit

B. WDMI civil forms

Graph wiring outcome

This lane populates:

    Court{WDMI} → Procedure{Civil Filing, IFP, Service, Summons, Subpoena} → FederalForm{AO/Local}
    This is the minimum structure needed to later map “procedure candidates” to “correct district form” deterministically.

4) SCOTUS lane: rules, guidance, and electronic filing portal references
Authoritative sources discovered

    SCOTUS Filing and Rules page (rules and guidance root):

B. Portal references (exportable artifacts)

Canonical acquisition mechanics (how to run it on your machine)
A) Path policy invariant (your stated requirement)

You want everything rooted at:

$ROOT = C:\Users\andre\Downloads\LITIGATIONOS__MASTERv1.0__SPEC_ADDENDUM_v0010

So the only configuration you should need is:

$ROOT\config\paths.json

    ROOT points to that folder

    OUT and CAS resolve under it

    Every tool reads paths from it, and every report stamps:

        paths_config_source

        paths_config_sha256

        expanded paths dictionary or reference id

B) Acquisition run sequence (no guessing, fail-closed)

    Capture discovery roots (HTML snapshots)

        SCAO forms portal landing page

        Muskegon packets index

        Muskegon forms publications index

        WDMI pro se forms landing

        U.S. Courts forms landing

        SCOTUS filing and efiling landings

    Build machine-checkable index exports per lane

        SCAO_FORMS_INDEX_EXPORT.jsonl (from portal payload capture)

        MUSKEGON_PACKETS_INDEX.jsonl (parse packet index and subpages)

        MUSKEGON_PUBLICATIONS_INDEX.jsonl (parse forms publications index)

        WDMI_FORMS_INDEX.jsonl (parse WDMI pro se and WDMI civil pages)

        FEDERAL_AO_FORMS_INDEX.jsonl (parse U.S. Courts forms listings)

        SCOTUS_RULES_INDEX.jsonl (parse SCOTUS rules/guidance listings)

    Download PDFs and stamp transform envelopes (mandatory)
    For every downloaded PDF:

        CAS origin pointer: sha256 + observed_path + observed_at + ingest_method

        transform metadata: tool, version, params

        input/output digests

        fail closed if any link is missing

    Produce corpora bundles

        OUT\RELEASES\CORPUS_SCAO_FORMS.zip

        OUT\RELEASES\CORPUS_MUSKEGON_PACKETS.zip

        OUT\RELEASES\CORPUS_WDMI_FORMS.zip

        OUT\RELEASES\CORPUS_SCOTUS_RULES.zip
        Each with:

        a top-level manifest

        index export

        envelope directory

        a short “readme provenance” file

Add-on corpora that materially upgrade “Michigan practice IQ” pillars

These are high-value, official, free corpora that directly improve your MI legal-language lane (courtroom-ready phrasing, standards, and procedure framing):

    Michigan Judicial Institute Benchbooks (judicial procedure and standards; direct source for “how courts think”):

    Michigan Model Civil Jury Instructions (elements, definitions, and pattern framing):

    Michigan Model Criminal Jury Instructions:

These are not forms, but they are the fastest way to build “practice IQ” nodes that attach to your Procedures pillar as:

    Procedure → Standard → BenchbookSection and JuryInstruction

What remains “missing” after today’s web discovery
Fully confirmed (authoritative, harvestable now)

    U.S. Courts national forms landing (AO forms).

    SCOTUS filing and efiling landing pages.

    SCAO form PDFs exist with predictable codes on Michigan Courts host (examples verified).

    SCAO appellate form PDF exists under legacy categorical URLs (example verified).

Still requires a “captured index export” step to guarantee completeness

    The complete statewide SCAO forms list in a single authoritative export depends on capturing the SCAO portal’s result set payload.

        Until that payload is captured, any list derived from ad hoc URL hunting is not provably complete.

If you want the next escalation step after this

The single highest leverage addition is a new corpus pillar and index:

CORPUS_SOURCES_REGISTRY (machine-checkable) that enumerates every corpus lane, discovery root, extraction method, and update cadence, and then emits:

    CORPUS_DISCOVERY_REPORT

    CORPUS_COMPLETENESS_REPORT (with explicit, typed blockers)

That is the clean bridge from “downloaded corpora” into “grand master SUPERABUNDENT graph” without inventing mappings.
