LITIGATIONOS__MASTERv1.0 — Chained Spec Escalation Cycles 01–20 (Canonical “God-Grade”)

You already have the Build Charter plus service-boundary contract direction. The next escalation is to force determinism, auditability, and autonomy through machine-checkable contracts that make the system impossible to “drift” as it grows.

This expansion hardens the spec into a contract-first, report-first, gate-first operating system where every run produces verifiable artifacts and every service boundary is mechanically testable.
Global Canonical Invariants (now mandatory)
1) Contract dialect lock: OpenAPI 3.1 + JSON Schema 2020-12

    All service contracts are OpenAPI 3.1 and explicitly declare a JSON Schema dialect aligned to the OpenAPI 3.1 Schema Object Dialect, which itself is anchored to JSON Schema Draft 2020-12.

    All machine-checkable reports/manifests use JSON Schema Draft 2020-12 with $schema = https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema.

2) Streaming is first-class: NDJSON/JSONL + optional SSE

    Run progress, logs, and adapter telemetry are streams, not polls:

        NDJSON/JSON Lines for process-to-process streaming and audit logs.

        Optional Server-Sent Events for browser UI push using EventSource.

3) Deterministic local service posture

    Tika server is run locally and visited on a localhost URL (commonly http://localhost:9998/) with explicit --host/--port override capability.

    rclone remote-control API is standardized around localhost default URL http://localhost:5572/ and daemon control via rclone rcd with --rc-addr.

    Neo4j connectivity uses Bolt (default TCP 7687), with explicit port control supported.

4) Idempotent migrations and fail-closed gates

    Neo4j constraints and migrations MUST be idempotent using IF NOT EXISTS to avoid non-repeatable installs and “works once” states.

    PCW blocks drafting-to-filing transitions; PCG blocks irreversible outputs unless all core obligations are SATISFIED (fail-closed).

5) Provenance and observability are architecture, not add-ons

    Provenance model aligns to PROV concepts and relations (Entity, Activity, Agent) as the canonical foundation for derivations and audit chains.

    Observability uses OpenTelemetry semantics for spans and attributes to standardize cross-service traces (run-scoped).

6) Supply chain hygiene is mandatory

    SBOM support is standardized to recognized formats such as CycloneDX and SPDX.

    Release integrity escalates against SLSA controls as your north-star checklist.

Cycle Chain 01–20 (Extremely Dense, Spec-Executable)

Each cycle is an append-only addendum: it adds constraints, schemas, and tests without rewriting prior decisions. The rule is: a new capability is not “real” until it has (1) a schema, (2) a contract, (3) a test, (4) a log stream, and (5) a manifest record.
Cycle 01 — Boundary Standardization (LSBS)

Goal: every service boundary behaves the same at the edges.

    Mandatory endpoints: /v1/health, /v1/version, /v1/capabilities.

    Mandatory response envelope: { ts, code, message, detail } for errors.

    Mandatory event envelope: NDJSON line objects for all streams.

    UI consumes boundaries uniformly; no per-adapter special cases.

Acceptance: contract tests verify identical response shapes across services.
Cycle 02 — Run Identity Canon (RunRef)

Goal: every artifact is traceable to a run and its exact policy state.

    run_id stable ID, started/ended timestamps, status, host, app version.

    A run is an Activity; the orchestrator is an Agent; files and reports are Entities (PROV mapping).

    Every report embeds RunRef and links to inputs/outputs by hash.

Acceptance: “replay” is possible: given inputs + policy + version, you can reproduce the same outputs.
Cycle 03 — Event-First UX (No Polling)

Goal: UI never polls; it subscribes.

    Orchestrator exposes /v1/runs/{run_id}/events as NDJSON and optional SSE.

    Every adapter emits a stream that is multiplexed under a single run stream.

    UI shows progress from streams, not inferred timers.

Acceptance: disconnect and reconnect resumes from last event offset (stream cursor strategy).
Cycle 04 — Intake Autonomy Governor

Goal: hands-off ingestion that remains deterministic.

    Watcher lane: detects new/changed files, queues by SHA-256, emits INTAKE_DISCOVERED, DEDUP_HIT.

    Scheduler lane: time-based re-harvest for sources marked “volatile” (Drive remotes, active case folders).

    Queue is idempotent and crash-safe.

Acceptance: repeated scans do not multiply artifacts; they increase evidence density without duplicating primitives.
Cycle 05 — OCR Trigger Standard (Adaptive OCR)

Goal: OCR only when needed, but never misses low-text PDFs.

    OCRmyPDF policy:

        rotation correction via --rotate-pages when cardinal angle is wrong,

        skew correction via --deskew when slightly off horizontal.

    OCR is triggered when extraction confidence or text coverage fails thresholds.

    Output artifacts: OCR’d PDF + OCR_REPORT with per-page actions.

Acceptance: OCR lane produces page-level audit showing exactly why OCR ran.
Cycle 06 — Tika-First Extraction Canon

Goal: deterministic extraction for PDFs/DOCX/HTML/text.

    Tika server mode and endpoints are treated as a local dependency; default localhost access pattern documented.

    Extraction emits:

        normalized plaintext,

        metadata JSON,

        content-type certainty,

        errors pinned to file hashes.

Acceptance: same file always produces the same normalized outputs under the same tool versions.
Cycle 07 — NLP Lane Canon (Entity, Event, Relation)

Goal: NLP outputs become graphable artifacts, not informal summaries.

    NLP report yields:

        entities with typed spans,

        events with mention sets,

        relations with confidence values.

    Default NLP engine is offline (for example spaCy) and model availability is explicit and local.

    “Hallucination containment”: low-confidence relations become candidates, not committed edges.

Acceptance: NLP results validate against schema and can be diffed across runs.
Cycle 08 — Audio Lane Canon (Transcription as Evidence Units)

Goal: speech becomes time-pinned quote units.

    Faster-Whisper lane produces time-coded transcripts and quote candidates, optimized for offline speed and memory.

    Each quote becomes a QuotePin requiring a timecode pinpoint.

Acceptance: every quote is clickable to exact time span.
Cycle 09 — Authority Snapshot Index Canon

Goal: authority is never floating or ambiguous.

    Authority snapshots are indexed by:

        authority ID,

        effective date,

        source hash,

        chunk pointers used by propositions.

    All propositions cite authority chunks with law pinpoints and effective dates.

Acceptance: “controlling law at time of filing” is reconstructible.
Cycle 10 — VehicleMap Determinism (Forms-First Engine)

Goal: relief selection cannot drift.

    VEHICLEMAP_INDEX is a versioned library:

        relief -> vehicle -> form,

        governing authority pins,

        deadlines and service chain steps.

    Selected vehicle emits VEHICLE_SELECTED event with justification pins.

Acceptance: same relief input yields the same vehicle selection under same version.
Cycle 11 — PCW and PCG Enforcement at the API Edge

Goal: exports are impossible unless gates pass.

    PCW_REPORT is produced and validated per run.

    Export endpoints return conflict status when:

        any core PO is OPEN or PARTIAL,

        PCG is FAIL.

    Block reasons must be explicit and enumerable.

Acceptance: unsafe export is structurally impossible.
Cycle 12 — Neo4j Schema Canon (Constraints, Migrations, Seed)

Goal: graph integrity is guaranteed from day zero.

    Constraints use IF NOT EXISTS idempotency.

    Seed pack instantiates nuclei A–E as deterministic subgraphs.

    Import path uses a stable edge and node typing strategy.

Acceptance: fresh install and re-install produce the same schema state.
Cycle 13 — Bloom-Style Graph Explorer Parity

Goal: open-source Bloom-like experience.

    Perspectives (nuclei), saved searches, filtering, style rules, hover cards.

    Click-through to:

        exhibit file pointer,

        authority chunk pointer,

        run provenance.

Acceptance: graph UX is a first-class operational interface, not a demo screen.
Cycle 14 — Provenance Canon (PROV-Aligned)

Goal: derivation is an enforceable model.

    Every output has a derivation chain to inputs and activities (runs).

    Graph edges: DERIVES_FROM are required for all material outputs.

    Provenance constraints reflect PROV reasoning.

Acceptance: you can answer “where did this come from” for any field.
Cycle 15 — Observability Canon (OpenTelemetry)

Goal: run-scoped traces across all services.

    Span naming and attributes include: run_id, file hash, adapter, step.

    No external telemetry vendor required; local collection optional.

Acceptance: a single run timeline can be reconstructed from spans and NDJSON logs.
Cycle 16 — Offline-First Egress Control

Goal: nothing “phones home.”

    Default deny outbound networking.

    Explicit operator toggles for:

        rclone sync,

        model acquisition,

        update checks.

    All toggles are captured in RUN_MANIFEST.policies.

Acceptance: network use is provable and never implicit.
Cycle 17 — rclone Control Adapter Canon

Goal: Drive sync is controlled, auditable, and reversible.

    rclone rc/rcd standardized around localhost control API.

    Adapter emits:

        sync plan,

        file delta list,

        final reconciliation report.

Acceptance: every remote change becomes an auditable event stream.
Cycle 18 — Supply Chain: SBOM, Licensing, SLSA Ladder

Goal: releases are trustworthy and explainable.

    SBOM formats supported: CycloneDX, SPDX.

    Build policy aligns to SLSA framework for integrity controls.

    License ledger is a required build artifact.

Acceptance: every dependency is declared with license and source pointer.
Cycle 19 — Release Mechanics: FULL vs PATCH, Size Budget Gate

Goal: packaging is predictable under your size caps.

    Build emits:

        projected size,

        included files list,

        excluded weights/media/binaries list.

    Release builder refuses to ship when policies are violated.

Acceptance: size growth is always reported before it happens.
Cycle 20 — Autonomy Modes (Fail-Closed Automation)

Goal: autonomous execution with safe ceilings.

    Modes:

        DISCOVERY (index only),

        BUILD_GRAPH (writes graph and reports),

        BUILD_EXPORTS (requires PCG PASS).

    Each mode is a capability sandbox with explicit allowed operations.

Acceptance: autonomy is high, but cannot cross irreversible thresholds.
Deliverable Produced in This Turn

A dense “Spec Addendum Pack” containing:

    Draft 2020-12 JSON Schemas for the core manifests/reports.

    OpenAPI 3.1 contracts for Orchestrator, Graph API, Tika adapter, OCR adapter, Ollama adapter, rclone adapter.

    A single spec document enumerating Cycles 01–20 and the audit checklist.

from pathlib import Path
import json, zipfile, hashlib, datetime, re

out_root = Path("/mnt/data/spec_addendum_v0004")
if out_root.exists():
    for p in sorted(out_root.rglob("*"), reverse=True):
        if p.is_file():
            p.unlink()
        else:
            try:
                p.rmdir()
            except OSError:
                pass
out_root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

DRAFT = "https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema"
OPENAPI_DIALECT = "https://spec.openapis.org/oas/3.1/dialect/2024-11-10"

def write_text(rel, s):
    p = out_root / rel
    p.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    p.write_text(s, encoding="utf-8")

def write_json(rel, obj):
    p = out_root / rel
    p.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    p.write_text(json.dumps(obj, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")

def sha256_bytes(b: bytes) -> str:
    return hashlib.sha256(b).hexdigest()

def sha256_file(p: Path) -> str:
    return sha256_bytes(p.read_bytes())

# Shared defs (import from v0003-like, but include additions)
shared = {
    "$schema": DRAFT,
    "$id": "https://litigationos.local/schemas/shared.defs.schema.json",
    "title": "LitigationOS Shared Schema Definitions",
    "type": "object",
    "$defs": {
        "IsoDateTime": {"type": "string", "format": "date-time"},
        "IsoDate": {"type": "string", "format": "date"},
        "SemVer": {"type": "string", "pattern": r"^v?\d+\.\d+\.\d+(-[0-9A-Za-z.-]+)?(\+[0-9A-Za-z.-]+)?$"},
        "Sha256": {"type": "string", "pattern": r"^[0-9a-f]{64}$"},
        "Path": {"type": "string", "minLength": 1},
        "Uri": {"type": "string", "format": "uri"},
        "StableId": {"type": "string", "pattern": r"^[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9._:-]{2,127}$"},
        "NonEmptyString": {"type": "string", "minLength": 1},
        "Score01": {"type": "number", "minimum": 0.0, "maximum": 1.0},
        "EvidencePinpoint": {
            "type": "object",
            "additionalProperties": False,
            "required": ["source_path", "captured_at"],
            "properties": {
                "source_path": {"$ref": "#/$defs/Path"},
                "page_or_timecode": {"type": "string"},
                "bates_or_hash_optional": {"type": "string"},
                "captured_at": {"$ref": "#/$defs/IsoDateTime"},
                "note": {"type": "string"}
            }
        },
        "LawPinpoint": {
            "type": "object",
            "additionalProperties": False,
            "required": ["authority_id", "chunk_pointer", "effective_date"],
            "properties": {
                "authority_id": {"$ref": "#/$defs/StableId"},
                "section": {"type": "string"},
                "effective_date": {"$ref": "#/$defs/IsoDate"},
                "chunk_pointer": {"type": "string"}
            }
        },
        "RunRef": {
            "type": "object",
            "additionalProperties": False,
            "required": ["run_id", "started_at"],
            "properties": {
                "run_id": {"$ref": "#/$defs/StableId"},
                "started_at": {"$ref": "#/$defs/IsoDateTime"},
                "ended_at": {"$ref": "#/$defs/IsoDateTime"},
                "status": {"type": "string", "enum": ["RUNNING", "SUCCESS", "FAIL", "PARTIAL", "BLOCKED", "DEGRADED"]},
                "host": {"type": "string"},
                "app_version": {"$ref": "#/$defs/SemVer"}
            }
        },
        "ErrorObject": {
            "type": "object",
            "additionalProperties": False,
            "required": ["code", "message", "ts"],
            "properties": {
                "ts": {"$ref": "#/$defs/IsoDateTime"},
                "code": {"$ref": "#/$defs/StableId"},
                "message": {"$ref": "#/$defs/NonEmptyString"},
                "detail": {"type": "object"}
            }
        },
        "JsonlEnvelope": {
            "type": "object",
            "additionalProperties": False,
            "required": ["ts", "event", "run_id"],
            "properties": {
                "ts": {"$ref": "#/$defs/IsoDateTime"},
                "event": {"$ref": "#/$defs/StableId"},
                "run_id": {"$ref": "#/$defs/StableId"},
                "level": {"type": "string", "enum": ["TRACE", "DEBUG", "INFO", "WARN", "ERROR"]},
                "message": {"type": "string"},
                "data": {"type": "object"}
            }
        }
    }
}
write_json("schemas/shared.defs.schema.json", shared)

def ref(def_name):
    return {"$ref": f"https://litigationos.local/schemas/shared.defs.schema.json#/$defs/{def_name}"}

def base_schema(schema_id, title, description):
    return {"$schema": DRAFT, "$id": schema_id, "title": title, "description": description, "type": "object", "additionalProperties": False}

# JSON-RPC 2.0 message schema (for Electron IPC / stdio)
jsonrpc = base_schema(
    "https://litigationos.local/schemas/ipc_jsonrpc.schema.json",
    "IPC_JSONRPC",
    "JSON-RPC 2.0 message schema for UI IPC. Used for Electron main<->renderer and UI<->local services over stdio or named pipes."
)
jsonrpc["required"] = ["jsonrpc"]
jsonrpc["properties"] = {
    "jsonrpc": {"type": "string", "const": "2.0"},
    "id": {"type": ["string", "number", "null"]},
    "method": {"type": "string"},
    "params": {"type": ["object", "array", "null"]},
    "result": {"type": ["object", "array", "string", "number", "boolean", "null"]},
    "error": {
        "type": "object",
        "additionalProperties": False,
        "required": ["code", "message"],
        "properties": {
            "code": {"type": "integer"},
            "message": {"type": "string"},
            "data": {"type": ["object", "array", "string", "number", "boolean", "null"]}
        }
    }
}
write_json("schemas/ipc_jsonrpc.schema.json", jsonrpc)

# UI IPC message catalog (strongly-typed methods)
ui_ipc = base_schema(
    "https://litigationos.local/schemas/ui_ipc_messages.schema.json",
    "UI_IPC_MESSAGES",
    "Whitelisted UI IPC methods, params, results, and required gate checks."
)
ui_ipc["required"] = ["version", "methods"]
ui_ipc["properties"] = {
    "version": ref("SemVer"),
    "methods": {
        "type": "array",
        "minItems": 1,
        "items": {
            "type": "object",
            "additionalProperties": False,
            "required": ["method", "params_schema", "result_schema", "requires_gates"],
            "properties": {
                "method": {"type": "string"},
                "params_schema": ref("Uri"),
                "result_schema": ref("Uri"),
                "requires_gates": {
                    "type": "array",
                    "items": {"type": "string", "enum": ["TRUTH_LOCK", "MI_AUTH_LOCK", "PCW", "PCG", "PII_REDACTION_PASS", "OPERATOR_CONFIRM"]},
                    "minItems": 0
                },
                "notes": {"type": "string"}
            }
        }
    }
}
write_json("schemas/ui_ipc_messages.schema.json", ui_ipc)

# Quote DB line schema (JSONL line)

    "type": "object",
    "additionalProperties": False,
    "required": ["quote_id", "text", "pin", "run_id", "ts"],
    "properties": {

        "run_id": ref("StableId"),

        "speaker": {"type": "string"},

        "confidence": ref("Score01"),
        "tags": {"type":"array","items":{"type":"string"}}

}

contradiction = base_schema(

contradiction["properties"] = {

    "nodes": {"type":"array","items":{"type":"object","additionalProperties":False,"required":["node_id","kind"],"properties":{

        "ref": {"type":"string"}
    }}},
    "edges": {"type":"array","items":{"type":"object","additionalProperties":False,"required":["src","dst","type","pins"],"properties":{
        "src": ref("StableId"),
        "dst": ref("StableId"),
        "type": {"type":"string","enum":["CONFLICTS_WITH","UNDERMINES","IMPEACHES","INCONSISTENT_WITH","MISMATCH_RECORD"]},
        "severity": {"type":"integer","minimum":1,"maximum":5},
        "rationale": {"type":"string"},
        "pins": {"type":"array","minItems":1,"items": ref("EvidencePinpoint")}
    }}},
    "errors": {"type":"array","items": ref("ErrorObject")}
}
write_json("schemas/contradiction_map.schema.json", contradiction)

        "pins": {"type":"array","items": ref("EvidencePinpoint")}

}

service_chain = base_schema(

service_chain["properties"] = {

        "step_id": ref("StableId"),
        "method": {"type":"string"},
        "rule_pin": ref("LawPinpoint"),
        "address": {"type":"string"},
        "proof_required": {"type":"boolean"},
        "status": {"type":"string","enum":["PLANNED","READY","SERVED","FAILED","BLOCKED"]},
        "pins": {"type":"array","items": ref("EvidencePinpoint")}
    }}},
    "errors": {"type":"array","items": ref("ErrorObject")}
}
write_json("schemas/service_chain_report.schema.json", service_chain)

# Notice deficiency log schema
notice_log = base_schema(
    "https://litigationos.local/schemas/notice_deficiency_log.schema.json",
    "NOTICE_DEFICIENCY_LOG",

)
notice_log["required"] = ["run", "entries"]
notice_log["properties"] = {
    "run": ref("RunRef"),
    "entries": {"type":"array","items":{"type":"object","additionalProperties":False,"required":["entry_id","issue","severity","pins"],"properties":{
        "entry_id": ref("StableId"),
        "issue": ref("NonEmptyString"),
        "severity": {"type":"string","enum":["LOW","MEDIUM","HIGH","CRITICAL"]},
        "rule_pin": ref("LawPinpoint"),
        "pins": {"type":"array","minItems":1,"items": ref("EvidencePinpoint")},
        "recommended_action": {"type":"string"}
    }}}
}
write_json("schemas/notice_deficiency_log.schema.json", notice_log)

# PII scan + redaction schemas
pii_scan = base_schema(

)

pii_scan["properties"] = {
    "run": ref("RunRef"),
    "artifact": {"type":"object","additionalProperties":False,"required":["path","sha256"],"properties":{"path":ref("Path"),"sha256":ref("Sha256")}},
    "findings": {"type":"array","items":{"type":"object","additionalProperties":False,"required":["kind","span","confidence"],"properties":{
        "kind": {"type":"string","enum":["SSN","DOB","MINOR_NAME","ADDRESS","PHONE","EMAIL","FINANCIAL","MEDICAL","OTHER"]},
        "span": {"type":"object","additionalProperties":False,"required":["start","end"],"properties":{"start":{"type":"integer","minimum":0},"end":{"type":"integer","minimum":0}}},
        "confidence": ref("Score01"),
        "pin": ref("EvidencePinpoint")
    }}},
    "errors": {"type":"array","items": ref("ErrorObject")}
}
write_json("schemas/pii_scan_report.schema.json", pii_scan)

    "output": {"type":"object","additionalProperties":False,"required":["path","sha256"],"properties":{"path":ref("Path"),"sha256":ref("Sha256")}},
    "redactions": {"type":"array","items":{"type":"object","additionalProperties":False,"required":["kind","span","reason"],"properties":{
        "kind": {"type":"string"},
        "span": {"type":"object","additionalProperties":False,"required":["start","end"],"properties":{"start":{"type":"integer"},"end":{"type":"integer"}}},
        "reason": {"type":"string"},
        "pin": ref("EvidencePinpoint")
    }}},
    "errors": {"type":"array","items": ref("ErrorObject")}
}
write_json("schemas/redaction_report.schema.json", redaction)

}

    }}}
}
write_json("schemas/retrieval_result.schema.json", retrieval_result)

# NLP adapter OpenAPI + Qdrant adapter OpenAPI + Packaging API + Forms API
def openapi_doc(title, description, tag, port, path_lines):
    lines = []
    lines.append("openapi: 3.1.0")
    lines.append("info:")
    lines.append(f"  title: {title}")
    lines.append("  version: v1.0.0")
    lines.append("  description: |")
    for ln in description.splitlines():
        lines.append(f"    {ln}")
    lines.append(f"jsonSchemaDialect: {OPENAPI_DIALECT}")
    lines.append("servers:")
    lines.append(f"  - url: http://127.0.0.1:{port}")
    lines.append("    description: Localhost (default)")
    lines.append("tags:")
    lines.append(f"  - name: {tag}")
    lines.append("paths:")
    lines.extend(path_lines)
    return "\n".join(lines) + "\n"

def path_block(path, method, tag, summary, req_ref=None, resp_ref=None, stream=False):
    m = method.lower()
    lines = [f"  {path}:", f"    {m}:", f"      tags:", f"        - {tag}", f"      summary: {summary}"]
    if req_ref:
        lines += ["      requestBody:", "        required: true", "        content:", "          application/json:", "            schema:", f"              $ref: '{req_ref}'"]
    lines += ["      responses:", "        '200':", "          description: OK", "          content:"]
    if stream:
        lines += [
            "            application/x-ndjson:",
            "              schema:",
            "                type: object",
            "                description: One JSON object per line.",
            "            text/event-stream:",
            "              schema:",
            "                type: string",
            "                description: Optional SSE channel."
        ]
    else:
        lines += ["            application/json:", "              schema:"]
        if resp_ref:
            lines.append(f"                $ref: '{resp_ref}'")
        else:
            lines.append("                type: object")
    return lines

nlp_paths = []
nlp_paths += path_block("/v1/health","get","nlp","NLP adapter health probe.")
nlp_paths += path_block("/v1/extract","post","nlp","Run NLP extraction on a canonical text artifact.", req_ref="https://litigationos.local/schemas/retrieval_query.schema.json", resp_ref="https://litigationos.local/schemas/nlp_report.schema.json")
nlp_paths += path_block("/v1/quote_db/append","post","nlp","Append pinned quotes to QuoteDB JSONL.", resp_ref="https://litigationos.local/schemas/quote_db_line.schema.json")
nlp_paths += path_block("/v1/chrono_db/append","post","nlp","Append bi-temporal events to ChronoDB JSONL.", resp_ref="https://litigationos.local/schemas/chrono_db_line.schema.json")
nlp_yaml = openapi_doc(
    "LitigationOS NLP Adapter",
    "Local NLP boundary for entity/event/relation extraction and QuoteDB/ChronoDB production. Outputs are candidates until pinned.\nNo authority invention permitted.",
    "nlp",
    32117,
    nlp_paths
)
write_text("openapi/nlp_adapter.yaml", nlp_yaml)

qdrant_paths = []
qdrant_paths += path_block("/v1/health","get","qdrant","Qdrant adapter health probe.")
qdrant_paths += path_block("/v1/index/build","post","qdrant","Build or update a collection index for a run.", resp_ref="https://litigationos.local/schemas/qdrant_index_report.schema.json")
qdrant_paths += path_block("/v1/query","post","qdrant","Hybrid vector query with optional reranking.", req_ref="https://litigationos.local/schemas/retrieval_query.schema.json", resp_ref="https://litigationos.local/schemas/retrieval_result.schema.json")
qdrant_yaml = openapi_doc(
    "LitigationOS Qdrant Adapter",
    "Local vector store boundary (Qdrant). Provides index build reports and hybrid retrieval with replayable query objects.",
    "qdrant",
    32118,
    qdrant_paths
)
write_text("openapi/qdrant_adapter.yaml", qdrant_yaml)

forms_paths = []
forms_paths += path_block("/v1/health","get","forms","Forms adapter health probe.")
forms_paths += path_block("/v1/overlay/apply","post","forms","Apply a form overlay and emit FORM_OVERLAY_AUDIT.", resp_ref="https://litigationos.local/schemas/form_overlay_audit.schema.json")
forms_yaml = openapi_doc(
    "LitigationOS Forms Adapter",
    "Local forms boundary for SCAO/FOC/MC overlays and field-level audit/provenance guarantees.",
    "forms",
    32119,
    forms_paths
)
write_text("openapi/forms_adapter.yaml", forms_yaml)

pack_paths = []
pack_paths += path_block("/v1/health","get","packaging","Packaging service health probe.")
pack_paths += path_block("/v1/bundle/build","post","packaging","Build a FULL or PATCH release and emit BUNDLE_MANIFEST.", resp_ref="https://litigationos.local/schemas/bundle_manifest.schema.json")
pack_paths += path_block("/v1/bundle/files","get","packaging","List files in last build with hashes.", resp_ref="https://litigationos.local/schemas/bundle_manifest.schema.json")
pack_yaml = openapi_doc(
    "LitigationOS Packaging API",
    "Local packaging boundary. Enforces size budgets, excludes weights by default, and emits immutable bundle manifests with attestations.",
    "packaging",
    32120,
    pack_paths
)
write_text("openapi/packaging_api.yaml", pack_yaml)

# UI IPC contract doc (method whitelist + gate mapping)
ui_ipc_md = """# UI IPC Contract (Electron Main/Renderer + UI↔Local Services)

This contract defines the **only permitted UI IPC methods** and the **gate requirements** enforced before any action is executed.

## IPC Transport Options (offline-first)
- Electron `ipcMain.handle` / `ipcRenderer.invoke` (renderer ↔ main).
- JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio or named pipes (main ↔ local services).
- Each message is validated against `schemas/ipc_jsonrpc.schema.json`.
- Each method must exist in `schemas/ui_ipc_messages.schema.json` whitelist.

## Required Gate Checks (enforced in main process)
- TRUTH_LOCK
- MI_AUTH_LOCK
- PCW (Proof-Carrying Workflow)
- PCG (Proof-Carrying Gate for irreversible acts)
- PII_REDACTION_PASS
- OPERATOR_CONFIRM (interactive approval barrier)

## Canonical Method Set (minimum)
- `run.start` → requires TRUTH_LOCK, MI_AUTH_LOCK
- `run.subscribeEvents` → no gates, read-only
- `artifact.open` → no gates
- `graph.query` → no gates when read-only, otherwise TRUTH_LOCK + OPERATOR_CONFIRM
- `forms.overlayApply` → TRUTH_LOCK, PCW
- `export.buildBundle` → TRUTH_LOCK, PCW, PCG, PII_REDACTION_PASS, OPERATOR_CONFIRM
- `settings.set` → OPERATOR_CONFIRM when relaxing gates

## Error Handling
- Every IPC error is surfaced as:
  - JSON-RPC `error.code` (integer)
  - `error.message` (operator-readable)
  - `error.data` containing `blockers` for gate failures
"""
write_text("ipc/UI_IPC_CONTRACT.md", ui_ipc_md)

# Model shortlist (grounded in HF hub search output above)
now = datetime.datetime.utcnow().replace(microsecond=0).isoformat() + "Z"
model_shortlist = {
    "generated_at": now,
    "notes": "Shortlist derived from Hugging Face Hub searches performed during spec generation. Treat as candidates; local licensing and revisions must be pinned before use.",
    "models": [
        {"id":"microsoft/layoutlmv3-base","kind":"DOC_LAYOUT","url":"https://hf.co/microsoft/layoutlmv3-base","license":"cc-by-nc-sa-4.0","use":"layout-aware parsing; structure + token classification"},
        {"id":"microsoft/layoutlmv3-large","kind":"DOC_LAYOUT","url":"https://hf.co/microsoft/layoutlmv3-large","license":"cc-by-nc-sa-4.0","use":"higher-capacity layout model; optional"},
        {"id":"microsoft/trocr-base-printed","kind":"OCR_VISION","url":"https://hf.co/microsoft/trocr-base-printed","license":"UNKNOWN_IN_THIS_PACK","use":"vision OCR model for image-to-text; optional supplement to OCRmyPDF/Tesseract"},
        {"id":"microsoft/trocr-large-printed","kind":"OCR_VISION","url":"https://hf.co/microsoft/trocr-large-printed","license":"UNKNOWN_IN_THIS_PACK","use":"higher-capacity vision OCR; optional"},
        {"id":"nlpaueb/legal-bert-base-uncased","kind":"NER","url":"https://hf.co/nlpaueb/legal-bert-base-uncased","license":"cc-by-sa-4.0","use":"legal domain encoder for MI-adjacent legal NLP tasks"},
        {"id":"nlpaueb/legal-bert-small-uncased","kind":"NER","url":"https://hf.co/nlpaueb/legal-bert-small-uncased","license":"cc-by-sa-4.0","use":"smaller legal encoder for faster local inference"},
        {"id":"BAAI/bge-m3","kind":"EMBEDDINGS","url":"https://hf.co/BAAI/bge-m3","license":"mit","use":"hybrid multilingual embeddings for vector store indexing and retrieval"},
        {"id":"BAAI/bge-reranker-v2-m3","kind":"RERANKER","url":"https://hf.co/BAAI/bge-reranker-v2-m3","license":"apache-2.0","use":"reranking top-k candidates; retrieval quality boost"},
    ],
    "datasets":[
        {"id":"pile-of-law/pile-of-law","url":"https://hf.co/datasets/pile-of-law/pile-of-law","license":"cc-by-nc-sa-4.0","use":"evaluation and generic legal language calibration; not MI-specific authority"}
    ]
}
write_json("registry/MODEL_SHORTLIST.json", model_shortlist)

# SPEC addendum markdown (cycles 21-40)
spec_md = f"""# LITIGATIONOS__MASTERv1.0 — SPEC ADDENDUM v0004 (Chained Expansion Cycles 21–40)

Generated: {now}

This addendum continues the append-only escalation with **20 additional cycles** focused on:
- Autonomy governors that remain fail-closed
- OCR + Document Layout + NLP pipelines treated as evidence transforms
- GraphRAG retrieval contracts (Neo4j + Qdrant) with replayable queries
- UI IPC whitelist hardening (no hidden execution paths)
- PII/redaction gating as a pre-export invariant

---

## 0) Files shipped in this pack (normative)
### Schemas (Draft 2020-12)
- `schemas/ipc_jsonrpc.schema.json`
- `schemas/ui_ipc_messages.schema.json`
- `schemas/quote_db_line.schema.json`
- `schemas/chrono_db_line.schema.json`
- `schemas/entity_ledger_line.schema.json`
- `schemas/contradiction_map.schema.json`
- `schemas/deadline_report.schema.json`
- `schemas/service_chain_report.schema.json`
- `schemas/notice_deficiency_log.schema.json`
- `schemas/pii_scan_report.schema.json`
- `schemas/redaction_report.schema.json`
- `schemas/model_registry.schema.json`
- `schemas/dataset_registry.schema.json`
- `schemas/llm_infer_audit.schema.json`
- `schemas/qdrant_index_report.schema.json`
- `schemas/retrieval_query.schema.json`
- `schemas/retrieval_result.schema.json`

### OpenAPI 3.1 contracts
- `openapi/nlp_adapter.yaml` (port 32117)
- `openapi/qdrant_adapter.yaml` (port 32118)
- `openapi/forms_adapter.yaml` (port 32119)
- `openapi/packaging_api.yaml` (port 32120)

### IPC contract
- `ipc/UI_IPC_CONTRACT.md`

### Registries
- `registry/MODEL_SHORTLIST.json`

---

## 1) Cycle 21 — UI IPC is a Whitelist, not a Convention
**Objective:** eliminate any hidden side-channel command execution.
- IPC messages MUST validate against JSON-RPC 2.0 schema.
- Method name MUST exist in the `UI_IPC_MESSAGES` whitelist.
- Each method declares required gates.
- Electron main process is the sole gate enforcer.

**Acceptance:** a method not in whitelist is rejected before any handler executes.

---

## 2) Cycle 22 — Gate-Aware UI Commands (PCW and PCG at the UI Edge)
**Objective:** prevent UI actions from bypassing safeguards.
- `export.buildBundle` requires: TRUTH_LOCK, MI_AUTH_LOCK, PCW, PCG, PII_REDACTION_PASS, OPERATOR_CONFIRM.
- `forms.overlayApply` requires: TRUTH_LOCK, PCW.
- `graph.query` is read-only by default; mutations require OPERATOR_CONFIRM.

**Acceptance:** gate relaxation triggers an explicit audit event and an operator confirmation barrier.

---

## 3) Cycle 23 — OCR is a Transform with Provenance, Not a “Feature”
**Objective:** treat OCR as a forensic transform.
- OCR run emits: `OCR_REPORT` (page actions, coverage, confidence proxy).
- OCR output is never “primary”; originals remain immutable.
- OCR pins back to original file hash and page index.

**Acceptance:** no OCR-derived statement can exist without a pinned origin.

---

## 4) Cycle 24 — Layout Understanding Lane (Structure Before Semantics)
**Objective:** split structure extraction from semantic extraction.
- Layout stage outputs: block hierarchy, headings, tables, footnotes, page regions.
- Semantic stage consumes layout artifacts to produce better QuotePins and Events.

**Model candidates (must pin license and revision before use):**
- https://hf.co/microsoft/layoutlmv3-base
- https://hf.co/microsoft/layoutlmv3-large

---

## 5) Cycle 25 — Vision OCR Supplements (Optional, License-Checked)
**Objective:** allow high-accuracy OCR on stubborn images.
- OCRmyPDF/Tesseract remains default.
- Vision OCR is optional for specific pages only.

**Model candidates:**
- https://hf.co/microsoft/trocr-base-printed
- https://hf.co/microsoft/trocr-large-printed

---

## 6) Cycle 26 — QuoteDB Canon (JSONL, Pinned, Reversible)
**Objective:** every quote is reversible to a pinpoint.
- QUOTE_DB is JSONL with `QUOTE_DB_LINE` schema.
- Quotes without pins are rejected, not stored.

**Acceptance:** QuoteDB ingestion fails fast on missing pins.

---

## 7) Cycle 27 — ChronoDB Canon (Bi-Temporal)
**Objective:** preserve event time and record time distinctly.
- CHRONO_DB is JSONL with `CHRONO_DB_LINE`.
- Event time may be partial, but it must be labeled and validated later.

**Acceptance:** ChronoDB is suitable for timeline warboard and appeals record work.

---

## 8) Cycle 28 — Entity Ledger Canon (Aliases + Pins)
**Objective:** unify names across filings, exhibits, and orders.
- ENTITY_LEDGER is JSONL with alias sets.
- No entity exists without pins.

**Acceptance:** entity normalization is deterministic and auditable.

---

## 9) Cycle 29 — Contradiction Map Canon (Justified Conflict Edges)
**Objective:** weaponizable contradictions without speculation.
- CONTRADICTION_MAP edges require:
  - severity,
  - rationale,
  - EvidencePinpoint list.

**Acceptance:** every contradiction is clickable to exact text evidence.

---

## 10) Cycle 30 — Deadline Engine Canon (Rule-Pinned Calculations)
**Objective:** computed deadlines require rule pins and triggers.
- DEADLINE_REPORT requires a LawPinpoint for each deadline.
- Missing controlling order pins creates explicit blockers.

**Acceptance:** a deadline without a rule pin is classified UNKNOWN and escalates.

---

## 11) Cycle 31 — Service Chain Canon (Notice and Proof Planning)
**Objective:** service is treated as a first-class procedural lane.
- SERVICE_CHAIN_REPORT supports planned and served states.
- NOTICE_DEFICIENCY_LOG records defects found in record artifacts.

**Acceptance:** packaging fails closed if required service proofs are missing.

---

## 12) Cycle 32 — PII Scan Before Export (Mandatory)
**Objective:** prevent accidental disclosure in generated filings.
- PII_SCAN_REPORT runs before any bundle export.
- Findings become required redactions or operator decisions.

**Acceptance:** export is blocked if scan required but not run.

---

## 13) Cycle 33 — Redaction as a Verified Transform (Mandatory When Required)
**Objective:** redaction is auditable, repeatable, and hashed.
- REDACTION_REPORT records:
  - what was redacted,
  - why,
  - output hash,
  - proof pins.

**Acceptance:** redaction output must validate; export uses redacted artifact, not the original.

---

## 14) Cycle 34 — GraphRAG Contract Canon (Replayable Retrieval)
**Objective:** retrieval is reproducible and explainable.
- RETRIEVAL_QUERY stores:
  - graph filters,
  - vector profile,
  - reranker profile.
- RETRIEVAL_RESULT stores ranked shards plus pins.

**Acceptance:** the same query object can be replayed and diffed.

---

## 15) Cycle 35 — Qdrant Boundary (Index Reports and Hybrid Queries)
**Objective:** treat vector indexing as a reportable build step.
- QDRANT_INDEX_REPORT required for each indexing run.
- Qdrant adapter serves hybrid retrieval with pinned results.

**Model candidates:**
- https://hf.co/BAAI/bge-m3
- https://hf.co/BAAI/bge-reranker-v2-m3

---

## 16) Cycle 36 — LLM Outputs Are Audited Objects (Truth-Lock Enforcement)
**Objective:** local LLM use is fully logged and fail-closed.
- LLM_INFER_AUDIT records:
  - required pins and pin coverage,
  - blocked reasons,
  - output hash.

**Acceptance:** any LLM output used downstream must have its audit record linked by run_id.

---

## 17) Cycle 37 — Evaluation Datasets Are Declared and License-Gated
**Objective:** prevent silent dataset contamination or licensing violations.
- DATASET_REGISTRY required when datasets are used.
- Any dataset with a non-permissive license is tagged and restricted.

**Candidate (evaluation only, not Michigan authority):**
- https://hf.co/datasets/pile-of-law/pile-of-law

---

## 18) Cycle 38 — Packaging Boundary Adds Gate Checks and Policy Attestations
**Objective:** bundle building must enforce size budgets and export gates.
- Packaging API emits BUNDLE_MANIFEST with attestations.
- Weights remain excluded by default.

**Acceptance:** packaging refuses to build a filing bundle when PCG is FAIL.

---

## 19) Cycle 39 — Autonomy Governor Gains Degraded Modes
**Objective:** autonomy continues safely in partial-failure conditions.
- Degraded modes include:
  - OCR unavailable → text extraction only, flag blockers
  - Qdrant down → graph-only retrieval, flag blockers
  - Neo4j down → flat-file reports only, flag blockers

**Acceptance:** system never invents; it produces blockers and preserves all logs.

---

## 20) Cycle 40 — Conformance Suite Becomes Release Gate
**Objective:** no release without interface conformance.
- Validate all JSON schemas.
- Validate all OpenAPI contracts.
- Validate method whitelist for UI IPC.
- Validate that pack catalogs match file hashes.

**Acceptance:** release build is impossible if catalogs drift or contracts fail.

---

## 2) Model shortlist (informational, must be pinned before use)

See `registry/MODEL_SHORTLIST.json`. Every candidate must be:
- license-validated
- revision-pinned
- local asset hashes recorded
before being activated in production profiles.
"""
write_text("SPEC_ADDENDUM_v0004.md", spec_md)

# Contract smoke test v2 (no external deps)
smoke = r'''#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
LitigationOS Contract Smoke Test v2 (offline, zero external deps)

Validates:
- JSON Schemas declare Draft 2020-12 ($schema)
- OpenAPI YAML declares openapi: 3.1.0 and jsonSchemaDialect
- Builds SCHEMA_CATALOG and CONTRACT_CATALOG with sha256
- Emits CONTRACT_TEST_REPORT.json

Usage:
  python tools/contract_smoke_test_v2.py --root /path/to/pack_root
"""

from __future__ import annotations
import argparse, json, hashlib
from pathlib import Path
from datetime import datetime, timezone

DRAFT = "https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema"

def sha256_file(p: Path) -> str:
    return hashlib.sha256(p.read_bytes()).hexdigest()

def utc_now() -> str:
    return datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(microsecond=0).isoformat()

def main() -> int:
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    ap.add_argument("--root", required=True)
    args = ap.parse_args()

    root = Path(args.root).resolve()
    schemas_dir = root / "schemas"
    openapi_dir = root / "openapi"
    registry_dir = root / "REGISTRY"

    report = {
        "generated_at": utc_now(),
        "root": str(root),
        "checks": [],
        "errors": [],
        "schema_catalog": {},
        "contract_catalog": {}
    }

    # Schemas
    if not schemas_dir.exists():
        report["errors"].append({"code":"MISSING_SCHEMAS_DIR","message":"schemas/ directory not found"})
    else:
        for p in sorted(schemas_dir.glob("*.json")):
            try:
                obj = json.loads(p.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
                if obj.get("$schema") != DRAFT:
                    report["errors"].append({"code":"BAD_SCHEMA_DIALECT","message":f"{p.name} $schema must be {DRAFT}"})
                report["schema_catalog"][str(p.relative_to(root)).replace("\\","/")] = sha256_file(p)
            except Exception as e:
                report["errors"].append({"code":"SCHEMA_PARSE_FAIL","message":f"{p.name} parse failed: {e}"})

    # OpenAPI YAML minimal validation
    if not openapi_dir.exists():
        report["errors"].append({"code":"MISSING_OPENAPI_DIR","message":"openapi/ directory not found"})
    else:
        for p in sorted(openapi_dir.glob("*.yaml")):
            try:
                txt = p.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
                if "openapi: 3.1.0" not in txt:
                    report["errors"].append({"code":"BAD_OPENAPI_VERSION","message":f"{p.name} must declare openapi: 3.1.0"})
                if "jsonSchemaDialect:" not in txt:
                    report["errors"].append({"code":"MISSING_JSONSCHEMA_DIALECT","message":f"{p.name} must declare jsonSchemaDialect"})
                report["contract_catalog"][str(p.relative_to(root)).replace("\\","/")] = sha256_file(p)
            except Exception as e:
                report["errors"].append({"code":"OPENAPI_READ_FAIL","message":f"{p.name} read failed: {e}"})

    # Registries (optional)
    if registry_dir.exists():
        for p in sorted(registry_dir.glob("*.json")):
            report["checks"].append({"kind":"registry_present","file":p.name,"sha256":sha256_file(p)})

    outdir = root / "reports"
    outdir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    (outdir / "CONTRACT_TEST_REPORT.json").write_text(json.dumps(report, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")

    # Emit catalogs
    regdir = root / "REGISTRY"
    regdir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    (regdir / "SCHEMA_CATALOG.json").write_text(json.dumps(report["schema_catalog"], indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
    (regdir / "CONTRACT_CATALOG.json").write_text(json.dumps(report["contract_catalog"], indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")

    return 0 if not report["errors"] else 2

if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())
'''
write_text("tools/contract_smoke_test_v2.py", smoke)

# Build pack manifest (hash list)
files = []
for p in sorted(out_root.rglob("*")):
    if p.is_file():
        rel = str(p.relative_to(out_root)).replace("\\","/")
        b = p.read_bytes()
        files.append({"path": rel, "bytes": len(b), "sha256": sha256_bytes(b)})
pack_manifest = {
    "bundle_id": "LITIGATIONOS__MASTERv1.0__SPEC_ADDENDUM_v0004",
    "built_at": now,
    "files": files
}
write_json("PACK_MANIFEST.json", pack_manifest)

# Also create REGISTRY catalogs now (pre-smoke test) for immediate drift protection
schema_catalog = {}
contract_catalog = {}
for p in sorted((out_root/"schemas").glob("*.json")):
    schema_catalog[str(p.relative_to(out_root)).replace("\\","/")] = sha256_file(p)
for p in sorted((out_root/"openapi").glob("*.yaml")):
    contract_catalog[str(p.relative_to(out_root)).replace("\\","/")] = sha256_file(p)
write_json("REGISTRY/SCHEMA_CATALOG.json", schema_catalog)
write_json("REGISTRY/CONTRACT_CATALOG.json", contract_catalog)

# Zip
zip_path = Path("/mnt/data/LITIGATIONOS__MASTERv1.0__SPEC_ADDENDUM_v0004.zip")
if zip_path.exists():
    zip_path.unlink()
with zipfile.ZipFile(zip_path, "w", compression=zipfile.ZIP_DEFLATED) as z:
    for p in sorted(out_root.rglob("*")):
        if p.is_file():
            z.write(p, arcname=str(p.relative_to(out_root)).replace("\\","/"))

(size := zip_path.stat().st_size, sha256_file(zip_path), str(zip_path))

from pathlib import Path
import json, zipfile, hashlib, datetime

out_root = Path("/mnt/data/spec_addendum_v0004")
assert out_root.exists()

def sha256_bytes(b: bytes) -> str:
    import hashlib
    return hashlib.sha256(b).hexdigest()

def sha256_file(p: Path) -> str:
    return sha256_bytes(p.read_bytes())

now = datetime.datetime.utcnow().replace(microsecond=0).isoformat() + "Z"

# Update MODEL_SHORTLIST.json to remove UNKNOWN wording, use PINPOINT_MISSING.

for m in ms["models"]:
    if m["id"] in ["microsoft/trocr-base-printed","microsoft/trocr-large-printed"]:
        m["license"] = "PINPOINT_MISSING:MODEL_LICENSE_NOT_DECLARED_IN_HF_METADATA_AT_QUERY_TIME"
        m["license_acquire_plan"] = "Acquire: open upstream repo card/README or LICENSE file; record license identifier and pin revision in MODEL_REGISTRY."
ms["updated_at"] = now
model_shortlist_path.write_text(json.dumps(ms, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")

# Update spec markdown references
spec_path = out_root / "SPEC_ADDENDUM_v0004.md"
txt = spec_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
txt = txt.replace("microsoft/trocr-base-printed\n- https://hf.co/microsoft/trocr-base-printed\n- https://hf.co/microsoft/trocr-large-printed",
                  "microsoft/trocr-base-printed\n- https://hf.co/microsoft/trocr-base-printed\n- https://hf.co/microsoft/trocr-large-printed\n\nLicense status for TrOCR models: PINPOINT_MISSING (not declared in Hugging Face metadata at query time); must be pinned before activation.")
spec_path.write_text(txt, encoding="utf-8")

pack_manifest["rebuilt_at"] = now
pack_manifest["files"] = files
pack_manifest_path.write_text(json.dumps(pack_manifest, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")

schema_catalog = {}
contract_catalog = {}

LITIGATIONOS Canonical Spec Escalation: Autonomy + OCR + NLP + Retrieval + UI IPC (Chained Cycles 21–40)

You are now at the point where “canonical spec god grade” requires four additional invariants beyond schemas and OpenAPI:

    Autonomy that is provably non-destructive (fail-closed governors, degraded modes, blocker emission as the default success path).

    Forensic transforms treated as record artifacts (OCR, layout, NLP, redaction) with manifests, hashes, and pinned provenance.

    Replayable retrieval (GraphRAG query objects that can be re-run and diffed).

    UI boundary hardening (IPC as a whitelist plus gate requirements, so there is no hidden execution surface).

This turn ships that escalation as Spec Addendum v0004, implementing the above as Draft 2020-12 JSON Schemas, OpenAPI 3.1 service contracts, and a UI IPC contract.
What is added at “God-Grade” level (dense, high leverage)
A) Autonomy Governor: “Run Forward, Never Invent”

Autonomy becomes a state machine with explicit degraded modes:

    OCR down → extraction continues without OCR, but emits PINPOINT_MISSING blockers for text coverage gaps.

    Neo4j down → emits flat-file reports and preserves artifacts; graph writes are deferred and logged.

    Qdrant down → graph-only retrieval continues; vector retrieval blockers emitted.

    LLM down → no drafting; extraction-only continues; LLM_INFER_AUDIT logs blocked actions.

Critical invariant: “partial success” is never silent. It is a first-class status with artifacts to prove what happened.
B) OCR + Layout + NLP as Evidence Transforms (not “features”)

To reach judicial-grade defensibility, you treat these as transform artifacts:

    Every derived text output is linked to an immutable original via hash.

    Every quote/event/entity is stored as a pinned unit:

        QuoteDB JSONL line schema: QUOTE_DB_LINE

        ChronoDB JSONL line schema: CHRONO_DB_LINE

        Entity ledger JSONL line schema: ENTITY_LEDGER_LINE

C) Contradictions, Deadlines, Service, Notice: First-Class Procedural Lanes

This is where systems become “litigation operating systems” instead of “document processors”:

    CONTRADICTION_MAP requires justifying pins for each conflict edge.

    DEADLINE_REPORT requires rule pins and trigger logic; unknown deadlines become explicit.

    SERVICE_CHAIN_REPORT and NOTICE_DEFICIENCY_LOG enforce service and notice tracking as release gates.

D) PII and Redaction Become Export Gates

Export is blocked until:

    PII_SCAN_REPORT exists, and

    required redactions (if any) are executed and proven via REDACTION_REPORT.

E) GraphRAG Retrieval is Replayable (No Mystery Search)

Two canonical objects enforce repeatability:

    RETRIEVAL_QUERY captures graph filters + vector profile + reranker profile.

    RETRIEVAL_RESULT returns ranked shards with pins.

This gives you: reproducible search, deterministic diffs, and audit-ready “why this context” provenance.
F) UI IPC is a Whitelist With Gate Declarations

Instead of “UI can call anything,” you get:

    JSON-RPC 2.0 message schema (IPC_JSONRPC)

    method whitelist schema (UI_IPC_MESSAGES)

    human-readable IPC contract (UI_IPC_CONTRACT.md) mapping methods → required gates.

This closes the most common real-world bypass: UI triggering exports or risky operations without running PCW and PCG.
Hugging Face grounded shortlist for OCR, layout, legal NLP, embeddings (offline candidates)

These candidates were pulled from Hugging Face Hub searches during pack generation (links included inside the pack). They are candidates only until you pin revision + license into MODEL_REGISTRY.

    Layout understanding:

        https://hf.co/microsoft/layoutlmv3-base

        https://hf.co/microsoft/layoutlmv3-large

    Vision OCR supplement (optional):

        https://hf.co/microsoft/trocr-base-printed

        https://hf.co/microsoft/trocr-large-printed

        License status for these TrOCR models is PINPOINT_MISSING in this pack (not declared in HF metadata at query time); the pack includes an acquisition plan.

    Legal-domain encoder:

        https://hf.co/nlpaueb/legal-bert-base-uncased

        https://hf.co/nlpaueb/legal-bert-small-uncased

    Embeddings + rerank:

        https://hf.co/BAAI/bge-m3

        https://hf.co/BAAI/bge-reranker-v2-m3

    Evaluation dataset (non-Michigan authority):

        https://hf.co/datasets/pile-of-law/pile-of-law

What is inside the shipped pack (v0004)
Schemas (Draft 2020-12)

    UI IPC + JSON-RPC: ipc_jsonrpc.schema.json, ui_ipc_messages.schema.json

    JSONL line schemas: quote_db_line.schema.json, chrono_db_line.schema.json, entity_ledger_line.schema.json

    Procedural lanes: contradiction_map.schema.json, deadline_report.schema.json, service_chain_report.schema.json, notice_deficiency_log.schema.json

    Export gates: pii_scan_report.schema.json, redaction_report.schema.json

    Autonomy / ML governance: llm_infer_audit.schema.json, model_registry.schema.json, dataset_registry.schema.json

    Retrieval: qdrant_index_report.schema.json, retrieval_query.schema.json, retrieval_result.schema.json

OpenAPI 3.1 service contracts

    openapi/nlp_adapter.yaml (NLP boundary)

    openapi/qdrant_adapter.yaml (vector boundary)

    openapi/forms_adapter.yaml (forms boundary)

    openapi/packaging_api.yaml (release boundary)

Conformance tooling

    tools/contract_smoke_test_v2.py (offline validation + catalog build)

Audit products inside the pack

    PACK_MANIFEST.json with file hashes and sizes

    REGISTRY/SCHEMA_CATALOG.json

    REGISTRY/CONTRACT_CATALOG.json

    registry/MODEL_SHORTLIST.json with PINPOINT_MISSING where required

Immediate next spec-level expansions (PINPOINT_MISSING targets)

If you want the next “hard escalation” after v0004, these are the highest-value missing spec objects:

    OCR_REPORT schema + OCR adapter OpenAPI (page-by-page actions, coverage, confidence proxy, transform hashes).

    LAYOUT_REPORT schema (blocks, headings, tables, regions, reading order; pinned to pages).

    ASR_REPORT schema + audio adapter OpenAPI (time-coded quote pin generation).

    SBOM + dependency ledger schemas (license inventory as a release gate, not documentation).

    Policy schemas that become runtime-loaded: PIPELINE_POLICY, GATE_POLICY, MODEL_POLICY, REDACTION_POLICY.

    Conformance matrix that binds: “endpoint X must emit schema Y” and blocks release if violated.
