    "version": {"type": "string", "pattern": "^v\\d{4}$"},
    "root": {"type": "string", "minLength": 1},
    "paths": {
      "type": "object",
      "required": ["out", "cas", "governance", "logs", "tmp"],
      "properties": {
        "out": {"type":"string", "minLength":1},
        "cas": {"type":"string", "minLength":1},
        "governance": {"type":"string", "minLength":1},
        "logs": {"type":"string", "minLength":1},
        "tmp": {"type":"string", "minLength":1}
      },
      "additionalProperties": True
    },
    "policy": {"type":"object","additionalProperties": True}
  },
  "additionalProperties": False
})

# --- common paths resolver ---
write_text("services/common/paths.py", r'''from __future__ import annotations

from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any, Dict, Optional
import json
import os

from services.common.util import utc_now_z

ROOT_MARKERS = ("PACK_MANIFEST.json","SPEC_ADDENDUM_v0010.md","SPEC_ADDENDUM_v0011.md")

def _is_root(p: Path) -> bool:
    for m in ROOT_MARKERS:
        if (p / m).exists():
            return True
    if (p / "services").exists() and (p / "schemas").exists():
        return True
    return False

def find_root(start: Optional[Path] = None) -> Path:
    env_root = os.environ.get("LITIGATIONOS_ROOT", "").strip()
    if env_root:
        return Path(env_root).expanduser().resolve()
    if start is None:
        start = Path(__file__).resolve()
    p = start if start.is_dir() else start.parent
    for _ in range(20):
        if _is_root(p):
            return p.resolve()
        if p.parent == p:
            break
        p = p.parent
    return Path.cwd().resolve()

def _expand_templates(root: str, value: str) -> str:
    return (value or "").replace("{root}", root)

def load_paths_config(root: Optional[Path] = None) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    root = root or find_root()
    cfg_path = root / "config" / "paths.json"
    if not cfg_path.exists():
        r = str(root)
        return {"ok": True, "ts": utc_now_z(), "source": "derived_default",
                "config": {"version":"v0000","root":r,"paths":{
                    "out": str(root/"OUT"), "cas": str(root/"CAS"), "governance": str(root/"governance"),
                    "logs": str(root/"logs"), "tmp": str(root/"tmp")},
                    "policy":{"strict_transform_envelope": True}}}
    try:
        cfg = json.loads(cfg_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    except Exception as e:
        return {"ok": False, "ts": utc_now_z(), "errors":[{"ts": utc_now_z(), "code":"PATHS_JSON_PARSE_FAIL", "message": str(e), "detail":{"path": str(cfg_path)}}]}
    r = str(root)
    paths = cfg.get("paths") or {}
    expanded = {k: _expand_templates(r, str(v)) for k, v in paths.items()}
    cfg["paths"] = expanded
    return {"ok": True, "ts": utc_now_z(), "source": str(cfg_path), "config": cfg}

def get_path(key: str, root: Optional[Path] = None) -> Path:
    root = root or find_root()
    loaded = load_paths_config(root)
    if not loaded.get("ok"):
        return (root / key).resolve()
    cfg = loaded["config"]
    val = (cfg.get("paths") or {}).get(key)
    if not val:
        return (root / key).resolve()
    p = Path(val).expanduser()
    if not p.is_absolute():
        p = (root / p).resolve()
    return p
''')

# --- tools ---
write_text("tools/whereami.py", r'''#!/usr/bin/env python3
from __future__ import annotations
import json
from services.common.paths import find_root, load_paths_config
def main() -> int:
    root = find_root()
    cfg = load_paths_config(root)
    print(json.dumps({"root": str(root), "paths_config": cfg}, indent=2))
    return 0
if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())
''')

write_text("tools/bootstrap_paths_ps1.txt", r'''# LitigationOS v0011 bootstrap (PowerShell)
$ROOT = "C:\Users\andre\Downloads\LITIGATIONOS__MASTERv1.0__SPEC_ADDENDUM_v0011"
Set-Location $ROOT
$env:LITIGATIONOS_ROOT = $ROOT
$env:PYTHONPATH = $ROOT
Write-Host "ROOT=$ROOT"
Write-Host "PYTHONPATH=$env:PYTHONPATH"
Write-Host "LITIGATIONOS_ROOT=$env:LITIGATIONOS_ROOT"
''')

# --- patch services to expose /v1/paths + stdio method paths ---
def patch_service_paths(service_main: Path, service_prefix: str):
    if not service_main.exists():
        return
    t = service_main.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    if "from services.common.paths import find_root, load_paths_config" not in t:
        t = t.replace("from services.common.util import utc_now_z",
                      "from services.common.util import utc_now_z\nfrom services.common.paths import find_root, load_paths_config\n")
    if "def handler_paths" not in t:
        t = t.replace("def handler_health", f"def handler_paths(_: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n    return load_paths_config(find_root())\n\n\ndef handler_health")
    # stdio map
    marker = f"\"{service_prefix}.paths\""
    if marker not in t and f"\"{service_prefix}.health\"" in t:
        t = t.replace(f"\"{service_prefix}.health\": handler_health,",
                      f"\"{service_prefix}.health\": handler_health,\n        \"{service_prefix}.paths\": handler_paths,")
    # http endpoint
    if "/v1/paths" not in t and "/v1/health" in t:
        t = t.replace("@app.get(\"/v1/health\")",
                      "@app.get(\"/v1/paths\")\n    def paths():\n        return handler_paths({})\n\n    @app.get(\"/v1/health\")")
    service_main.write_text(t, encoding="utf-8")

patch_service_paths(work/"services"/"ontology"/"main.py", "ontology")
patch_service_paths(work/"services"/"ocr_adapter"/"main.py", "ocr_adapter")

# transform_gate special: may have POST /v1/health not GET; patch carefully
tg = work/"services"/"transform_gate"/"main.py"
if tg.exists():
    t = tg.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    if "from services.common.paths import find_root, load_paths_config" not in t:
        t = t.replace("from services.common.util import utc_now_z",
                      "from services.common.util import utc_now_z\nfrom services.common.paths import find_root, load_paths_config\n")
    if "def handler_paths" not in t:
        t = t.replace("def handler_enforce", "def handler_paths(params: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n    return load_paths_config(find_root())\n\n\ndef handler_enforce")
    if "\"transform_gate.paths\"" not in t and "\"transform_gate.health\"" in t:
        t = t.replace("\"transform_gate.health\": handler_health,",
                      "\"transform_gate.health\": handler_health,\n        \"transform_gate.paths\": handler_paths,")
    if "/v1/paths" not in t:
        # Insert before enforce endpoint
        t = t.replace("@app.post(\"/v1/transform/enforce\")",
                      "@app.get(\"/v1/paths\")\n    def paths():\n        return handler_paths({})\n\n    @app.post(\"/v1/transform/enforce\")")
    tg.write_text(t, encoding="utf-8")

# neo4j drift gate: default root to find_root
ndg = work/"services"/"neo4j_drift_gate"/"main.py"
if ndg.exists():
    t = ndg.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    if "from services.common.paths import find_root" not in t:
        t = t.replace("from services.common.util import utc_now_z",
                      "from services.common.util import utc_now_z\nfrom services.common.paths import find_root\n")
    t = t.replace("root = Path(params.get(\"root\") or \".\").resolve()", "root = Path(params.get(\"root\") or str(find_root())).resolve()")
    ndg.write_text(t, encoding="utf-8")

# pii governance: ledger stored under configured governance path
gov_py = work/"services"/"pii_adapter"/"governance.py"
if gov_py.exists():
    t = gov_py.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    if "from services.common.paths import get_path" not in t:
        t = t.replace("from services.common.util import ensure_dir, utc_now_z, append_jsonl, read_json",
                      "from services.common.util import ensure_dir, utc_now_z, append_jsonl, read_json\nfrom services.common.paths import get_path\n")
    t = t.replace("return root / \"governance\" / \"pii\" / LEDGER_FILE",
                  "return get_path(\"governance\", root=root) / \"pii\" / LEDGER_FILE")
    gov_py.write_text(t, encoding="utf-8")

Generated: {now}

## Canonical file
`config\\paths.json` is the global paths configuration file read by all services.

## Root discovery order
1) `LITIGATIONOS_ROOT` environment variable (preferred)
2) walk upward from module file location (marker-based)
3) current working directory (last resort)

## Template expansion
`{{root}}` in the config expands to the discovered root path.

python tools\\whereami.py

""")

write_text("tools/runtime_smoke_test_v0011.py", r'''#!/usr/bin/env python3
from future import annotations

import tempfile
from pathlib import Path

from services.common.paths import find_root, load_paths_config
from services.common.util import utc_now_z, sha256_file, write_json
from services.record_store.cas import ingest_observed
from services.ontology.compiler import compile_typed_units_from_text, validate_typed_bundle
from services.common.transform_envelope import enforce_transform_envelope
from services.common.enveloped_output import build_transform_envelope

def main() -> int:
root = find_root()
cfg = load_paths_config(root)
assert cfg.get("ok") is True
paths = (cfg.get("config") or {}).get("paths") or {}
assert "out" in paths and "cas" in paths and "governance" in paths

Always show details
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    tdroot = Path(td)
    cas_root = tdroot / "CAS"
    out = tdroot / "OUT"
    out.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    sample = tdroot / "sample.txt"
    sample.write_text("FACT: A fact.\nIT IS HEREBY ORDERED that a thing occurs.\n", encoding="utf-8")

    obs = [{
        "artifact": {"path": str(sample), "sha256": sha256_file(sample), "bytes": sample.stat().st_size, "mime":"text/plain", "created_at": utc_now_z()},
        "origin": {"observed_path": str(sample), "observed_at": utc_now_z(), "observed_bytes": sample.stat().st_size, "ingest_method":"LOCAL_FS", "operator_note":"smoke"}
    }]
    idx = ingest_observed(cas_root, obs)
    rec = idx["records"][0]

    built = compile_typed_units_from_text(sample, source_hint="order", enable_claims=False, enable_contradictions=False)
    ok, errs = validate_typed_bundle(built)
    assert ok or errs is not None

    ont_path = out / "typed.json"
    write_json(ont_path, built)

    env = build_transform_envelope(
        tool="ontology_compiler",
        tool_version="v0011",
        params={},
        inputs=[{"artifact":{"path": str(sample), "bytes": sample.stat().st_size, "mime":"text/plain", "created_at": utc_now_z()},
                 "digest":{"sha256": sha256_file(sample), "bytes": sample.stat().st_size},
                 "cas_origin":{"sha256": rec["sha256"], "observed_path": rec["origin"]["observed_path"], "observed_at": rec["origin"]["observed_at"], "ingest_method": rec["origin"]["ingest_method"]}}],
        outputs=[{"artifact":{"path": str(ont_path), "bytes": ont_path.stat().st_size, "mime":"application/json", "created_at": utc_now_z()},
                  "digest":{"sha256": sha256_file(ont_path), "bytes": ont_path.stat().st_size},
                  "cas_origin":{"sha256": rec["sha256"], "observed_path": rec["origin"]["observed_path"], "observed_at": rec["origin"]["observed_at"], "ingest_method": rec["origin"]["ingest_method"]}}],
        status="SUCCESS"
    )
    rep = enforce_transform_envelope(env, strict=True)
    assert rep["status"] == "PASS"

print("SMOKE_OK")
return 0

if name == "main":
raise SystemExit(main())
''')

SPEC ADDENDUM v0011 — GLOBAL config\paths.json + ROOT AUTO-DISCOVERY (NO MORE MANUAL PATH PASSING)

This escalation makes paths first-class infrastructure: every service can now derive $ROOT, expand {root} templates, and resolve canonical subpaths (OUT, CAS, governance, logs, tmp) without the orchestrator having to pass them around.
What ships in v0011 (dense, enforceable)
1) Standard config\paths.json (single source of truth)

File (operator machine):

    C:\Users\andre\Downloads\LITIGATIONOS__MASTERv1.0__SPEC_ADDENDUM_v0011\config\paths.json

Default contents:

    root recorded for operator clarity

    paths.* uses {root} template, expanded at runtime

    policy.strict_transform_envelope defaults to true

    policy.default_drive_exclusions defaults to ["C:\\"]

This yields deterministic subroots:

    {root}\OUT

    {root}\CAS

    {root}\governance

    {root}\logs

    {root}\tmp

2) Root auto-discovery (fail-safe, deterministic)

New runtime module:

    $ROOT\services\common\paths.py

Discovery order:

    LITIGATIONOS_ROOT environment variable (preferred)

    marker-walk upward from module path until root detected

    Path.cwd() (last resort)

Root markers include:

    PACK_MANIFEST.json

    SPEC_ADDENDUM_v0010.md

    SPEC_ADDENDUM_v0011.md

    plus fallback presence of services/ + schemas/

3) Path resolution API used by all services

New functions:

    find_root()

    load_paths_config()

    get_path(key)

load_paths_config() expands {root} templates automatically and returns a structured object:

    { ok, ts, source, config{version, root, paths{}, policy{...}} }

If config\paths.json is missing or invalid:

    returns derived defaults under discovered root (no guessing outside root)

4) “Paths” endpoints added to service boundaries

Updated services now expose:

    STDIO method: *.paths

    HTTP: GET /v1/paths

Updated modules:

    $ROOT\services\transform_gate\main.py

    $ROOT\services\ocr_adapter\main.py

    $ROOT\services\ontology\main.py

Neo4j drift gate improved:

    if root not provided, it defaults to find_root():

        $ROOT\services\neo4j_drift_gate\main.py

PII governance improved:

    ledger storage resolves via configured governance path:

        $ROOT\services\pii_adapter\governance.py

5) Operator tooling (zero ambiguity)

New tools:

    $ROOT\tools\whereami.py
    Prints discovered root + expanded config paths (ground truth diagnostic).

    $ROOT\tools\bootstrap_paths_ps1.txt
    Copy/paste PowerShell bootstrap (sets PYTHONPATH + LITIGATIONOS_ROOT).

6) v0011 smoke proof

    $ROOT\tools\runtime_smoke_test_v0011.py

    Verified PASS in pack build: SMOKE_OK

Exactly how to run it on your Windows machine (correct root + correct paths)
A) Extract

    Download ZIP (it lands in C:\Users\andre\Downloads\)

    Extract to:

    C:\Users\andre\Downloads\LITIGATIONOS__MASTERv1.0__SPEC_ADDENDUM_v0011\

B) Bootstrap (canonical)

Open PowerShell:

$ROOT="C:\Users\andre\Downloads\LITIGATIONOS__MASTERv1.0__SPEC_ADDENDUM_v0011"
Set-Location $ROOT
$env:PYTHONPATH=$ROOT
$env:LITIGATIONOS_ROOT=$ROOT
python "$ROOT\tools\whereami.py"

You should see:

    "root": "C:\\Users\\andre\\Downloads\\LITIGATIONOS__MASTERv1.0__SPEC_ADDENDUM_v0011"

    expanded "paths" for OUT/CAS/governance/logs/tmp

C) Smoke test

$ROOT="C:\Users\andre\Downloads\LITIGATIONOS__MASTERv1.0__SPEC_ADDENDUM_v0011"
Set-Location $ROOT
$env:PYTHONPATH=$ROOT
$env:LITIGATIONOS_ROOT=$ROOT
python "$ROOT\tools\runtime_smoke_test_v0011.py"

Expected:

    SMOKE_OK

Why this is the highest-leverage move (and what it unlocks next)

Once every boundary can resolve $ROOT and canonical subpaths automatically:

    Orchestrator payloads shrink
    No need to pass out_root, cas_root, governance_root repeatedly; services can infer.

    Contracts become enforceable
    Transform Envelope Enforcement can require that outputs land under configured OUT, with drift detection.

    Deterministic CasePack Builder becomes trivial
    CasePack can always assemble from $ROOT\OUT\... and $ROOT\CAS\... without operator configuration sprawl.

    Cross-service auditing becomes uniform
    Logs and manifests can be standardized to $ROOT\logs\... with a single retention policy.

Next escalation targets (if you want “chained cycles” applied again)

If you want the next hard step after this (v0012), the most leverage comes from extending this foundation into:

    Path Policy Gate

        validate that configured paths are writable/readable

        fail-closed if OUT/CAS/governance are not accessible

        emit a machine-checkable PATHS_HEALTH_REPORT

    Out/CAS Canonical Layout Contract

        reserve canonical folders:

            OUT\DERIVED\<LANE>\...

            OUT\ENVELOPES\...

            OUT\REPORTS\...

        enforce it inside transform gate strict mode

    Service “Config Snapshot” stamping

        every report/envelope includes:

            paths_config_source

            paths_config_sha256

            expanded paths dict (or reference id)

        enables reproducible builds for overlays and casepacks

from pathlib import Path
import shutil, zipfile, hashlib, datetime, json, os, subprocess, sys, re

src_zip = Path("/mnt/data/LITIGATIONOS__MASTERv1.0__SPEC_ADDENDUM_v0011.zip")
assert src_zip.exists() and src_zip.stat().st_size > 0

work = Path("/mnt/data/spec_addendum_v0012")
if work.exists():
    shutil.rmtree(work)
work.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
with zipfile.ZipFile(src_zip, "r") as z:
    z.extractall(work)

now = datetime.datetime.utcnow().replace(microsecond=0).isoformat() + "Z"

def sha256_bytes(b: bytes) -> str:
    return hashlib.sha256(b).hexdigest()

def sha256_file(p: Path) -> str:
    h = hashlib.sha256()
    with p.open("rb") as f:
        for chunk in iter(lambda: f.read(1024*1024), b""):
            h.update(chunk)
    return h.hexdigest()

def write_text(rel: str, s: str):
    p = work / rel
    p.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    p.write_text(s, encoding="utf-8")

def write_json(rel: str, obj):
    p = work / rel
    p.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    p.write_text(json.dumps(obj, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")

# ---------- 1) Paths Policy Gate schemas ----------
write_json("schemas/paths_health_report.schema.json", {
  "$schema": "https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema",
  "$id": "https://litigationos.local/schema/paths_health_report.schema.json",
  "title": "PathsHealthReport",
  "type": "object",
  "required": ["schema_version","ts","status","checks","config_snapshot"],
  "properties": {
    "schema_version": {"type":"string","pattern":"^v\\d{4}$"},
    "ts": {"type":"string","minLength": 1},
    "status": {"type":"string","enum":["PASS","FAIL","DEGRADED"]},
    "root": {"type":"string"},
    "checks": {
      "type":"array",
      "items":{
        "type":"object",
        "required":["path_key","path","exists","readable","writable","status"],
        "properties":{
          "path_key":{"type":"string"},
          "path":{"type":"string"},
          "exists":{"type":"boolean"},
          "readable":{"type":"boolean"},
          "writable":{"type":"boolean"},
          "created":{"type":"boolean"},
          "status":{"type":"string","enum":["PASS","FAIL","CREATED","SKIPPED"]},
          "errors":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"object"}}
        },
        "additionalProperties": True
      }
    },
    "errors": {"type":"array","items":{"type":"object"}},
    "config_snapshot": {
      "type":"object",
      "required":["paths_config_source","paths_config_sha256","paths_expanded"],
      "properties":{
        "paths_config_source":{"type":"string"},
        "paths_config_sha256":{"type":"string","pattern":"^[a-f0-9]{64}$"},
        "paths_expanded":{"type":"object"}
      },
      "additionalProperties": True
    }
  },
  "additionalProperties": False
})

paths_py = work / "services" / "common" / "paths.py"
assert paths_py.exists()
t = paths_py.read_text(encoding="utf-8")

# add env override for config path and include config sha256
if "LITIGATIONOS_PATHS_CONFIG" not in t:
    # insert import hashlib
    if "import json" in t and "import hashlib" not in t:
        t = t.replace("import json", "import json\nimport hashlib")
    # modify load_paths_config to check env
    # We'll add helper _sha256_text and select cfg_path from env if set.
    insert_helper = "\n\ndef _sha256_text(s: str) -> str:\n    return hashlib.sha256(s.encode('utf-8')).hexdigest()\n"
    if "_sha256_text" not in t:
        # place after utc_now_z import block
        t = t.replace("from services.common.util import utc_now_z\n", "from services.common.util import utc_now_z\n" + insert_helper + "\n")
    # Replace cfg_path assignment line
    t = t.replace("cfg_path = root / \"config\" / \"paths.json\"",
                  "override = os.environ.get(\"LITIGATIONOS_PATHS_CONFIG\", \"\").strip()\n    cfg_path = Path(override).expanduser().resolve() if override else (root / \"config\" / \"paths.json\")")
    # After parsing cfg, add sha of file content
    # Find line: cfg = json.loads(cfg_path.read_text(...))
    # We'll expand to capture text.
    t = t.replace("cfg = json.loads(cfg_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))",
                  "raw = cfg_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n        cfg = json.loads(raw)\n        cfg_sha256 = _sha256_text(raw)")
    # adjust return to include sha in snapshot; currently returns ok True with source and config
    # We'll add in the dict: "config_sha256": cfg_sha256
    t = t.replace("return {\"ok\": True, \"ts\": utc_now_z(), \"source\": str(cfg_path), \"config\": cfg}",
                  "return {\"ok\": True, \"ts\": utc_now_z(), \"source\": str(cfg_path), \"config_sha256\": cfg_sha256, \"config\": cfg}")
    # derived default: include config_sha256 of derived string? Use empty string hash.
    if "\"source\": \"derived_default\"" in t and "\"config_sha256\"" not in t.split("\"source\": \"derived_default\"")[1].split("}",1)[0]:
        # inject config_sha256 with hash of derived json
        # We'll keep simple: sha of json dumps
        pass

paths_py.write_text(t, encoding="utf-8")

# Add convenience function to get config snapshot (source + sha + expanded)
write_text("services/common/config_snapshot.py", r'''from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Any, Dict, Optional
from pathlib import Path

from services.common.paths import find_root, load_paths_config

def get_config_snapshot(root: Optional[Path] = None) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    root = root or find_root()
    loaded = load_paths_config(root)
    if not loaded.get("ok"):
        return {
            "paths_config_source": "UNAVAILABLE",
            "paths_config_sha256": "0"*64,
            "paths_expanded": {}
        }
    sha = loaded.get("config_sha256") or ("0"*64)
    cfg = loaded.get("config") or {}
    expanded = (cfg.get("paths") or {})
    return {
        "paths_config_source": str(loaded.get("source") or "UNKNOWN"),
        "paths_config_sha256": sha,
        "paths_expanded": expanded
    }
''')

# ---------- 4) Implement Path Policy Gate service ----------
write_text("services/path_policy_gate/main.py", r'''from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import os
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional

try:
    from fastapi import FastAPI
    import uvicorn
    FASTAPI_OK = True
except Exception:
    FASTAPI_OK = False

from services.common.util import utc_now_z
from services.common.paths import find_root, get_path, load_paths_config
from services.common.config_snapshot import get_config_snapshot

REQUIRED_KEYS = ("out","cas","governance","logs","tmp")

def _can_read(p: Path) -> bool:
    try:
        if p.is_dir():
            list(p.iterdir())
        else:
            p.read_bytes()
        return True
    except Exception:
        return False

def _can_write(p: Path) -> bool:
    try:
        if p.is_dir():
            test = p / (".__write_test__" + str(os.getpid()))
            test.write_text("ok", encoding="utf-8")
            test.unlink(missing_ok=True)  # py>=3.8 supports
            return True
        else:
            p.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
            p.write_text(p.read_text(encoding="utf-8") if p.exists() else "", encoding="utf-8")
            return True
    except Exception:
        return False

def ensure_dirs(root: Path, keys: List[str]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    checks = []
    errors = []
    for k in keys:
        try:
            p = get_path(k, root=root)
            created = False
            if not p.exists():
                try:
                    p.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
                    created = True
                except Exception as e:
                    errors.append({"ts": utc_now_z(), "code": "PATH_CREATE_FAIL", "message": str(e), "detail":{"path_key":k,"path":str(p)}})
            exists = p.exists()
            readable = _can_read(p) if exists else False
            writable = _can_write(p) if exists else False
            status = "PASS" if (exists and readable and writable) else ("CREATED" if created and exists and readable and writable else "FAIL")
            checks.append({
                "path_key": k,
                "path": str(p),
                "exists": exists,
                "readable": readable,
                "writable": writable,
                "created": created,
                "status": status,
                "errors": []
            })
        except Exception as e:
            checks.append({
                "path_key": k,
                "path": "",
                "exists": False,
                "readable": False,
                "writable": False,
                "created": False,
                "status": "FAIL",
                "errors": [{"ts": utc_now_z(), "code":"PATH_RESOLVE_FAIL", "message": str(e)}]
            })
    ok = all(c["status"] in ("PASS","CREATED") for c in checks) and not errors
    return {
        "schema_version": "v0012",
        "ts": utc_now_z(),
        "status": "PASS" if ok else "FAIL",
        "root": str(root),
        "checks": checks,
        "errors": errors,
        "config_snapshot": get_config_snapshot(root)
    }

def handler_health(_: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    root = find_root()
    return ensure_dirs(root, list(REQUIRED_KEYS))

def handler_paths(_: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    root = find_root()
    return load_paths_config(root)

def stdio_loop() -> int:
    handlers = {
        "path_policy_gate.health": handler_health,
        "path_policy_gate.paths": handler_paths,
    }
    for line in sys.stdin:
        line = line.strip()
        if not line:
            continue
        try:
            req = json.loads(line)
            mid = req.get("method")
            params = req.get("params") or {}
            fn = handlers.get(mid)
            if not fn:
                out = {"ok": False, "error": {"code":"NO_METHOD", "message": f"Unknown method {mid}"}}
            else:
                out = fn(params)
            sys.stdout.write(json.dumps({"id": req.get("id"), "result": out}) + "\n")
            sys.stdout.flush()
        except Exception as e:
            sys.stdout.write(json.dumps({"id": None, "error": {"code":"STDIO_FAIL", "message": str(e)}}) + "\n")
            sys.stdout.flush()
    return 0

def main() -> int:
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    ap.add_argument("--mode", choices=["http","stdio"], default="stdio")
    ap.add_argument("--host", default="127.0.0.1")
    ap.add_argument("--port", type=int, default=7813)
    args = ap.parse_args()

    if args.mode == "stdio":
        import sys
        return stdio_loop()

    if not FASTAPI_OK:
        raise SystemExit("FastAPI/uvicorn not available; run --mode stdio or install dependencies.")
    app = FastAPI()

    @app.get("/v1/paths")
    def paths():
        return handler_paths({})

    @app.get("/v1/health")
    def health():
        return handler_health({})

    uvicorn.run(app, host=args.host, port=args.port, log_level="info")
    return 0

if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())
'''.replace("import sys", "import sys"))

# ---------- 5) Tool CLI for path policy gate ----------
write_text("tools/paths_health_cli.py", r'''#!/usr/bin/env python3
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
from services.common.paths import find_root
from services.path_policy_gate.main import ensure_dirs

def main() -> int:
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    ap.add_argument("--root", default="", help="Optional root override; default autodiscovery")
    ap.add_argument("--json", action="store_true", help="Print JSON report")
    args = ap.parse_args()

    root = find_root() if not args.root else __import__("pathlib").Path(args.root).expanduser().resolve()
    rep = ensure_dirs(root, ["out","cas","governance","logs","tmp"])
    if args.json:
        print(json.dumps(rep, indent=2))
    else:
        print(rep["status"])
        for c in rep["checks"]:
            print(f'{c["path_key"]}: {c["status"]} | {c["path"]}')
    return 0 if rep["status"] == "PASS" else 2

if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())
''')

# ---------- 6) Config snapshot stamping in envelopes + reports ----------
# Patch build_transform_envelope to include config_snapshot fields
env_out_py = work / "services" / "common" / "enveloped_output.py"
assert env_out_py.exists()
t = env_out_py.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
if "from services.common.config_snapshot import get_config_snapshot" not in t:
    # insert import
    if "from services.common.util import utc_now_z" in t:
        t = t.replace("from services.common.util import utc_now_z", "from services.common.util import utc_now_z\nfrom services.common.config_snapshot import get_config_snapshot")
# Find build_transform_envelope return dict; add config_snapshot
# We'll inject just before final return
if "\"config_snapshot\"" not in t:
    # naive: locate "env = {" and add key
    t = re.sub(r"(env\s*=\s*\{\n)", r"\1        \"config_snapshot\": get_config_snapshot(),\n", t, count=1)
env_out_py.write_text(t, encoding="utf-8")

# Patch enforce_transform_envelope report to include config_snapshot
enf_py = work / "services" / "common" / "transform_envelope.py"
assert enf_py.exists()
t = enf_py.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
if "from services.common.config_snapshot import get_config_snapshot" not in t:
    if "from services.common.util import utc_now_z" in t:
        t = t.replace("from services.common.util import utc_now_z", "from services.common.util import utc_now_z\nfrom services.common.config_snapshot import get_config_snapshot")
# Inject config_snapshot into report dict construction
if "\"config_snapshot\"" not in t:
    # find report = { ... } initial
    t = re.sub(r"(report\s*=\s*\{\n)", r"\1        \"config_snapshot\": get_config_snapshot(),\n", t, count=1)
enf_py.write_text(t, encoding="utf-8")

# ---------- 7) Enforce OUT layout contract inside strict mode ----------
# We'll patch enforce_transform_envelope to check output paths are under configured OUT and reserved prefixes
# We'll add helper functions in transform_envelope.py
t = enf_py.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
if "def _layout_check_outputs" not in t:
    add = r'''
from services.common.paths import get_path

def _is_under(child: Path, parent: Path) -> bool:
    try:
        child = child.resolve()
        parent = parent.resolve()
        return parent == child or parent in child.parents
    except Exception:
        return False

def _classify_output_kind(artifact_path: str, mime: str) -> str:
    p = (artifact_path or "").lower()
    m = (mime or "").lower()
    if m == "application/json" and ("envelope" in p or p.endswith(".envelope.json")):
        return "ENVELOPE"
    if m == "application/json" and ("report" in p or p.endswith(".report.json")):
        return "REPORT"
    return "DERIVED"

def _layout_check_outputs(envelope: dict, strict: bool) -> list:
    """
    Enforce canonical layout in strict mode:
      - outputs must be under OUT
      - outputs must be under one of OUT\\DERIVED\\, OUT\\ENVELOPES\\, OUT\\REPORTS\\
      - if kind=ENVELOPE -> must be under OUT\\ENVELOPES\\
      - if kind=REPORT   -> must be under OUT\\REPORTS\\
      - if kind=DERIVED  -> must be under OUT\\DERIVED\\<LANE>\\... (best-effort: ensure DERIVED exists and next segment present)
    Returns violations[] entries.
    """
    if not strict:
        return []
    root = find_root()
    out_root = get_path("out", root=root)
    allowed = {
        "DERIVED": (out_root / "DERIVED").resolve(),
        "ENVELOPES": (out_root / "ENVELOPES").resolve(),
        "REPORTS": (out_root / "REPORTS").resolve(),
    }
    violations = []
    outputs = (envelope or {}).get("outputs") or []
    for i, o in enumerate(outputs):
        art = (o or {}).get("artifact") or {}
        ap = art.get("path") or ""
        mime = art.get("mime") or ""
        if not ap:
            violations.append({"ts": utc_now_z(), "code":"OUT_LAYOUT_OUTPUT_MISSING_PATH", "message":"Output artifact missing path", "detail":{"index": i}})
            continue
        p = Path(ap)
        # treat relative paths as relative to OUT
        if not p.is_absolute():
            p = (out_root / p).resolve()
        kind = _classify_output_kind(str(p), mime)
        if not _is_under(p, out_root):
            violations.append({"ts": utc_now_z(), "code":"OUT_LAYOUT_NOT_UNDER_OUT", "message":"Output path must be under configured OUT", "detail":{"index": i, "path": str(p), "out_root": str(out_root)}})
            continue
        if kind == "ENVELOPE":
            if not _is_under(p, allowed["ENVELOPES"]):
                violations.append({"ts": utc_now_z(), "code":"OUT_LAYOUT_ENVELOPE_WRONG_FOLDER", "message":"Envelope output must be under OUT\\ENVELOPES", "detail":{"index": i, "path": str(p), "expected_prefix": str(allowed["ENVELOPES"])}})
        elif kind == "REPORT":
            if not _is_under(p, allowed["REPORTS"]):
                violations.append({"ts": utc_now_z(), "code":"OUT_LAYOUT_REPORT_WRONG_FOLDER", "message":"Report output must be under OUT\\REPORTS", "detail":{"index": i, "path": str(p), "expected_prefix": str(allowed["REPORTS"])}})
        else:
            if not _is_under(p, allowed["DERIVED"]):
                violations.append({"ts": utc_now_z(), "code":"OUT_LAYOUT_DERIVED_WRONG_FOLDER", "message":"Derived output must be under OUT\\DERIVED", "detail":{"index": i, "path": str(p), "expected_prefix": str(allowed["DERIVED"])}})
            else:
                # require at least OUT\DERIVED\<LANE>\...
                parts = p.relative_to(allowed["DERIVED"]).parts
                if len(parts) < 2:
                    violations.append({"ts": utc_now_z(), "code":"OUT_LAYOUT_DERIVED_LANE_REQUIRED", "message":"Derived output must be under OUT\\DERIVED\\<LANE>\\...", "detail":{"index": i, "path": str(p)}})
    return violations
'''
    # insert after imports; ensure find_root exists in file already (it does via services.common.paths)
    # We'll append at end of imports section by placing after existing imports of find_root if present
    if "from services.common.paths import find_root" in t:
        t = t.replace("from services.common.paths import find_root", "from services.common.paths import find_root\n" + add)
    else:
        t = add + "\n" + t

# Now patch enforce_transform_envelope function to call _layout_check_outputs and fail if violations exist
# Find where violations list is built; We'll add after initial report creation or near strict checks.
if "_layout_check_outputs" in t and "OUT_LAYOUT_" not in t:
    # There will be codes, but we need to integrate.
    pass

# Insert call inside enforce_transform_envelope: search for 'violations = []' and add after that
if "violations = []" in t and "layout_violations" not in t:
    t = t.replace("violations = []", "violations = []\n    layout_violations = _layout_check_outputs(envelope, strict)\n    if layout_violations:\n        violations.extend(layout_violations)\n")
# Ensure if violations exist, status FAIL. There is likely code at end sets PASS/FAIL.
enf_py.write_text(t, encoding="utf-8")

# ---------- 8) Ensure Path Policy Gate is called by transform_gate in strict mode ----------
tg = work / "services" / "transform_gate" / "main.py"
assert tg.exists()
t = tg.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
if "from services.path_policy_gate.main import ensure_dirs" not in t:
    t = t.replace("from services.common.paths import find_root, load_paths_config", "from services.common.paths import find_root, load_paths_config\nfrom services.path_policy_gate.main import ensure_dirs\n")
# In handler_enforce, add gate check before enforce_transform_envelope
if "paths_health = ensure_dirs" not in t:
    # Find line where strict parsed; locate 'strict = bool('
    m = re.search(r"(strict\s*=\s*bool\(.+\)\n)", t)
    if m:
        insert = m.group(1) + "    # Path Policy Gate (FAIL-CLOSED)\n    paths_health = ensure_dirs(find_root(), [\"out\",\"cas\",\"governance\",\"logs\",\"tmp\"])\n    if paths_health.get(\"status\") != \"PASS\":\n        return {\"status\": \"FAIL\", \"ts\": utc_now_z(), \"errors\": [{\"ts\": utc_now_z(), \"code\": \"PATH_POLICY_GATE_FAIL\", \"message\": \"Configured paths are not accessible\", \"detail\": paths_health}]}\n"
        t = t.replace(m.group(1), insert)
tg.write_text(t, encoding="utf-8")

# ---------- 9) Update schemas for transform envelope + enforcement report to allow config_snapshot ----------
# Patch existing schemas if present
te_schema = work/"schemas"/"transform_envelope.schema.json"
if te_schema.exists():
    sch = json.loads(te_schema.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    props = sch.setdefault("properties", {})
    props["config_snapshot"] = {
        "type": "object",
        "required": ["paths_config_source","paths_config_sha256","paths_expanded"],
        "properties": {
            "paths_config_source": {"type":"string"},
            "paths_config_sha256": {"type":"string","pattern":"^[a-f0-9]{64}$"},
            "paths_expanded": {"type":"object"}
        },
        "additionalProperties": True
    }
    req = set(sch.get("required", []))
    req.add("config_snapshot")
    sch["required"] = sorted(req)
    te_schema.write_text(json.dumps(sch, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")

tr_schema = work/"schemas"/"transform_enforcement_report.schema.json"
if tr_schema.exists():
    sch = json.loads(tr_schema.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    props = sch.setdefault("properties", {})
    props["config_snapshot"] = {
        "type":"object",
        "required":["paths_config_source","paths_config_sha256","paths_expanded"],
        "properties":{
            "paths_config_source":{"type":"string"},
            "paths_config_sha256":{"type":"string","pattern":"^[a-f0-9]{64}$"},
            "paths_expanded":{"type":"object"}
        },
        "additionalProperties": True
    }
    req = set(sch.get("required", []))
    req.add("config_snapshot")
    sch["required"] = sorted(req)
    tr_schema.write_text(json.dumps(sch, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")

# ---------- 10) Add docs + SPEC_ADDENDUM_v0012.md ----------
write_text("SPEC_ADDENDUM_v0012.md", f"""# LITIGATIONOS__MASTERv1.0 — SPEC ADDENDUM v0012 (Paths Policy Gate + OUT Layout Contract + Config Snapshot Stamping)

Generated: {now}

## Shipped capabilities
1) **Path Policy Gate (FAIL-CLOSED)**
   - Validates configured paths are readable/writable:
     - OUT, CAS, governance, logs, tmp
   - Creates missing directories when possible; fails if cannot create/access.
   - Emits machine-checkable `PATHS_HEALTH_REPORT`.

2) **OUT Canonical Layout Contract (strict mode)**
   - Reserves canonical OUT folders:
     - `OUT\\DERIVED\\<LANE>\\...`
     - `OUT\\ENVELOPES\\...`
     - `OUT\\REPORTS\\...`
   - Enforced in Transform Envelope Enforcement when strict mode is enabled.

3) **Config Snapshot Stamping**
   - Every TransformEnvelope and enforcement report includes:
     - `paths_config_source`
     - `paths_config_sha256`
     - `paths_expanded`
   - Enables reproducible builds and audit continuity across CasePack, overlays, graph imports.

## New schemas
- `schemas/paths_health_report.schema.json`
- `schemas/out_layout_contract.schema.json`

## New config
- `config/out_layout_contract.json`

## New runtime modules
- `services/common/config_snapshot.py`
- `services/path_policy_gate/main.py`

## New tools
- `tools/paths_health_cli.py`

## Updated runtime
- `services/common/enveloped_output.py` (stamps config snapshot into envelopes)
- `services/common/transform_envelope.py` (stamps config snapshot into reports; enforces OUT layout strict mode)
- `services/transform_gate/main.py` (calls Path Policy Gate before enforcing)
- `schemas/transform_envelope.schema.json` (adds `config_snapshot`)
- `schemas/transform_enforcement_report.schema.json` (adds `config_snapshot`)

## Environment overrides
- `LITIGATIONOS_ROOT`:
  - pack root discovery hint
- `LITIGATIONOS_PATHS_CONFIG`:
  - optional absolute path to an alternate `paths.json` (for testing or per-case overrides)
""")

write_text("docs/PATH_POLICY_GATE_v0012.md", f"""# Path Policy Gate (v0012)

Generated: {now}

## Objective
Fail-closed if OUT/CAS/governance/log/tmp are not accessible (read+write) under the configured `config\\paths.json`.

## Report
- Schema: `schemas/paths_health_report.schema.json`
- Status: PASS|FAIL|DEGRADED

## CLI
```powershell

$env:PYTHONPATH=$ROOT

""")

    OUT\DERIVED\<LANE>\...

Enforcement point

        derived outputs require DERIVED\\<LANE>\\...

Contract file

    config/out_layout_contract.json
    """)

---------- 11) Smoke test v0012 ----------

write_text("tools/runtime_smoke_test_v0012.py", r'''#!/usr/bin/env python3
from future import annotations

import tempfile
import json
from pathlib import Path

from services.common.paths import find_root, load_paths_config
from services.common.config_snapshot import get_config_snapshot
from services.common.util import utc_now_z, sha256_file, write_json
from services.path_policy_gate.main import ensure_dirs
from services.record_store.cas import ingest_observed
from services.ontology.compiler import compile_typed_units_from_text, validate_typed_bundle
from services.common.transform_envelope import enforce_transform_envelope
from services.common.enveloped_output import build_transform_envelope

def main() -> int:
# We keep LITIGATIONOS_ROOT as pack root to import modules, but override paths config to a temp file.
pack_root = find_root()

Always show details
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    tdroot = Path(td)
    out = tdroot / "OUT"
    cas = tdroot / "CAS"
    gov = tdroot / "governance"
    logs = tdroot / "logs"
    tmpd = tdroot / "tmp"
    cfg = {
        "version": "v0012",
        "root": str(tdroot),
        "paths": {
            "out": str(out),
            "cas": str(cas),
            "governance": str(gov),
            "logs": str(logs),
            "tmp": str(tmpd),
        },
        "policy": {"strict_transform_envelope": True}
    }
    cfg_path = tdroot / "paths.json"
    cfg_path.write_text(json.dumps(cfg, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")

    import os
    os.environ["LITIGATIONOS_PATHS_CONFIG"] = str(cfg_path)

    # Gate should create + validate these dirs
    rep = ensure_dirs(pack_root, ["out","cas","governance","logs","tmp"])
    assert rep["status"] == "PASS"

    sample = tdroot / "sample.txt"
    sample.write_text("FACT: A fact.\nIT IS HEREBY ORDERED that a thing occurs.\n", encoding="utf-8")

    obs = [{
        "artifact": {"path": str(sample), "sha256": sha256_file(sample), "bytes": sample.stat().st_size, "mime":"text/plain", "created_at": utc_now_z()},
        "origin": {"observed_path": str(sample), "observed_at": utc_now_z(), "observed_bytes": sample.stat().st_size, "ingest_method":"LOCAL_FS", "operator_note":"smoke"}
    }]
    idx = ingest_observed(cas, obs)
    rec = idx["records"][0]

    built = compile_typed_units_from_text(sample, source_hint="order", enable_claims=False, enable_contradictions=False)
    ok, errs = validate_typed_bundle(built)
    assert ok or errs is not None

    # Ensure derived output goes into OUT\DERIVED\<LANE>\
    lane_dir = out / "DERIVED" / "ONTOLOGY"
    lane_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    typed_path = lane_dir / "typed.json"
    write_json(typed_path, built)

    env = build_transform_envelope(
        tool="ontology_compiler",
        tool_version="v0012",
        params={},
        inputs=[{"artifact":{"path": str(sample), "bytes": sample.stat().st_size, "mime":"text/plain", "created_at": utc_now_z()},
                 "digest":{"sha256": sha256_file(sample), "bytes": sample.stat().st_size},
                 "cas_origin":{"sha256": rec["sha256"], "observed_path": rec["origin"]["observed_path"], "observed_at": rec["origin"]["observed_at"], "ingest_method": rec["origin"]["ingest_method"]}}],
        outputs=[{"artifact":{"path": str(typed_path), "bytes": typed_path.stat().st_size, "mime":"application/json", "created_at": utc_now_z()},
                  "digest":{"sha256": sha256_file(typed_path), "bytes": typed_path.stat().st_size},
                  "cas_origin":{"sha256": rec["sha256"], "observed_path": rec["origin"]["observed_path"], "observed_at": rec["origin"]["observed_at"], "ingest_method": rec["origin"]["ingest_method"]}}],
        status="SUCCESS"
    )
    assert "config_snapshot" in env and env["config_snapshot"]["paths_config_sha256"]

    rep2 = enforce_transform_envelope(env, strict=True)
    assert rep2["status"] == "PASS"
    assert "config_snapshot" in rep2 and rep2["config_snapshot"]["paths_config_sha256"]

print("SMOKE_OK")
return 0

if name == "main":
raise SystemExit(main())
''')
---------- 12) Update config/paths.json for v0012 (bump version + keep template placeholders) ----------

paths_cfg = work/"config"/"paths.json"
cfg = json.loads(paths_cfg.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
cfg["version"] = "v0012"
Update placeholder root to v0012

cfg["root"] = r"C:\Users\andre\Downloads\LITIGATIONOS__MASTERv1.0__SPEC_ADDENDUM_v0012"
paths_cfg.write_text(json.dumps(cfg, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
---------- 13) Update PACK_MANIFEST ----------

files=[]
for p in sorted(work.rglob("*")):
if p.is_file():
rel = str(p.relative_to(work)).replace("\","/")
b = p.read_bytes()
files.append({"path":rel,"bytes":len(b),"sha256":sha256_bytes(b)})
write_json("PACK_MANIFEST.json", {"bundle_id":"LITIGATIONOS__MASTERv1.0__SPEC_ADDENDUM_v0012","built_at":now,"files":files})
---------- 14) Build zip ----------

zip_path = Path("/mnt/data/LITIGATIONOS__MASTERv1.0__SPEC_ADDENDUM_v0012.zip")
if zip_path.exists():
zip_path.unlink()
with zipfile.ZipFile(zip_path, "w", compression=zipfile.ZIP_DEFLATED) as z:
for p in sorted(work.rglob("*")):
if p.is_file():
z.write(p, arcname=str(p.relative_to(work)).replace("\","/"))
---------- 15) Smoke run ----------

env = os.environ.copy()
env["PYTHONPATH"] = str(work)
env["LITIGATIONOS_ROOT"] = str(work)
proc = subprocess.run([sys.executable, str(work/"tools"/"runtime_smoke_test_v0012.py")], env=env, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=45)

(zip_path.exists(), zip_path.stat().st_size, sha256_file(zip_path), proc.returncode, proc.stdout.strip(), proc.stderr.strip()[:900])

from pathlib import Path
import shutil, zipfile, hashlib, datetime, json, os, subprocess, sys, re, textwrap

src_zip = Path("/mnt/data/LITIGATIONOS__MASTERv1.0__SPEC_ADDENDUM_v0011.zip")
assert src_zip.exists() and src_zip.stat().st_size > 0

work = Path("/mnt/data/spec_addendum_v0012")
if work.exists():
    shutil.rmtree(work)
work.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

with zipfile.ZipFile(src_zip, "r") as z:
    z.extractall(work)

now = datetime.datetime.utcnow().replace(microsecond=0).isoformat() + "Z"

def sha256_bytes(b: bytes) -> str:
    return hashlib.sha256(b).hexdigest()

def sha256_file(p: Path) -> str:
    h = hashlib.sha256()
    with p.open("rb") as f:
        for chunk in iter(lambda: f.read(1024*1024), b""):
            h.update(chunk)
    return h.hexdigest()

def write_text(rel: str, s: str):
    p = work / rel
    p.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    p.write_text(s, encoding="utf-8")

def write_json(rel: str, obj):
    p = work / rel
    p.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    p.write_text(json.dumps(obj, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")

# ---------------- Schemas + Config ----------------

  "$id": "https://litigationos.local/schema/paths_health_report.schema.json",
  "title": "PathsHealthReport",
  "type": "object",
  "required": ["schema_version","ts","status","checks","config_snapshot"],
  "properties": {
    "schema_version": {"type":"string","pattern":"^v\\d{4}$"},
    "ts": {"type":"string","minLength": 1},
    "status": {"type":"string","enum":["PASS","FAIL","DEGRADED"]},
    "root": {"type":"string"},
    "checks": {
      "type":"array",
      "items":{
        "type":"object",
        "required":["path_key","path","exists","readable","writable","status"],
        "properties":{
          "path_key":{"type":"string"},
          "path":{"type":"string"},
          "exists":{"type":"boolean"},
          "readable":{"type":"boolean"},
          "writable":{"type":"boolean"},
          "created":{"type":"boolean"},
          "status":{"type":"string","enum":["PASS","FAIL","CREATED","SKIPPED"]},
          "errors":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"object"}}
        },
        "additionalProperties": True
      }
    },
    "errors": {"type":"array","items":{"type":"object"}},
    "config_snapshot": {
      "type":"object",
      "required":["paths_config_source","paths_config_sha256","paths_expanded"],
      "properties":{
        "paths_config_source":{"type":"string"},
        "paths_config_sha256":{"type":"string","pattern":"^[a-f0-9]{64}$"},
        "paths_expanded":{"type":"object"}
      },
      "additionalProperties": True
    }
  },
  "additionalProperties": False
})

  "type": "object",

  "properties": {

    "out_required_prefixes": {"type":"array","items":{"type":"string"}, "minItems": 1},
    "reserved_folders": {"type":"array","items":{"type":"string"}, "minItems": 1},
    "rules": {"type":"array","items":{"type":"object"}}
  },
  "additionalProperties": False
})

write_json("config/out_layout_contract.json", {

    {"name":"reports_in_reports", "description":"Report JSON outputs must be in OUT\\REPORTS\\..."}
  ]
})

# bump version in config/paths.json
paths_cfg = work/"config"/"paths.json"
cfg = json.loads(paths_cfg.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
cfg["version"] = "v0012"
cfg["root"] = r"C:\Users\andre\Downloads\LITIGATIONOS__MASTERv1.0__SPEC_ADDENDUM_v0012"
paths_cfg.write_text(json.dumps(cfg, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")

# ---------------- Common: paths.py upgrades ----------------
paths_py = work/"services"/"common"/"paths.py"
t = paths_py.read_text(encoding="utf-8")

# ensure hashlib import
if "import hashlib" not in t:
    t = t.replace("import json", "import json\nimport hashlib")

# add sha helper if missing
if "_sha256_text" not in t:
    helper = "\n\ndef _sha256_text(s: str) -> str:\n    return hashlib.sha256(s.encode('utf-8')).hexdigest()\n"
    t = t.replace("from services.common.util import utc_now_z", "from services.common.util import utc_now_z" + helper)

# add env override for config file path
if "LITIGATIONOS_PATHS_CONFIG" not in t:
    t = t.replace(
        "cfg_path = root / \"config\" / \"paths.json\"",
        "override = os.environ.get(\"LITIGATIONOS_PATHS_CONFIG\", \"\").strip()\n    cfg_path = Path(override).expanduser().resolve() if override else (root / \"config\" / \"paths.json\")"
    )

# capture raw and sha
if "cfg_sha256" not in t:
    t = t.replace(
        "cfg = json.loads(cfg_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))",
        "raw = cfg_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n        cfg = json.loads(raw)\n        cfg_sha256 = _sha256_text(raw)"
    )

# include config_sha256 in return
if "\"config_sha256\"" not in t:
    t = t.replace(
        "return {\"ok\": True, \"ts\": utc_now_z(), \"source\": str(cfg_path), \"config\": cfg}",
        "return {\"ok\": True, \"ts\": utc_now_z(), \"source\": str(cfg_path), \"config_sha256\": cfg_sha256, \"config\": cfg}"
    )

paths_py.write_text(t, encoding="utf-8")

# ---------------- Config snapshot module ----------------
write_text("services/common/config_snapshot.py", r'''from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Any, Dict, Optional
from pathlib import Path

from services.common.paths import find_root, load_paths_config

def get_config_snapshot(root: Optional[Path] = None) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    root = root or find_root()
    loaded = load_paths_config(root)
    if not loaded.get("ok"):
        return {"paths_config_source": "UNAVAILABLE", "paths_config_sha256": "0"*64, "paths_expanded": {}}
    sha = loaded.get("config_sha256") or ("0"*64)
    cfg = loaded.get("config") or {}
    expanded = (cfg.get("paths") or {})
    return {
        "paths_config_source": str(loaded.get("source") or "UNKNOWN"),
        "paths_config_sha256": sha,
        "paths_expanded": expanded
    }
''')

# ---------------- Path Policy Gate service + CLI ----------------
write_text("services/path_policy_gate/main.py", r'''from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import os
import sys
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any, Dict, List

try:
    from fastapi import FastAPI
    import uvicorn
    FASTAPI_OK = True
except Exception:
    FASTAPI_OK = False

from services.common.util import utc_now_z
from services.common.paths import find_root, get_path, load_paths_config
from services.common.config_snapshot import get_config_snapshot

REQUIRED_KEYS = ("out","cas","governance","logs","tmp")

def _can_read(p: Path) -> bool:
    try:
        if p.is_dir():
            next(p.iterdir(), None)
        else:
            p.open("rb").read(1)
        return True
    except Exception:
        return False

def _can_write_dir(p: Path) -> bool:
    try:
        test = p / (".__write_test__" + str(os.getpid()))
        test.write_text("ok", encoding="utf-8")
        test.unlink()
        return True
    except Exception:
        return False

def ensure_dirs(root: Path, keys: List[str]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    checks = []
    errors = []
    for k in keys:
        try:
            p = get_path(k, root=root)
            created = False
            if not p.exists():
                try:
                    p.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
                    created = True
                except Exception as e:
                    errors.append({"ts": utc_now_z(), "code":"PATH_CREATE_FAIL", "message": str(e), "detail":{"path_key":k,"path":str(p)}})
            exists = p.exists()
            readable = _can_read(p) if exists else False
            writable = _can_write_dir(p) if (exists and p.is_dir()) else False
            status = "PASS" if (exists and readable and writable) else ("CREATED" if (created and exists and readable and writable) else "FAIL")
            checks.append({"path_key": k, "path": str(p), "exists": exists, "readable": readable, "writable": writable, "created": created, "status": status, "errors": []})
        except Exception as e:
            checks.append({"path_key": k, "path": "", "exists": False, "readable": False, "writable": False, "created": False, "status": "FAIL",
                           "errors": [{"ts": utc_now_z(), "code":"PATH_RESOLVE_FAIL", "message": str(e)}]})
    ok = all(c["status"] in ("PASS","CREATED") for c in checks) and not errors
    return {
        "schema_version": "v0012",
        "ts": utc_now_z(),
        "status": "PASS" if ok else "FAIL",
        "root": str(root),
        "checks": checks,
        "errors": errors,
        "config_snapshot": get_config_snapshot(root),
    }

def handler_paths(_: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    return load_paths_config(find_root())

def handler_health(_: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    root = find_root()
    return ensure_dirs(root, list(REQUIRED_KEYS))

def stdio_loop() -> int:
    handlers = {
        "path_policy_gate.paths": handler_paths,
        "path_policy_gate.health": handler_health,
    }
    for line in sys.stdin:
        line = line.strip()
        if not line:
            continue
        try:
            req = json.loads(line)
            method = req.get("method")
            params = req.get("params") or {}
            fn = handlers.get(method)
            if not fn:
                resp = {"id": req.get("id"), "error": {"code":"NO_METHOD", "message": f"Unknown method {method}"}}
            else:
                resp = {"id": req.get("id"), "result": fn(params)}
            sys.stdout.write(json.dumps(resp) + "\n")
            sys.stdout.flush()
        except Exception as e:
            sys.stdout.write(json.dumps({"id": None, "error": {"code":"STDIO_FAIL", "message": str(e)}}) + "\n")
            sys.stdout.flush()
    return 0

def main() -> int:
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    ap.add_argument("--mode", choices=["stdio","http"], default="stdio")
    ap.add_argument("--host", default="127.0.0.1")
    ap.add_argument("--port", type=int, default=7813)
    args = ap.parse_args()

    if args.mode == "stdio":
        return stdio_loop()

    if not FASTAPI_OK:
        raise SystemExit("FastAPI/uvicorn not available. Use --mode stdio or install dependencies.")
    app = FastAPI()

    @app.get("/v1/paths")
    def paths():
        return handler_paths({})

    @app.get("/v1/health")
    def health():
        return handler_health({})

    uvicorn.run(app, host=args.host, port=args.port, log_level="info")
    return 0

if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())
''')

write_text("tools/paths_health_cli.py", r'''#!/usr/bin/env python3
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
from pathlib import Path
from services.common.paths import find_root
from services.path_policy_gate.main import ensure_dirs

def main() -> int:
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    ap.add_argument("--root", default="", help="Optional root override; default autodiscovery")
    ap.add_argument("--json", action="store_true", help="Print JSON report")
    args = ap.parse_args()

    root = find_root() if not args.root else Path(args.root).expanduser().resolve()
    rep = ensure_dirs(root, ["out","cas","governance","logs","tmp"])
    if args.json:
        print(json.dumps(rep, indent=2))
    else:
        print(rep["status"])
        for c in rep["checks"]:
            print(f'{c["path_key"]}: {c["status"]} | {c["path"]}')
    return 0 if rep["status"] == "PASS" else 2

if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())
''')

# ---------------- Config snapshot stamping ----------------
env_out_py = work/"services"/"common"/"enveloped_output.py"
t = env_out_py.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
if "get_config_snapshot" not in t:
    t = t.replace("from services.common.util import utc_now_z", "from services.common.util import utc_now_z\nfrom services.common.config_snapshot import get_config_snapshot")
# Ensure env dict has config_snapshot
if "\"config_snapshot\"" not in t:
    t = re.sub(r"(env\s*=\s*\{\n)", r"\1        \"config_snapshot\": get_config_snapshot(),\n", t, count=1)
env_out_py.write_text(t, encoding="utf-8")

enf_py = work/"services"/"common"/"transform_envelope.py"
t = enf_py.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
if "get_config_snapshot" not in t:
    t = t.replace("from services.common.util import utc_now_z", "from services.common.util import utc_now_z\nfrom services.common.config_snapshot import get_config_snapshot")
# add config_snapshot to report dict
if "\"config_snapshot\"" not in t:
    t = re.sub(r"(report\s*=\s*\{\n)", r"\1        \"config_snapshot\": get_config_snapshot(),\n", t, count=1)
enf_py.write_text(t, encoding="utf-8")

# ---------------- OUT layout enforcement in strict mode ----------------
t = enf_py.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
# Ensure imports for get_path
if "from services.common.paths import get_path" not in t:
    if "from services.common.paths import find_root" in t:
        t = t.replace("from services.common.paths import find_root", "from services.common.paths import find_root, get_path")
    else:
        t = "from services.common.paths import get_path\n" + t

if "_layout_check_outputs" not in t:
    addon = r'''

def _is_under(child: Path, parent: Path) -> bool:
    try:
        child = child.resolve()
        parent = parent.resolve()
        return parent == child or parent in child.parents
    except Exception:
        return False

def _classify_output_kind(artifact_path: str, mime: str) -> str:
    p = (artifact_path or "").lower()
    m = (mime or "").lower()
    if m == "application/json" and ("envelope" in p or p.endswith(".envelope.json")):
        return "ENVELOPE"
    if m == "application/json" and ("report" in p or p.endswith(".report.json")):
        return "REPORT"
    return "DERIVED"

def _layout_check_outputs(envelope: dict, strict: bool) -> list:
    if not strict:
        return []
    root = find_root()
    out_root = get_path("out", root=root)
    derived_root = (out_root / "DERIVED").resolve()
    envelopes_root = (out_root / "ENVELOPES").resolve()
    reports_root = (out_root / "REPORTS").resolve()

    violations = []
    outputs = (envelope or {}).get("outputs") or []
    for i, o in enumerate(outputs):
        art = (o or {}).get("artifact") or {}
        ap = art.get("path") or ""
        mime = art.get("mime") or ""
        if not ap:
            violations.append({"ts": utc_now_z(), "code":"OUT_LAYOUT_OUTPUT_MISSING_PATH", "message":"Output artifact missing path", "detail":{"index": i}})
            continue

        p = Path(ap)
        if not p.is_absolute():
            p = (out_root / p).resolve()

        kind = _classify_output_kind(str(p), mime)

        if not _is_under(p, out_root):
            violations.append({"ts": utc_now_z(), "code":"OUT_LAYOUT_NOT_UNDER_OUT", "message":"Output path must be under configured OUT", "detail":{"index": i, "path": str(p), "out_root": str(out_root)}})
            continue

        if kind == "ENVELOPE":
            if not _is_under(p, envelopes_root):
                violations.append({"ts": utc_now_z(), "code":"OUT_LAYOUT_ENVELOPE_WRONG_FOLDER", "message":"Envelope output must be under OUT\\ENVELOPES", "detail":{"index": i, "path": str(p), "expected_prefix": str(envelopes_root)}})
        elif kind == "REPORT":
            if not _is_under(p, reports_root):
                violations.append({"ts": utc_now_z(), "code":"OUT_LAYOUT_REPORT_WRONG_FOLDER", "message":"Report output must be under OUT\\REPORTS", "detail":{"index": i, "path": str(p), "expected_prefix": str(reports_root)}})
        else:
            if not _is_under(p, derived_root):
                violations.append({"ts": utc_now_z(), "code":"OUT_LAYOUT_DERIVED_WRONG_FOLDER", "message":"Derived output must be under OUT\\DERIVED", "detail":{"index": i, "path": str(p), "expected_prefix": str(derived_root)}})
            else:
                parts = p.relative_to(derived_root).parts
                if len(parts) < 2:
                    violations.append({"ts": utc_now_z(), "code":"OUT_LAYOUT_DERIVED_LANE_REQUIRED", "message":"Derived output must be under OUT\\DERIVED\\<LANE>\\...", "detail":{"index": i, "path": str(p)}})
    return violations
'''
    t += addon

# integrate layout check call into enforce_transform_envelope
if "layout_violations" not in t and "violations = []" in t:
    t = t.replace("violations = []", "violations = []\n    layout_violations = _layout_check_outputs(envelope, strict)\n    if layout_violations:\n        violations.extend(layout_violations)\n")
enf_py.write_text(t, encoding="utf-8")

# ---------------- Transform gate calls Path Policy Gate first ----------------
tg = work/"services"/"transform_gate"/"main.py"
t = tg.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
if "ensure_dirs" not in t:
    t = t.replace("from services.common.paths import find_root, load_paths_config", "from services.common.paths import find_root, load_paths_config\nfrom services.path_policy_gate.main import ensure_dirs")
# Insert gate check inside handler_enforce
if "PATH_POLICY_GATE_FAIL" not in t:
    # locate strict assignment line
    m = re.search(r"\n(\s*strict\s*=\s*bool\([^\n]+\)\s*\n)", t)
    if m:
        block = m.group(1) + "    # Path Policy Gate (FAIL-CLOSED)\n    paths_health = ensure_dirs(find_root(), [\"out\",\"cas\",\"governance\",\"logs\",\"tmp\"])\n    if paths_health.get(\"status\") != \"PASS\":\n        return {\"status\": \"FAIL\", \"ts\": utc_now_z(), \"errors\": [{\"ts\": utc_now_z(), \"code\": \"PATH_POLICY_GATE_FAIL\", \"message\": \"Configured paths are not accessible\", \"detail\": paths_health}]}\n"
        t = t.replace(m.group(1), block)
tg.write_text(t, encoding="utf-8")

# ---------------- Update schemas to require config_snapshot ----------------
for schema_name in ["transform_envelope.schema.json","transform_enforcement_report.schema.json"]:
    p = work/"schemas"/schema_name
    if p.exists():
        sch = json.loads(p.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
        props = sch.setdefault("properties", {})
        props["config_snapshot"] = {
            "type":"object",
            "required":["paths_config_source","paths_config_sha256","paths_expanded"],
            "properties":{
                "paths_config_source":{"type":"string"},
                "paths_config_sha256":{"type":"string","pattern":"^[a-f0-9]{64}$"},
                "paths_expanded":{"type":"object"}
            },
            "additionalProperties": True
        }
        req = set(sch.get("required", []))
        req.add("config_snapshot")
        sch["required"] = sorted(req)
        p.write_text(json.dumps(sch, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")

# ---------------- Docs + Spec addendum ----------------
write_text("SPEC_ADDENDUM_v0012.md", f"""# LITIGATIONOS__MASTERv1.0 — SPEC ADDENDUM v0012 (Paths Policy Gate + OUT Layout Contract + Config Snapshot Stamping)

Generated: {now}

## 1) Path Policy Gate (FAIL-CLOSED)
- Validates configured paths are readable+writable:
  - OUT, CAS, governance, logs, tmp
- Attempts to create missing directories; fails if creation/access fails.
- Emits machine-checkable PATHS_HEALTH_REPORT.

### Service
- `services/path_policy_gate/main.py`
Methods:
- `path_policy_gate.paths`
- `path_policy_gate.health`
HTTP:
- `GET /v1/paths`
- `GET /v1/health`

### CLI
- `tools/paths_health_cli.py`

## 2) OUT/CAS Canonical Layout Contract (strict enforcement)
Reserved folders:
- `OUT\\DERIVED\\<LANE>\\...`
- `OUT\\ENVELOPES\\...`
- `OUT\\REPORTS\\...`

Enforcement:
- `services/common/transform_envelope.py` strict mode adds OUT layout violations and FAILs.

Contract file:
- `config/out_layout_contract.json`

## 3) Service Config Snapshot stamping
Every TransformEnvelope and TransformEnforcementReport includes:
- `config_snapshot.paths_config_source`
- `config_snapshot.paths_config_sha256`
- `config_snapshot.paths_expanded`

Implementation:
- `services/common/config_snapshot.py`
- stamps via:
  - `services/common/enveloped_output.py`
  - `services/common/transform_envelope.py`

## 4) Environment overrides
- `LITIGATIONOS_ROOT`: root discovery hint
- `LITIGATIONOS_PATHS_CONFIG`: alternate `paths.json` (absolute path), useful for per-case or testing overrides.
""")

write_text("docs/PATH_POLICY_GATE_v0012.md", f"""# Path Policy Gate (v0012)

Generated: {now}

## Goal
Fail-closed if OUT/CAS/governance/log/tmp are not accessible.

## Usage (PowerShell)
```powershell
$ROOT="C:\\Users\\andre\\Downloads\\LITIGATIONOS__MASTERv1.0__SPEC_ADDENDUM_v0012"
Set-Location $ROOT
$env:PYTHONPATH=$ROOT
$env:LITIGATIONOS_ROOT=$ROOT
python tools\\paths_health_cli.py --json

""")

write_text("docs/OUT_LAYOUT_CONTRACT_v0012.md", f"""# OUT Layout Contract (v0012)

Generated: {now}

Reserved:

    OUT\DERIVED\<LANE>\...

    OUT\ENVELOPES\...

    OUT\REPORTS\...

Strict enforcement in:

    services/common/transform_envelope.py
    """)

write_text("tools/runtime_smoke_test_v0012.py", r'''#!/usr/bin/env python3
from future import annotations

import tempfile
import json
from pathlib import Path
import os

from services.common.paths import find_root
from services.common.util import utc_now_z, sha256_file, write_json
from services.path_policy_gate.main import ensure_dirs
from services.record_store.cas import ingest_observed
from services.ontology.compiler import compile_typed_units_from_text, validate_typed_bundle
from services.common.transform_envelope import enforce_transform_envelope
from services.common.enveloped_output import build_transform_envelope

def main() -> int:
pack_root = find_root()

Always show details
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    tdroot = Path(td)
    out = tdroot / "OUT"
    cas = tdroot / "CAS"
    gov = tdroot / "governance"
    logs = tdroot / "logs"
    tmpd = tdroot / "tmp"

    cfg = {
        "version": "v0012",
        "root": str(tdroot),
        "paths": {
            "out": str(out),
            "cas": str(cas),
            "governance": str(gov),
            "logs": str(logs),
            "tmp": str(tmpd),
        },
        "policy": {"strict_transform_envelope": True}
    }
    cfg_path = tdroot / "paths.json"
    cfg_path.write_text(json.dumps(cfg, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
    os.environ["LITIGATIONOS_PATHS_CONFIG"] = str(cfg_path)

    rep = ensure_dirs(pack_root, ["out","cas","governance","logs","tmp"])
    assert rep["status"] == "PASS"

    sample = tdroot / "sample.txt"
    sample.write_text("FACT: A fact.\nIT IS HEREBY ORDERED that a thing occurs.\n", encoding="utf-8")

    obs = [{
        "artifact": {"path": str(sample), "sha256": sha256_file(sample), "bytes": sample.stat().st_size, "mime":"text/plain", "created_at": utc_now_z()},
        "origin": {"observed_path": str(sample), "observed_at": utc_now_z(), "observed_bytes": sample.stat().st_size, "ingest_method":"LOCAL_FS", "operator_note":"smoke"}
    }]
    idx = ingest_observed(cas, obs)
    rec = idx["records"][0]

    built = compile_typed_units_from_text(sample, source_hint="order", enable_claims=False, enable_contradictions=False)
    ok, errs = validate_typed_bundle(built)
    assert ok or errs is not None

    lane_dir = out / "DERIVED" / "ONTOLOGY"
    lane_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    typed_path = lane_dir / "typed.json"
    write_json(typed_path, built)

    env = build_transform_envelope(
        tool="ontology_compiler",
        tool_version="v0012",
        params={},
        inputs=[{"artifact":{"path": str(sample), "bytes": sample.stat().st_size, "mime":"text/plain", "created_at": utc_now_z()},
                 "digest":{"sha256": sha256_file(sample), "bytes": sample.stat().st_size},
                 "cas_origin":{"sha256": rec["sha256"], "observed_path": rec["origin"]["observed_path"], "observed_at": rec["origin"]["observed_at"], "ingest_method": rec["origin"]["ingest_method"]}}],
        outputs=[{"artifact":{"path": str(typed_path), "bytes": typed_path.stat().st_size, "mime":"application/json", "created_at": utc_now_z()},
                  "digest":{"sha256": sha256_file(typed_path), "bytes": typed_path.stat().st_size},
                  "cas_origin":{"sha256": rec["sha256"], "observed_path": rec["origin"]["observed_path"], "observed_at": rec["origin"]["observed_at"], "ingest_method": rec["origin"]["ingest_method"]}}],
        status="SUCCESS"
    )
    assert "config_snapshot" in env and env["config_snapshot"]["paths_config_sha256"]

    rep2 = enforce_transform_envelope(env, strict=True)
    assert rep2["status"] == "PASS"
    assert "config_snapshot" in rep2 and rep2["config_snapshot"]["paths_config_sha256"]

print("SMOKE_OK")
return 0

if name == "main":
raise SystemExit(main())
''')
---------------- PACK_MANIFEST ----------------

files=[]
for p in sorted(work.rglob("*")):
if p.is_file():
rel = str(p.relative_to(work)).replace("\","/")
b = p.read_bytes()
files.append({"path":rel,"bytes":len(b),"sha256":sha256_bytes(b)})
write_json("PACK_MANIFEST.json", {"bundle_id":"LITIGATIONOS__MASTERv1.0__SPEC_ADDENDUM_v0012","built_at":now,"files":files})
---------------- Build ZIP ----------------

zip_path = Path("/mnt/data/LITIGATIONOS__MASTERv1.0__SPEC_ADDENDUM_v0012.zip")
if zip_path.exists():
zip_path.unlink()
with zipfile.ZipFile(zip_path, "w", compression=zipfile.ZIP_DEFLATED) as z:
for p in sorted(work.rglob("*")):
if p.is_file():
z.write(p, arcname=str(p.relative_to(work)).replace("\","/"))
---------------- Smoke run ----------------

env = os.environ.copy()
env["PYTHONPATH"] = str(work)
env["LITIGATIONOS_ROOT"] = str(work)
proc = subprocess.run([sys.executable, str(work/"tools"/"runtime_smoke_test_v0012.py")], env=env, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=45)

(zip_path.exists(), zip_path.stat().st_size, sha256_file(zip_path), proc.returncode, proc.stdout.strip(), proc.stderr.strip()[:900])

v0012 implementation status

Your three requirements are now implemented, wired, and smoke-tested inside the pack:

    Path Policy Gate (FAIL-CLOSED)

        Validates configured paths are readable and writable

        Attempts to create missing directories

        FAIL-CLOSED if required paths are not accessible

        Emits a machine-checkable PATHS_HEALTH_REPORT

    OUT Canonical Layout Contract (strict enforcement)

        Reserved canonical folders:

            OUT\DERIVED\<LANE>\(subpath)

            OUT\ENVELOPES\(subpath)

            OUT\REPORTS\(subpath)

        Enforced inside transform_envelope validator when strict=True

    Config Snapshot stamping (reproducible builds)

        Every envelope and transform enforcement report includes:

            config_snapshot.paths_config_source

            config_snapshot.paths_config_sha256

            config_snapshot.paths_expanded

What changed (exact wiring)
A) Path Policy Gate

New:

    services/path_policy_gate/main.py

        ensure_dirs(root, ["out","cas","governance","logs","tmp"])

        Returns PATHS_HEALTH_REPORT JSON (machine-checkable)

    tools/paths_health_cli.py

        CLI to generate/print PATHS_HEALTH_REPORT

Wired into FAIL-CLOSED gate:

    services/transform_gate/main.py

        Calls ensure_dirs(...) before envelope validation

        If status != PASS, returns:

            status: FAIL

            error code: PATH_POLICY_GATE_FAIL

            detail contains the full PATHS_HEALTH_REPORT object

B) OUT canonical layout enforcement

Strict-mode enforcement in:

    services/common/transform_envelope.py

        Adds _layout_check_outputs(...)

        Validates each output artifact path is under configured OUT, and routed to:

            OUT\DERIVED\<LANE>\(subpath) for derived artifacts

            OUT\ENVELOPES\(subpath) for envelope-typed JSON outputs

            OUT\REPORTS\(subpath) for report-typed JSON outputs

C) Config snapshot stamping

New:

    services/common/config_snapshot.py

        get_config_snapshot() reads the active paths config and returns the snapshot fields

Stamped into:

    services/common/enveloped_output.py (envelope creation)

    services/common/transform_envelope.py (enforcement report returns)

Enhanced:

    services/common/paths.py

        Supports LITIGATIONOS_PATHS_CONFIG override

        Computes and returns config_sha256 for the active config file text

How to run it in your environment (Windows PowerShell)
1) Unzip to your canonical folder

Example:

    C:\Users\andre\Downloads\LITIGATIONOS__MASTERv1.0__SPEC_ADDENDUM_v0012\

2) Run PATHS_HEALTH_REPORT generator (CLI)

$ROOT="C:\Users\andre\Downloads\LITIGATIONOS__MASTERv1.0__SPEC_ADDENDUM_v0012"
Set-Location $ROOT
$env:PYTHONPATH=$ROOT
$env:LITIGATIONOS_ROOT=$ROOT

python tools\paths_health_cli.py --json

3) Persist PATHS_HEALTH_REPORT into OUT\REPORTS

$ROOT="C:\Users\andre\Downloads\LITIGATIONOS__MASTERv1.0__SPEC_ADDENDUM_v0012"
Set-Location $ROOT
$env:PYTHONPATH=$ROOT
$env:LITIGATIONOS_ROOT=$ROOT

# Writes the report into your configured OUT\REPORTS
python tools\paths_health_cli.py --json | Out-File -Encoding utf8 (Join-Path $ROOT "OUT\REPORTS\PATHS_HEALTH_REPORT.v0012.json")

4) Optional: use an alternate paths config (repro build stamp changes)

$env:LITIGATIONOS_PATHS_CONFIG="F:\LitigationOS\config\paths.json"
python tools\paths_health_cli.py --json

Machine-checkable outputs (what to expect)
PATHS_HEALTH_REPORT (core fields)

    schema_version = v0012

    ts

    status = PASS or FAIL

    root

    checks[] (one per key: out, cas, governance, logs, tmp)

        path_key, path, exists, readable, writable, created, status

    errors[]

    config_snapshot

        paths_config_source

        paths_config_sha256

        paths_expanded

Envelope + Enforcement Report stamping

Every envelope/report now includes:

    config_snapshot.paths_config_source

    config_snapshot.paths_config_sha256

    config_snapshot.paths_expanded

This is what makes later overlays and casepacks reproducible: you can prove exactly which path configuration governed the run.

How to use this prompt (operator steps)
