SUPERPIN prompt (paste exactly)

SUPERPIN_MI_LEGAL_IQ_CORE_PILLARS_v1
Role: LitigationOS / Michigan-Locked Legal Brain / Neo4j GraphRAG Architect
Objective: Add new “Core Pillars” focused on Michigan practice IQ, Michigan court forms (all areas), and corporate/business-law entity modeling, producing machine-checkable specs and runnable local artifacts.

NON-NEGOTIABLE HARD GATES
1) TRUTH-LOCK:
   - No invented facts, no invented authority, no invented citations.
   - Any proposition without a pinned pointer is DISPUTED and must be emitted as PINPOINT_MISSING with an acquisition plan.
2) AUTHORITY LOCK (Michigan-first):
   - Use Michigan primary authorities only unless I explicitly authorize overlays.
   - Authority classes: MCR, MCL, MRE, Michigan benchbooks, SCAO form instructions, local admin orders (when supplied).
   - Caselaw governance: classify each case cite by court and published status; no proposition without holding + pinpoint + status.
3) FORMS-FIRST:
   - Relief must map to mandated-use forms (SCAO/MC/FOC) or explicitly justified pleadings.
   - Every “vehicle” must define prerequisites, deadlines, service, fees/bonds, controlling orders, required exhibits, and proof obligations.
4) PROOF-CARRYING WORKFLOW:
   - All filing-capable outputs are blocked until mandatory Proof Obligations are SATISFIED.
   - PCG is FAIL-CLOSED for anything that would be filed/served/exported as courtroom-ready.
5) ENGINEERING:
   - No skeletons. Deliver runnable, complete files only.
   - Deterministic paths. Default: exclude C:\ unless explicitly enabled.
   - All artifacts must include manifests, schemas, and validation steps.

OPERATOR INPUTS (provide immediately after this prompt)
- ROOT_HINT: "C:\Users\andre\Downloads\LITIGATIONOS__MASTERv1.0"
- TARGET_DOMAINS:
  - MI_PRACTICE_IQ: true
  - MI_FORMS_UNIVERSE: true
  - CORPORATE_BUSINESS_PILLAR: true
- TARGET_ENTITIES (strings only, no assumed facts):
  - ["Shady Oaks Park MHP LLC", "Alden Global"]
- COURT_SCOPE:
  - ["District Court", "Circuit Court", "Court of Appeals", "Supreme Court", "Probate Court", "Municipal where applicable"]
- CASE_TYPES_SCOPE:
  - ["civil", "family", "criminal", "probate", "landlord-tenant", "small claims", "PPO", "traffic", "collections", "business/contract", "administrative overlays if supplied"]
- PRIVACY:
  - PII_POLICY_DEFAULT: "OFF"
  - PII_EXPORT_POLICY: "courtroom-grade, do-not-redact required identifiers when legally necessary"
- OUTPUT_MODE:
  - "Spec Addendum Pack" with version bump and a single release ZIP

AUTONOMY AND TOOLING RULES
- You must autonomously use available tools to obtain up-to-date Michigan form inventories and official instructions.
- If web access is allowed, you must browse official Michigan sources and SCAO repositories and capture provenance.
- If local packs are attached, you must treat them as primary inputs and prefer them over public sources.
- If you cannot retrieve an item, emit PINPOINT_MISSING with an acquisition plan (exact source to fetch, exact query, and where it will be pinned).

DELIVERABLES (CORE PILLARS TO SHIP)

PILLAR 1: MICHIGAN PRACTICE IQ AND LEGAL LANGUAGE ENGINE
A) “Michigan Legal Language Canon”
   - Pattern library: courtroom-safe phrasing, motion framing, notice language, verification/affidavit language, reservation of rights, objection and preservation language.
   - Each pattern must include: allowed uses, prohibited uses, required foundations, and “record survival” notes.
B) “Practice IQ Training Program”
   - Micro-drills: hearing prep, evidentiary objections, offers of proof, service mechanics, deadlines computation, filing sequence.
   - A graded rubric: PASS/FAIL + reasons + remedial steps.
   - Output artifacts: MI_PRACTICE_IQ_INDEX.json + DRILLS.json + RUBRIC.json with schemas.

PILLAR 2: MICHIGAN COURTS AND FORMS UNIVERSE (ALL AREAS)
A) Forms Universe Catalog
   - Enumerate SCAO/MC/FOC forms across all Michigan court areas in scope.
   - For each form:
     - form_id, title, category, court level, prerequisites, governing authority pointers, filing steps, service steps, fees/bonds, typical exhibits, common failure modes.
   - Build VehicleMap bindings:
     - Relief → Vehicle → Form → Standards → ProofObligations → Deadlines → Service → Exhibits.
B) Authority Snapshot Index
   - Build a pinned “AuthorityChunk” library for any form instruction text you rely on.
   - No paraphrase-only authority. Every rule proposition must be tied to a chunk pointer.

PILLAR 3: CORPORATE AND BUSINESS LAW PILLAR (ENTITY-OPERATOR MODELING)
A) Entity Shell Mapper Lane
   - Treat TARGET_ENTITIES as nodes to investigate and map.
   - Map: entity type, registered agent, assumed names, parent-subsidiary indicators (only when pinned).
   - Build a “Corporate Evidence Intake” checklist (documents to obtain, how to pin them, chain-of-custody).
B) Business-Law Vehicle Library (Michigan)
   - Contract and consumer/business dispute vehicles (Michigan-locked) with prerequisites and proofs.
   - Landlord-tenant and utilities/habitability-adjacent corporate operator angles as vehicles only when Michigan authority pins exist.
C) Corporate Governance and Supply-Chain Hygiene
   - A policy module: what can be alleged, when, and what evidence is required before escalation.

CROSS-CUTTING ENFORCEMENT UPGRADES (MUST SHIP)
1) PATH POLICY GATE
   - Validate OUT/CAS/governance/log/tmp readability and writability, fail-closed, emit PATHS_HEALTH_REPORT (machine-checkable schema).
2) OUT/CAS CANONICAL LAYOUT CONTRACT
   - Reserve:
     - OUT\DERIVED\<LANE>\(subpath)
     - OUT\ENVELOPES\(subpath)
     - OUT\REPORTS\(subpath)
   - Enforce in Transform Envelope strict mode with explicit violation codes.
3) CONFIG SNAPSHOT STAMPING
   - Every report/envelope includes:
     - paths_config_source
     - paths_config_sha256
     - expanded paths dict (or reference id)
   - Enables reproducible builds for overlays and CasePack assembly.

OUTPUT ARTIFACTS (MUST DELIVER AS A SINGLE RELEASE ZIP)
- Spec Addendum Pack with monotonic version bump:
  - SPEC_ADDENDUM_vNNNN.md
  - schemas/ (Draft 2020-12 JSON Schema)
  - openapi/ (OpenAPI 3.1 per service boundary)
  - services/ (runnable services updated)
  - tools/ (CLI utilities)
  - PACK_MANIFEST.json (sha256 per file)
  - smoke test scripts + PASS logs
- Must include a “how to run” checklist for Windows PowerShell.

QUALITY GATES (MUST RUN AND REPORT)
- Smoke test must:
  1) Discover ROOT
  2) Load config\paths.json and compute sha256
  3) Run Path Policy Gate PASS
  4) Produce a PATHS_HEALTH_REPORT and validate against schema
  5) Create a derived artifact under OUT\DERIVED\<LANE>\ and validate OUT layout strict mode
  6) Emit at least one envelope and one report with config snapshot stamping
- If any gate fails: output INCOMPLETE + blockers + the exact failing file and function.

BEGIN EXECUTION NOW
- First emit: Execution Plan (bullet-tight, no filler)
- Then build: specs, schemas, service updates, tools
- Then run: smoke tests
- Then output: one release ZIP + sha256 + exact Windows run steps

“Authoritative source preference: SCAO official forms repository + official instruction PDFs; no third-party mirrors unless pinned and cross-validated.”

That forces the system to build the Forms Universe on primary Michigan sources and produce a defensible catalog with provenance.

ChatGPT said:
Canonical “Spec-God” escalation pillars to append alongside Procedures + Court Jurisdiction

You already have the right backbone. The next step is to turn “procedures” into a complete, machine-checkable universe where every court action is:

    typed (what it is),

    jurisdiction-bound (where it can be done),

    vehicle-bound (how it is done procedurally),

    form-bound (what filing vehicle is required or allowed), and

    authority/pin-bound (why it is legally valid, with pinpoints).

Below is the “core pillars” set that gets you to canonical spec grade without hand-listing hundreds of forms in chat.
A) New core pillars (graph-first, machine-checkable)
Pillar A1: Forms Universe (SCAO Master Catalog)

Purpose: Ingest the entire State Court Administrative Office form universe into your graph, not as anecdotes, but as a complete catalog.

Authoritative source of truth: SCAO “Search for a form” index, which is the practical entry point to the complete statewide form set. citeturn3search1

Graph objects

    Form (MC/DC/CC/PC/FOC form IDs, title, revision date, PDF URL, instructions URL)

    FormInstruction (instructions PDFs)

    FormFieldClass (typed classes: identity, address, child, case, relief, service, fees, dates, exhibits)

    FormUseConstraint (mandated-use, court-only, optional)

    FormJurisdictionScope (district, circuit, probate, statewide, specialized)

Edges

    AVAILABLE_IN(Form -> Court/Jurisdiction)

    IMPLEMENTS(Form -> Vehicle)

    REQUIRES_FIELDCLASS(Form -> FormFieldClass)

    HAS_INSTRUCTIONS(Form -> FormInstruction)

    GOVERNED_BY(Form/Vehicle -> Authority/Proposition)

Why this is the highest leverage: it eliminates “missing forms” permanently by turning SCAO into a harvested dataset rather than a memory exercise.
Pillar A2: Procedure Universe (Typed Action Taxonomy)

Purpose: represent “ALL possible procedures/court actions” as a taxonomy, then attach forms and vehicles.

Graph objects

    ProcedureFamily (CaseStart, MotionPractice, Service, Discovery, Settlement, Enforcement, Appellate, ExtraordinaryRelief, Administration)

    Procedure (leaf node: specific action)

    FilingArtifactType (Complaint, Petition, Motion, Application, ClaimOfAppeal, NoticeOfAppeal, Brief, ProposedOrder)

Edges

    BELONGS_TO(Procedure -> ProcedureFamily)

    AVAILABLE_IN(Procedure -> Court/Jurisdiction)

    USES_FORM(Procedure -> Form) or USES_DOCUMENT_TYPE(Procedure -> FilingArtifactType) (when no SCAO form exists)

    TRIGGERS_DEADLINE(Procedure -> DeadlineTemplate)

    REQUIRES(Procedure -> ProofObligationTemplate)

Pillar A3: Jurisdiction Ladder (Muskegon and Beyond)

Purpose: hard-bind actions to venues and jurisdiction limits.

Muskegon anchor facts (examples you can cite in-court)

    60th District Court jurisdiction categories include civil suits ≤ $25k, small claims, landlord-tenant eviction proceedings, misdemeanors, and it is the starting point for adult criminal proceedings and felony prelim processes. citeturn6search2

    Muskegon County Circuit Court Records filing methods and constraints (no legal advice from clerk staff, mail or in-person filing). citeturn6search5

    Probate court packet guidance exists with local instructions and fee expectations. citeturn6search3

Graph objects

    Court (60th District, 14th Circuit, Muskegon Probate, MI COA, MI Supreme Court, WDMI, 6th Cir, SCOTUS)

    Jurisdiction (subject-matter, monetary limit, geographic scope)

    VenueRule (county, district, service-based venue triggers)

    LocalProcedureNote (local filing constraints and methods)

Edges

    HAS_JURISDICTION(Court -> Jurisdiction)

    APPEALS_TO(Court -> Court) (example: district decisions generally appealable to circuit). citeturn6search2

    HAS_LOCAL_RULES(Court -> LocalProcedureNote)

Pillar A4: Appellate Vehicles and Extraordinary Relief (MI COA, MI Supreme Court)

Purpose: unify appeal-by-right, leave, interlocutory, and original actions.

Authoritative anchors

    Michigan Court of Appeals civil appeal guide (filing steps, required components, process). citeturn3search37

    Michigan Supreme Court “Application for Leave to Appeal” rule is explicitly governed by MCR 7.305. citeturn3search5

    Michigan Supreme Court internal operating procedures reflect the governing MCR 7.300-series framework in practice. citeturn5search3

Graph objects

    AppellateVehicle (ClaimOfAppeal, ApplicationLeave, InterlocutoryApplication, SuperintendingControl, Mandamus, Prohibition)

    RecordComponent (ROA, transcripts, orders, exhibits, proof of service)

    StandardOfReview (abuse of discretion, de novo, clear error)

    PreservationRequirement (objection, offer of proof, evidentiary hearing tag)

Edges

    USES(Procedure -> AppellateVehicle)

    REQUIRES_COMPONENT(AppellateVehicle -> RecordComponent)

    GOVERNED_BY(AppellateVehicle -> Authority)

Pillar A5: Federal Overlay (WDMI, Appeals, SCOTUS)

Purpose: bring in federal procedure without diluting Michigan-first lock.

Anchors

    WDMI provides a Pro Se forms page (federal filing artifacts and guidance for unrepresented litigants). citeturn3search0

    SCOTUS “Filing and Rules” official portal is the canonical source for SCOTUS filing requirements and resources. citeturn5search0turn5search1

Graph objects

    FederalCourt (WDMI, 6th Circuit, SCOTUS)

    FederalProcedure (Complaint, IFP, Summons, NoticeOfAppeal, Motion, Brief, PetitionCert)

    FederalForm (AO forms, court-specific forms, SCOTUS forms)

Edges

    AVAILABLE_IN(FederalProcedure -> FederalCourt)

    USES_FORM(FederalProcedure -> FederalForm)

    GOVERNED_BY(FederalProcedure -> Authority) (FRCP, FRAP, SCOTUS Rules as separate authority sets, with your lock gating when allowed)

Pillar A6: Landlord–Tenant and Summary Proceedings (Michigan Trial Form Binding)

Purpose: hard-wire the high-volume housing lane (Shady Oaks and similar) into procedures and forms.

Anchors

    Michigan landlord-tenant benchbook has a “Summary Proceedings” chapter (procedural frame for eviction actions). citeturn4search0

    Demand for Possession forms are published as DC 100 (and DC 100a instructions), which are form-bound artifacts you can graph. citeturn4search39turn4search36

Graph objects

    Procedure: DemandForPossession, SummaryProceedingsComplaint, OrderOfEviction, etc.

    Form: DC100, DC100a, DC102 (and the rest harvested programmatically)

    ServiceRule, HearingType, JudgmentType

Pillar A7: PPO / Protection / Enforcement (Form-bound and pinned)

Anchors

    PPO petition instructions exist for CC 375 (and should be represented as both Form and FormInstruction). citeturn2search61

Graph objects

    Procedure: PPO_Petition, PPO_Modify, PPO_Terminate, Contempt_ShowCause

    Form: CC 375 family of PPO materials (harvest full set via SCAO catalog)

    Finding, Order, Statement, QuotePin, Contradiction (your existing ontology lanes)

Pillar A8: Corporate / Entity / Registry Evidence Router (Business IQ + leverage)

Purpose: make entity structure a first-class graph domain for corporate landlords and related entities.

Anchors

    LARA launched the MiBusiness Registry Portal and states it supports searching and viewing business records as part of that portal ecosystem. citeturn7search0turn7search6

Graph objects

    Entity (LLC, Corp, LP)

    RegistryRecord (formation, registered agent, annual statement status)

    OwnershipHypothesis (typed as allegation until pinned)

    EvidencePinpoint linking registry documents to claims

Edges

    RECORDED_IN(Entity -> RegistryRecord)

    HAS_REGISTERED_AGENT(Entity -> Person/Organization)

    ASSOCIATED_WITH(Entity -> Property/Address)

    SUPPORTED_BY(RegistryRecord -> EvidencePinpoint)

B) “Autonomy + OCR/NLP” escalations that become spec-grade pillars (not features)
Pillar B1: Autonomy Contract (Runbook-Guaranteed, Fail-Closed)

Core rule: every autonomous action must emit:

    RUN_MANIFEST.json (inputs, outputs, config hashes, versions)

    LANE_DECISION_REPORT.json (why OCR or NLP ran)

    FAIL_CLOSED_REASON.json (if blocked)

This ties directly into your existing PCW/PCG gates.
Pillar B2: OCR Lane (Evidence-grade provenance)

You already defined the correct constraints. The missing “spec grade” add is:

    Raster policy (when to raster PDFs, DPI, color mode, threshold rules)

    Page-level provenance (each page image hash, OCR engine version, language model selection)

    Multi-language policy (language detection decision report, override rules, confidence gating)

    Image OCR lane for PNG/JPG scans in addition to PDF OCR

These become nodes and edges:

    OcrRun, OcrPage, OcrEngine, LanguageDecision, TransformEnvelope

Pillar B3: NLP Lane (Typed-unit compiler)

Your requested ontology compiler becomes a full pipeline:

    Extracted text shards

    TypedUnit generation: QUOTE, FACT, STATEMENT, FINDING, ORDER

    Pin binding: each unit must bind to EvidencePinpoint or LawPinpoint

    Contradictions: allowed only between pinned typed units, with explicit scope rules

This converts “analysis” into “graph objects” deterministically.
Pillar B4: PII Governance (Policy-ledger per CaseProfile)

This is a governance pillar, not a switch:

    RedactionPolicyLedger per case profile

    ExportPolicy (courtroom-safe output rules)

    MappingRetentionPolicy (whether reversible mapping is allowed, and where it is stored)

    RedactionTransformEnvelope attached to every derived artifact

C) The “ALL procedures + all forms” problem is solved by harvesting, not hand-authoring

You do not want a human-curated list in chat. The canonical way is:

    Harvest statewide SCAO form catalog from the official form search/index. citeturn3search1

    Normalize into Form nodes with stable IDs and revision metadata.

    Build a Procedure taxonomy that is complete at the category level and expandable at leaf level.

What “ALL court rules / ALL procedures / ALL forms” means operationally

To make this real (and not hand-wavy), you grow the nucleus by layers, each layer producing:

        Circuit: family (custody/PT), PPO, civil general, criminal

        required authority nodes (rules/statutes/local orders)

        forms

        deadlines

        service chain

This is how you reach “ALL” without fiction: you’re not asserting completeness; you’re building a completeness-measured corpus where each missing edge is explicitly tracked as a missing obligation.
Neo4j + Bloom “Nucleus” setup (FP32-style + dense themes)
What “FP32 nucleus” means here

    Node initial coordinates are quantized to float32-equivalent values before emission (initial positions), then the browser/force engine simulates.

    Neo4j itself stores properties as standard numeric types; Bloom positions are layout-driven.

Color themes

In the HTML exporter, color is based on kind:

    AUTHORITY: violet

    RULE: blue

    PROCEDURE: green

    FORM: amber

    VEHICLE / PO (future layers): distinct colors

Bloom Themes (inside Bloom) are handled via:

    Perspectives

    Styling by property (kind, status, assurance)
    Neo4j Bloom supports this workflow through its perspective + styling model.

No metaphors, no placeholders. This is the hard expansion.
CHAINED CYCLE N+1
Procedural Exhaustion + Rule/Form Saturation + Jurisdiction Closure
I. PROCEDURE UNIVERSE — FULL SATURATION MODEL

You already have ProcedureFamily → Procedure.
This cycle forces closure by introducing ProcedurePhase and ProcedureMode, which eliminates ambiguity and collapses “missing edge” chaos.
New node types (append-only)
1. ProcedurePhase

Every court action MUST belong to one of these phases:

    INITIATION

    PRELIMINARY

    INTERLOCUTORY

    MERITS

    ENFORCEMENT

    POST_JUDGMENT

    APPELLATE

    EXTRAORDINARY

    ADMINISTRATIVE

Rule:

    A Procedure node cannot exist without exactly one HAS_PHASE edge.

2. ProcedureMode

Captures how the court acts:

    BY_RIGHT

    BY_LEAVE

    EX_PARTE

    ON_NOTICE

    EMERGENCY

    DISCRETIONARY

    MANDATED

    AUTOMATIC

Rule:

    ProcedureMode is required to compute deadlines, service, and appealability.

3. Mandatory edges (now enforced)

    Procedure —HAS_PHASE→ ProcedurePhase

    Procedure —HAS_MODE→ ProcedureMode

    Procedure —AVAILABLE_IN→ Court

    Procedure —GOVERNED_BY→ Authority (MCR/MCL/etc)

    Procedure —USES_VEHICLE→ Vehicle

    Vehicle —USES_FORM→ Form OR Vehicle —USES_DOCUMENT_TYPE→ FilingArtifactType

If any edge is missing → FAIL CLOSED.
II. COURT JURISDICTION — CLOSURE GRAPH

You already model courts. This cycle locks jurisdiction logic so no procedure can float.
New node types
1. JurisdictionBasis

Examples:

    SUBJECT_MATTER

    MONETARY_LIMIT

    PERSONAL

    VENUE

    CONTINUING

    APPELLATE

2. JurisdictionConstraint

Concrete constraints, e.g.:

    “≤ $25,000”

    “Child custody matters”

    “Final orders only”

    “Interlocutory by leave”

Mandatory edges

    Court —HAS_JURISDICTION→ JurisdictionBasis

    JurisdictionBasis —LIMITED_BY→ JurisdictionConstraint

    Procedure —REQUIRES_JURISDICTION→ JurisdictionBasis

This is what lets the system refuse to attach an improper procedure to the wrong court.
III. FORMS UNIVERSE — COMPLETE NORMALIZATION

This cycle finishes the Forms pillar so it becomes computational, not descriptive.
New node types
1. FormRevision

Every form revision becomes a node:

    revision_date

    supersedes_form_id

    effective_until

2. FormMandate

Encodes whether a form is:

    MANDATED

    OPTIONAL

    COURT_ONLY

    PROHIBITED

3. FormFieldRule

Rules attached to form fields:

    required

    conditional

    jurisdiction-dependent

    sealed/redacted

Mandatory edges

    Form —HAS_REVISION→ FormRevision

    FormRevision —HAS_MANDATE→ FormMandate

    Form —HAS_FIELD_CLASS→ FormFieldClass

    FormFieldClass —GOVERNED_BY→ Authority

Result:
The system can now answer:

    “Can I file this procedure in this court without this form?”

With a deterministic YES/NO and explanation.
IV. AUTHORITY SATURATION — RULES → PROCEDURES → FORMS

This cycle introduces AuthorityScope to stop overbroad rule usage.
New node types
1. AuthorityScope

Examples:

    TRIAL_ONLY

    APPELLATE_ONLY

    CIVIL

    CRIMINAL

    FAMILY

    PROBATE

    GENERAL

Mandatory edges

    Authority —HAS_SCOPE→ AuthorityScope

    Procedure —WITHIN_SCOPE→ AuthorityScope

Enforcement rule:
If a Procedure references an Authority outside its scope, block it and mark:

    CONFLICT_SCOPE

V. DEADLINE + SERVICE — HARD COMPUTATION LAYER

This cycle upgrades deadlines from “notes” to first-class computable objects.
New node types
1. DeadlineRule

    trigger_event

    days

    business_days_flag

    tolling_allowed

2. ServiceRule

    method_required (personal, mail, electronic)

    who_may_serve

    proof_required

Mandatory edges

    Procedure —TRIGGERS_DEADLINE→ DeadlineRule

    Procedure —REQUIRES_SERVICE→ ServiceRule

    ServiceRule —GOVERNED_BY→ Authority

This enables automatic deadline computation and service compliance checks.
VI. APPELLATE CLOSURE — NO MORE AMBIGUITY

This cycle fully closes appellate logic.
New node types
1. Appealability

    FINAL

    INTERLOCUTORY

    NON_APPEALABLE

2. StandardOfReview

    de novo

    abuse of discretion

    clear error

    plain error

Mandatory edges

    Procedure —HAS_APPEALABILITY→ Appealability

    Appealability —LEADS_TO→ AppellateVehicle

    AppellateVehicle —REQUIRES_STANDARD→ StandardOfReview

Now the graph can explain why something is appealable and how.
VII. BLOOM / FORCE GRAPH — NUCLEUS EXPANSION

    Procedure (green)

    Vehicle (cyan)

    Form (amber)

    Deadline/Service (red)

    Court (blue)

Bloom Perspectives

Create perspectives for:

    “Trial Workflow”

    “Appeal Pathways”

    “Forms Compliance”

    “Jurisdiction Errors”

    “Deadline Risk”

Each perspective is now lossless, not decorative.

0) What “convergence” means in this domain (and how to measure it)

You asked for “proliferate until convergence.” In legal graph terms, convergence is not “infinite expansion,” it is stabilization of coverage metrics such that new ingestion no longer produces unmapped or orphan artifacts.
Convergence targets (machine-checkable)

A cycle is “converging” when deltas drop toward zero in these metrics:
Metric	Meaning	Stop condition
unmapped_procedures	ProcedureAction nodes with no Court route	== 0
orphan_forms	Form nodes with no ProcedureAction attachment	== 0
authority_without_pins	Authority units lacking pinpoint chunks	decreases monotonically
crosswalk_edge_delta	new (Procedure↔Form↔Authority↔Court) edges	< eps for N cycles
jurisdiction_gaps	Court topics without court mapping	== 0 for chosen scope

This is how you get “limitless chained cycles” that are controlled rather than chaotic: every cycle appends, but the delta becomes the stopping signal.
1) New core pillars (alongside Procedures) that unlock “ALL rules, ALL forms, ALL actions”

To elevate Procedures into the “Grand Master SUPERABUNDENT graph,” you need adjacent pillars that Procedures can bind to deterministically:
Pillar A — Jurisdiction Router

Purpose: every action routes to the correct court level and appellate ladder.

Minimum nucleus:

    Court ladder: trial → appellate mapping (MI + federal overlay)

    Subject matter topics (civil/family/probate/criminal/LT/small claims/PPO)

    Routing rules (venue, monetary limit, exclusivity, appellate path)

Michigan Court Rules are the authoritative procedural backbone and explicitly govern practice and procedure in MI courts. The official Michigan Court Rules publication is hosted by Michigan Courts.
Sources: Michigan Courts “Michigan Court Rules” publication pages and chapters (official).
Pillar B — Forms Universe

Purpose: every action has the correct form set attached to its jurisdiction and filing lane.

Minimum nucleus:

    Form nodes (SCAO/FOC/MC; appellate packets; federal forms; SCOTUS rules/forms)

    FormPacket nodes (bundle concept: appeal packet, PPO packet, custody packet)

    FilingMethod nodes (MiFILE vs paper vs hybrid)

SCAO form revision tracking exists and is published through Michigan Courts.
MiFILE guidance is published as a statewide e-filing program memorandum.
Michigan Court of Appeals publishes civil appeal guidance and forms guidance.
Federal overlay: Western District of Michigan local rules and pro se resources are published by the court.
SCOTUS rules are published by the Court.
Sixth Circuit forms/rules/appendix checklist exist as published circuit materials.
Pillar C — Authority Corpus

Purpose: rules and statutes become pin-addressable “authority units” that can govern procedures and form fields.

Minimum nucleus:

    MCR (official)

    MRE (official PDF)

    MCL (official legislature portal)

    Benchbooks (MJI)

    Local administrative orders (append-only, sourced)

Official Michigan Rules of Evidence PDF is hosted by Michigan Courts.
Michigan Compiled Laws are accessible via Michigan Legislature search portal.
Local administrative order example for Muskegon exists as a court-published PDF (use as local practice lane input).
Pillar D — Service & Deadlines

Purpose: every action carries deadline triggers and service methods.

Anchor: MCR provisions include service and filing mechanics, including electronic service rules inside the MCR text.
Pillar E — Record Spine / Products

Purpose: every action produces record items and derived products:

    Proof of service, ROA entry, file-stamped copy, transcripts, exhibit list, proposed order

    Derived: timeline, contradiction map, exhibit matrix, authority triples

This is where your Proof-Carrying Workflow gates become computable: PCW blocks exports until required record artifacts exist and are pinned.
2) “ALL COURT ACTIONS / PROCEDURES” taxonomy (deterministic scaffolding)

Instead of trying to hand-list everything in one pass, you win by building a ProcedureAction universe that is:

    hierarchical

    typed

    jurisdiction-tagged

    later expanded via ingestion of rules/form instructions

Core trunks (each becomes a hub in the graph):

    Initiation & pleadings

    Motions (dismissal, summary disposition, interim relief)

    Discovery (requests, subpoenas, depositions, compel/sanctions)

    Hearings & trial (pretrial, evidentiary hearings, trial)

    Orders & post-judgment (reconsideration, relief from judgment, enforcement/contempt, collections)

    Appeals (claim of appeal, leave, stay pending appeal, record/appendix)

    Extraordinary relief (superintending control, mandamus/prohibition)

    Federal overlay (civil complaint, TRO/PI, habeas)

    SCOTUS overlay (certiorari)

Michigan Court of Appeals civil appeal process guidance is an official anchor for appeal-lane procedures and artifacts.
SCOTUS rules anchor certiorari lane.
3) “Fully baked Bloom-style forced graph” (open-source, dense, themeable)

You asked for a robust force graph with hubs/wheels, gravity boundaries, and themes.
Practical open-source stack

    D3 force simulation (for force layout in the browser; deterministic seeds + fp32 quantization)

    Sigma.js (WebGL, scales to larger graphs)

    Cytoscape.js (graph UI/interaction model)

    Graphology (graph ops, centrality, partitioning)

    D3-force module reference exists via npm docs (API ref).

“FP32 nucleus” interpretation (what it actually means)

    Store initial positions as float32 (quantized) into node attributes

    Run force simulation from those initial positions

    Cache final positions by “Perspective” (pillar / lane / case profile)

Result: large graph becomes stable and fast on re-open.
4) Chained cycles (x20) — a concrete escalation ladder

This is the deterministic “cycle chain” for unlimited appending:
Cycle 01 — URL seed normalization (authority + forms discovery)

Input: your urls.csv
Output: URL index JSONL + domain report + fetch queue
Cycle 02 — Jurisdiction ladder seed

Output: Court nodes + APPEAL_TO edges
Cycle 03 — Procedure universe seed

Output: ProcedureAction nodes
Cycle 04 — MCR extraction + authority unit index

Input: official MCR content (your zip or official HTML)
Output: rule citations → AuthorityUnit nodes (never invented)
Cycle 05 — MRE ingestion (official PDF)

Output: MRE units
Cycle 06 — MCL ingestion scaffolding

Output: statute anchors from Legislature portal
Cycle 07 — Forms catalog build from local caches

You already have a local-catalog tool: build from local PDF caches, no invention.
Cycle 08 — Appeal lane forms and artifacts mapping

Use COA civil appeal guide as anchor.
Cycle 09 — MiFILE lane tagging (filing method edges)

Use MiFILE memo as anchor.
Cycle 10 — Federal overlay lane (WDMI + 6th)

Use WDMI local rules + 6th circuit docs.
Cycle 11 — SCOTUS overlay lane

Use SCOTUS rules.
Cycle 12 — Local admin orders lane (Muskegon example)

Append LAO nodes, never overwrite.
Cycle 13 — Service and deadline extraction hooks

From MCR.
Cycle 14 — Proof obligations templating per vehicle

PCW binds here: procedure → proof obligations → required pins.
Cycle 15 — Form-field overlay contract expansion

Field types and required pin classes.
Cycle 16 — Transform envelope enforcement across lanes

Every derived artifact must reference original CAS pointer (sha256 + observed path + ingest method).
Cycle 17 — Ontology compiler (typed units)

QUOTE vs FACT vs FINDING vs ORDER vs STATEMENT typed generation.
Cycle 18 — Contradiction engine

Only typed pinned claims can contradict; contradiction edges are typed.
Cycle 19 — Neo4j drift gate enforcement

Block imports until schema contract matches.
Cycle 20 — Graph export perspectives + theme packs

Color-coded by pillar, court lane, case profile.

That’s the chained escalation ladder that can run “unlimited” because every cycle ends with:

    Coverage report

    Delta report

    Drift report

    Fail-closed gate outcomes

5) Path discipline (your Downloads-root reality)

You clarified that everything you click downloads into:

C:\Users\andre\Downloads\LITIGATIONOS__MASTERv1.0__SPEC_ADDENDUM_v0010

The design therefore must assume:

    $ROOT is discovered by finding config\paths.json

    no tool should “point somewhere else”

    all file pincites must be relative to the exact folder you placed them

The pack you get below is built to run with a single paths.json, and the new tools default to reading that config so your system does not depend on manual path passing.
6) What is still incomplete (explicitly)

You asked for ALL rules, forms, and procedures crosswired for all MI levels and federal/supreme overlays. The only honest answer is:

    The full enumeration of every form across every MI domain is not something a human should type manually.

    The correct approach is exactly what you’re building: machine ingestion + deterministic crosswalk generation + convergence metrics.

    This response (and the pack) provides the scaffold and cycle chain needed to converge, but it does not claim the corpus is already complete.

The next step to “true completeness” is to run the importer against your URL list and caches, then generate unmapped lists and close the gaps cycle by cycle.
